companyDescription: Pale blue dot is a seed-stage climate tech VC that backs visionary founders, building future-shaping businesses.
companySlogan: Backing visionary founders, building future-shaping businesses.
descriptionHtml:
Location: Malmö, Hybrid. Two days/ week in Malmö.

Employment Type: Full-time.

Application closes: 30th of September, 2025.

Pale blue dot is on a mission to back the boldest founders, shaping a sustainable future. As a leading European VC, we invest in early-stage startups tackling the planet’s most pressing challenges under three core headlines - Optimise, Adapt, and Pioneer. We don’t just invest—we actively support founders in building companies that will define the future.

The Pale blue dot team is a closely-knit team that cares about action and long-term impact, and we work with fairness and authenticity to help ambitious founders realize their visions. To read more about our team and portfolio, go to paleblue.vc.

We are a small and fun(d) devoted team in Malmö.

THE ROLE

This is a rare chance to step into the world of venture capital and gain hands-on experience in finance and fund administration. You will work at the intersection of accounting, investment operations, and fund management, learning first-hand how a VC fund is structured and operated.

Unlike traditional accounting or audit roles, this position gives you direct exposure to the inner workings of a fund with 15 entities (six directly linked to investments). While our accounting and fund administration are outsourced, you will coordinate external partners, review processes, and gradually take ownership of financial operations across the firm.

You will work closely with our Head of Finance, giving you a unique opportunity to learn, grow, and contribute directly to shaping the finance function of a venture capital fund.

💡This is not a typical finance role, it is an opportunity to grow into a venture finance professional. You will learn how funds are structured, how investor reporting works, and how finance supports innovation at scale. If you’re looking for a place to develop unique skills that will shape your career, this is the chance.

SOME AREAS YOU WILL TAKE PART IN

  • Take ownership of quarterly and year-end closings for our Manager, Holding, and other non-fund entities.
  • Support budgeting, forecasting, VAT, and tax filings.
  • Contribute to quarterly investor reporting and support investment performance tracking.
  • Assist with drawdown and distribution calculations.
  • Manage re-invoicing of costs between entities.
  • Review and approve expenses in Pleo.

YOU ARE A GOOD FIT IF YOU ARE/HAVE

  • 2–3 years of experience in accounting or auditing.
  • Strong accounting knowledge (Swedish GAAP K3; IFRS is a plus but not required).
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in finance.
  • Organised, proactive, and eager to learn.
  • Curious about venture capital, finance, and fund operations.
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills in both English and Swedish (written and spoken).

WE'D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU

If you are excited about working with us, taking ownership, and being part of an ambitious and passionate team, click below to apply and get the conversation going!

Please note that we do not accept applications via email or Linkedin.

A FAIR CHANCE

Pale blue dot is open to applications from all sections of society. We believe that you should be fit for the role, motivated, and experienced enough, regardless of ethnicity, belief or religion, family structure, sexual orientation or gender identity, or anything else that makes you, you.

companyDescription:
Cint is a global leader in research and measurement technology. We connect brands, researchers, academics, or anyone with a question, to the world’s largest network of over 800 suppliers, representing millions of engaged respondents, in 130+ countries. The Cint Exchange allows users to get their surveys answered and gather insights at scale to build business strategies, develop research-enabled solutions, publish credible research, and more.  Lucid Measurement by Cint, our advanced set of media measurement solutions, gives advertisers, media owners, and agencies the tools to measure the effectiveness and brand lift of cross-channel advertising campaigns in real time to optimize media performance while campaigns are live.  Both products leverage Cint’s global network of suppliers including panel providers, mobile apps, loyalty programs, and other online communities. These companies use our audience monetization tools to monetize their communities by matching them to survey opportunities.  

At Cint, we’re feeding the world’s curiosity. 

Cint Group AB (publ), listed on Nasdaq Stockholm (STO: CINT), has a global workforce of over 800. Cint has offices in Stockholm, London, New York, New Orleans, Singapore, Gurgaon, and Sydney, among other locations.
companySlogan: Feeding the world’s curiosity. https://www.cint.com/
descriptionHtml:
Vilka vi är

Cint är en pionjär inom forskningsteknologi (ResTech). Våra kunder använder Cint-plattformen för att ställa frågor och få svar från riktiga människor för att bygga affärsstrategier, publicera forskning med säkerhet, noggrant mäta effekten av digital annonsering och mer. Cint-plattformen är byggd på en programmatisk marknadsplats, som är världens största, med nästan 300 miljoner respondenter i över 150 länder som samtycker till att dela sina åsikter, motivationer och beteenden.

Vi matar världens nyfikenhet!



Jobbeskrivning

Om rollen

Vi söker en motiverad och kunnig ekonom till en dynamisk roll som är central både för de lokala finansiella processerna i Sverige och för den globala rapporteringen. Rollen är tvärfunktionell och innebär nära samarbete med team inom regelefterlevnad, redovisning och extern rapportering.

Den ideala kandidaten har gedigen erfarenhet av att upprätta årsredovisningar enligt K3 och besitter en stark vilja att utveckla sina kunskaper inom koncernredovisning. Rollen rapporterar direkt till Director of Accounting och har en indirekt rapporteringslinje till SVP of Finance.

Ansvarsområden

  • Ansvara för att upprätta och lämna in årsredovisningar enligt K3 och tillhörande noter, i samarbete med revisorer och externa konsulter.
  • Stödja skatteteamet med årliga deklarationer för svenska bolag, i nära samarbete med externa rådgivare.
  • Ha övergripande ansvar för månadsbokslutsprocessen och bidra där det behövs för att säkerställa korrekthet och punktlighet.
  • Utföra noggranna granskningar av balansräkningsposter och ge input på viktiga konton.
  • Delta i delårs- och årsbokslutsrevisioner och säkerställa snabb och korrekt leverans av underlag.
  • Leda, stötta och utveckla den lokala redovisningsekonomen så att arbetet utförs i tid och enligt gällande regelverk.
  • Delta i upprättandet av månads-, kvartals- och årsbokslut för koncernen, inklusive årsredovisningen.
  • Stödja rapporteringsteamet i koncernens månatliga IFRS-konsolidering i flera valutor samt i koncernens interna rapportering.
  • Hantera enklare tekniska aspekter av koncernens finansiella rapporter
  • Säkerställa att all rapportering uppfyller lagstadgade och regulatoriska krav, inklusive revisioner och offentliggöranden.
  • Samarbeta nära med lokala ekonomiavdelningar, revisorer och externa rådgivare för att samla in, analysera och konsolidera finansiella data.
  • Delta i ad hoc-projekt och bidra med stöd till både rapporteringsteamet och de lokala ekonomiavdelningarna

Kvalifikationer

Erfarenhet:

  • Erfarenhet av svensk bolagsrapportering där erfarenhet från koncernredovisning och börsnoterat bolag är meriterande, samt ledarerfarenhet.
  • Noggrann, strukturerad och van att arbeta mot deadlines
  • Stark systemkompetens, gärna i NetSuite och Aaro, samt mycket goda kunskaper i Excel med förmåga att analysera och presentera komplex finansiell information.
  • Erfarenhet av svenska årsredovisningar och relevanta upplysningskrav, där god förståelse för IFRS är meriterande.
  • En trygg kommunikatör som kan bygga relationer och presentera finansiell information tydligt, både på svenska och på engelska.
  • Proaktiv och självgående med ett starkt intresse för att ta ägarskap över projekt och ansvarsområden.

Kvalifikationer:

  • Kandidatexamen inom ekonomi, redovisning eller motsvarande.
  • Minst 5 års erfarenhet från kvalificerade redovisningsroller
  • Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både muntligt och skriftligt.







Ytterligare information

Please submit your application in English.





Our Values

Collaboration is our superpower

  • We uncover rich perspectives across the world
  • Success happens together
  • We deliver across borders.

Innovation is in our blood

  • We’re pioneers in our industry
  • Our curiosity is insatiable
  • We bring the best ideas to life.

We do what we say

  • We’re accountable for our work and actions
  • Excellence comes as standard
  • We’re open, honest and kind, always.

We are caring

  • We learn from each other’s experiences
  • Stop and listen; every opinion matters
  • We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion.



More About Cint

We’re proud to be recognised in Newsweek’s 2025 Global Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces®, reflecting our commitment to a culture of trust, respect, and employee growth.



In June 2021, Cint acquired Berlin-based GapFish – the world’s largest ISO certified online panel community in the DACH region – and in January 2022, completed the acquisition of US-based Lucid – a programmatic research technology platform that provides access to first-party survey data in over 110 countries.



Cint Group AB (publ), listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, this growth has made Cint a strong global platform with teams across its many global offices, including Stockholm, London, New York, New Orleans, Singapore, Tokyo and Sydney. (www.cint.com)

companyDescription:
Smart Eye is the global leader in Human Insight AI, technology that understands, supports and predicts human behavior in complex environments. Bridging the gap between humans and machines for a safe and sustainable future.  
  
Today, our technology is embedded in next-generation vehicles, leading the way towards human-centric mobility through Driver Monitoring Systems and Interior Sensing solutions. Our Research Instruments offer unparalleled insights into Automotive, Aviation & Aerospace, Assistive Technology, Media & Marketing, Psychology and many more fields. Our subsidiary Affectiva is pioneering Emotion AI, connecting machine logic with human empathy to gain a deeper understanding of how consumers engage with content and products. 
  
Smart Eye was founded in 1999 and is headquartered in Sweden with offices in the US, UK, Germany, Egypt, Japan and China. A publicly traded company since 2016, our customers include NASA, Nissan, Boeing, Honeywell, Volvo, GM, BMW, Geely, Harvard University, 70% of the world’s largest advertisers and 28% of the Fortune Global 500 companies. 

For more information: www.smarteye.ai 

Subscribe to our Youtube channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCNUWOTX9l2nno9u-0fl_FTA
companySlogan: Bridging the gap between humans and machines since 1999.
descriptionHtml:
Smart Eye is the global leader in Human Insight AI, technology that understands, supports and predicts human behavior in complex environments. Together with our subsidiaries Affectiva and iMotions, we are bridging the gap between humans and machines for a safe and sustainable future.

Today, our technology is embedded in next-generation vehicles, leading the way towards human-centric mobility through Driver Monitoring Systems and Interior Sensing solutions. Our Research Instruments offer unparalleled insights into automotive, aviation, assistive technology, behavioral science and many more fields.

Our subsidiary Affectiva is humanizing technology by pioneering Emotion AI, helping companies gain a deeper understanding of how consumers engage with their content, products and services, in automotive, media & entertainment, market research and beyond.

Our subsidiary iMotions provides the world’s leading biosensor software platform, that synchronizes data streams in real time from multiple sensors.

Smart Eye was founded in 1999 and is headquartered in Sweden with offices in the US, UK, Germany, Denmark, Egypt, Japan, Singapore and China. A publicly traded company since 2016, our customers include NASA, Nissan, Boeing, Honeywell, Volvo, GM, BMW, Geely, Harvard University, over 1,300 research organizations around the world, 70% of the world’s largest advertisers and 28% of the Fortune Global 500 companies.

Visit www.smarteye.ai for more information.

Smart Eye is leading the global market for Driver Monitoring Systems, which will soon be a standard component in every car. Monitoring the driver behavior is safety critical both for normal driving as well as for autonomous driving. We are constantly growing and are now looking for a Senior Accountant who wants to join our great Finance team.

At Smart Eye, we offer a high-tech work environment in an exciting and fast-growing industry. You'll have excellent opportunities for personal and professional development, be challenged, and have a lot of fun along the way. You’ll also be part of a team of highly skilled professionals and a workplace culture that is open, inclusive, and creative. We believe in true teamwork—where everyone’s contribution matters.

Our modern headquarters are located in the heart of Gothenburg, easily accessible by bus, bike, tram, boat, or car. And yes, our espresso machines serve the best coffee in town!

Your Role

We are looking for an experienced Accountant who can manage the entire accounting process related to monthly financial closings. In this role, you will be responsible for all tasks from daily bookkeeping to reconciliations, accruals, and final reporting.

As our Senior Accountant, you will lead the accounting and period end closing, as well as taking part in the daily tasks, working closely with all team members in the Finance team, especially our Financial Assistant.

You will play a key role in driving continuous improvements across financial processes.

Your Daily Tasks Would Be

  • Accounting & Reporting
  • Period end closing
  • Balance sheet and PnL analysis
  • Payroll taxes and benefits - validation and reporting
  • Project accounting and Revenue recognition (successiv vinstavräkning)
  • Cash management
  • Inventory accounting
  • Approval matrix compliance
  • Working capital management

You’ll have an active role in refining processes and supporting the company’s growth journey, working cross-functionally with other teams.

We will be calling for interviews after Summer vacations, starting in week 34, 18th of August.

Reporting to: Mats Benjaminsson, Chief Financial Officer

Location: Smart Eye AB, Headquarters (Gothenburg)

Must-have-skills

  • Senior accounting skills: +5 years of relevant work experience as Accountant or Senior Accountant
  • University degree within Business Administration, preferably Accounting, Finance or similar
  • Good capabilities in Excel, experience in creating spreadsheets with advanced formulas is a plus
  • Communication skills with fluency in both Swedish and English
  • Confident in K3 accounting

Meritorious

  • Visma NET and Power BI
  • International work experience and dealing with multiple stakeholders
  • Other languages
  • IFRS

Your Profile

We believe that a dynamic work environment is built on a mix of personalities and backgrounds. To thrive in this role, you’re someone who is independent and self-driven, with a curious mindset and strong analytical and creative skills. You bring a positive and enthusiastic attitude, are business-driven, and approach your work with openness, structure and a down-to-earth mindset. You’re also collaborative and communicative, and motivated by contributing to something bigger together with your team.

Interviews are held on a continuous basis, so we highly recommend that you submit your application at your earliest convenience. Welcome with your application.

companyDescription:
CareDx: Transforming Transplant Patient Care Through Novel Surveillance Management Solutions

CareDx, Inc. is dedicated to improving the lives of organ transplant patients through non-invasive diagnostics. By combining the latest advances in genomics and bioinformatics technology, with a commitment to generating high quality clinical evidence through trials and registries, CareDx is at the forefront of organ transplant surveillance and pre-transplant HLA typing solutions.

NASDAQ:CDNA

About AlloSure®

AlloSure is the first and only non-invasive blood test that directly measures allograft injury and identifies the probability of active rejection to better manage kidney transplant patients. AlloSure is a clinical-grade, proprietary Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) based test to detect donor-derived cell-free DNA (dd-cfDNA) in order to identify organ injury in kidney transplant recipients. AlloSure is analytically validated as a sensitive, specific, and precise measurement of dd-cfDNA. AlloSure detects active rejection (acute active ABMR, chronic active ABMR, or TCMR) with high accuracy, outperforms serum creatinine in kidney transplant recipients, and is highly sensitive in distinguishing ABMR from no ABMR. AlloSure is performed in the CareDx CLIA-certified laboratory. 

http://www.allosure.com/


About AlloMap® 

AlloMap Molecular Expression Testing is a non-invasive blood test used to aid in the identification of heart transplant recipients with stable allograft function who have a low probability of moderate/severe acute cellular rejection at the time of testing in conjunction with standard clinical assessment. AlloMap testing measures the expression levels of 20 genes from a blood sample. The combined expression of these genes is represented as an AlloMap test score. AlloMap is performed in the CareDx CLIA-certified laboratory and has been commercially available in the United States since 2005. AlloMap was cleared by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in 2008 and was CE marked for the European Union in 2011. Use of AlloMap is also included in the International Society for Heart and Lung Transplantation (ISHLT) Practice Guidelines, published in August 2010, the worldwide standard for the care of heart transplant patients.

http://www.allomap.com/
companySlogan: Creating life-changing solutions that enable transplant patients to thrive.
descriptionHtml:
CareDx, Inc. is a leading precision medicine solutions company focused on the discovery, development, and commercialization of clinically differentiated, high-value healthcare solutions for transplant patients and caregivers. CareDx offers products, testing services, and digital healthcare solutions along the pre- and post-transplant patient journey, and is the leading provider of genomics-based information for transplant patients.

We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Finance Accountant to join our team in Stockholm. This role requires background in finance or accounting and hands-on experience with NetSuite or similar ERP systems. The successful candidate will be well-organized, proactive, and capable of working in a fast-paced international environment.

This role is based on-site in Stockholm, with a requirement to work from the office at least three days per week

Responsibilities:

  • Prepare salary documentation for employees across multiple countries and coordinate with local payroll providers
  • Review payroll documentation and process salary payments
  • Review and process employee expense reports
  • Book vendor invoices and manage approval workflows via Centsoft
  • Match purchase orders to invoices
  • Prepare payment proposals for accounts payable
  • Amortize vendor invoices
  • Reconcile accounts and support month-end close
  • Book received customer payments and manage collections
  • Perform intercompany reconciliations

Italicized tasks are performed as backup during vacation periods.

Requirements:

  • Fluent in English and Swedish (required); German or Spanish is a plus
  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office
  • Experience with NetSuite or other integrated ERP systems
  • Prior experience in an international company
  • Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
descriptionText: CareDx, Inc. is a leading precision medicine solutions company focused on the discovery, development, and commercialization of clinically differentiated, high-value healthcare solutions for transplant patients and caregivers. CareDx offers products, testing services, and digital healthcare solutions along the pre- and post-transplant patient journey, and is the leading provider of genomics-based information for transplant patients.We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Finance Accountant to join our team in Stockholm. This role requires background in finance or accounting and hands-on experience with NetSuite or similar ERP systems. The successful candidate will be well-organized, proactive, and capable of working in a fast-paced international environment.This role is based on-site in Stockholm, with a requirement to work from the office at least three days per weekResponsibilities: Prepare salary documentation for employees across multiple countries and coordinate with local payroll providersReview payroll documentation and process salary paymentsReview and process employee expense reportsBook vendor invoices and manage approval workflows via CentsoftMatch purchase orders to invoicesPrepare payment proposals for accounts payableAmortize vendor invoices Reconcile accounts and support month-end close  Book received customer payments and manage collections  Perform intercompany reconciliations Italicized tasks are performed as backup during vacation periods.Requirements:Fluent in English and Swedish (required); German or Spanish is a plusStrong proficiency in Microsoft OfficeExperience with NetSuite or other integrated ERP systemsPrior experience in an international companyExcellent attention to detail and organizational skillsAbility to work independently and manage multiple priorities

companyDescription:
Europe’s largest private forest owner manufacturing wood products, pulp, packaging paper and renewable energy. 

The core of SCA’s business is the growing forest, Europe’s largest private forest holding. Around this unique resource, we have built a well-developed value chain based on renewable raw material from our own and others’ forests. We offer packaging paper, pulp, wood products, renewable energy, services for forest owners and efficient transport solutions. 

2024 the forest products company SCA had approximately 3,400 employees and sales amounted to approximately SEK 20 bn. SCA was founded in 1929 and has its headquarters in Sundsvall, Sweden. 

Offcial LinkedIn account for SCA.
descriptionHtml:
Placering

Sundsvall, SE

Ett hållbart arbetsliv

På SCA har du möjlighet att göra skillnad på riktigt. Med skogens kraft bidrar vi till en hållbar framtid och hjälper våra kunder över hela världen att minska sina fossila avtryck. Vill du utvecklas och tänka nytt så kommer du att trivas hos oss, för vi ger alla möjlighet att nå sin fulla potential. Vi vet att det är människorna som gör framgångarna!

Inom Business Services Finans arbetar vi mot målet att vara SCAs kompetenscenter för redovisning och rapportering samt med att leverera operationella ekonomi- och masterdatatjänster av högsta kvalitet i globala och värdeskapande processer, till hela bolaget. Som börsnoterat bolag är kraven på ekonomifunktionen höga och vi är mitt inne i en spännande transformationsresa där det pågår utveckling av människor, processer och system.

Nu söker vi vår nästa Accountant till Accounts Payable.

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

Din nästa utmaning

Inom Business Services Finans arbetar ett av våra team, Accounts Payable, i inköpsprocessen med hantering av leverantörsfakturor. Vårt mål är att leverera högklassig service till SCA och våra leverantörer – och du blir i rollen som Accountant en viktig spelare i vårt team.

Som Accountant inom Accounts Payable ansvarar du för att säkerställa att våra leverantörsfakturor hanteras korrekt, i tid och enligt gällande regelverk. I arbetet ingår bland annat löpande hantering av leverantörsfakturor, avvikelsehantering, påminnelsehantering och bokslutsarbete.

Du förväntas vara delaktig i olika typer av utredningsarbete samt bidra till att vidareutveckla och ständigt förbättra rutiner och arbetssätt inom leverantörsfakturaområdet. Du bidrar även till att säkerställa god intern styrning och kontroll samtidigt som du ger våra intressenter god service.

Vem är du?

Vi söker dig som har utbildning på minst gymnasial nivå med några års arbetslivserfarenhet eller relevant eftergymnasial utbildning. Tidigare erfarenhet av arbete inom redovisning, löpande bokföring och/eller hantering av reskontra är meriterande. Vi ser också att du har god systemvana och ett genuint tekniskt intresse. Vidare behärskar du svenska och engelska mycket väl i tal och skrift.

Som person är du initiativtagande, lösningsorienterad och kan snabbt sätta dig in i nya frågeställningar. Du är även noggrann och har god samarbets- och kommunikationsförmåga. Du trivs i en miljö med hög förändringstakt, har positiv inställning och vill utvecklas tillsammans med oss.

På SCA arbetar vi efter värdeorden Ansvar, Högklassighet och Respekt. Vi söker dig vars beteende samspelar med dessa värdeord och som vill bidra till en sund kultur på arbetsplatsen.

Vad erbjuder vi dig?

Vi arbetar aktivitetsbaserat i helt nya moderna lokaler anpassade till vår verksamhet och framtiden. För oss är en hälsosam och säker arbetsmiljö en självklarhet. Hos oss får du stora möjligheter att växa och utvecklas. Vi uppmuntrar till kompetensutveckling, till att ta ansvar och till att du förverkligar dina idéer. På SCA är vi måna om varandra och vi erbjuder ett öppet, stöttande arbetsklimat. Vi förstår också vikten av ett hållbart arbetsliv.

Som anställd hos oss får du jobba med den allra senaste tekniken och vara en del i omställningen till ett hållbart samhälle. Våra skogar och de produkter vi sätter på marknaden bidrar med en klimatnytta större än utsläppen från Sveriges alla personbilar under ett år. Vi tar vara på precis varje del av de träd som vi avverkar och producerar produkter från skogen som bidrar till att motverka klimatförändringarna. Att göra skillnad på riktigt ger arbetet en extra dimension.

Vill du veta mer?

Har du frågor om rollen så är du välkommen att kontakta Jennie Kjellin, gruppchef Accounts Payables, jennie.kjellin@sca.com

Frågor kring rekryteringsprocessen besvaras av Nina Sterning-Näslund, HR specialist rekrytering, nina.sterning@sca.com

Fackliga Företrädare

Maria Jonsson, Unionen, maria.jonsson@sca.com

Martin Holmvall, Akademikerföreningen, martin.holmvall@sca.com

Vi jobbar med löpande urval och tillsättning så skicka din ansökan omgående så kanske vi ses snart!

Sista ansökan är 22/9-2025

I samband med intervju kontrollerar vi giltig identitetshandling.

Om SCA

Kärnan i SCAs verksamhet är skogen, 2,7 miljoner hektar i norra Sverige, Estland, Lettland och Litauen. Kring denna unika resurs har vi byggt en välutvecklad värdekedja baserad på förnybar råvara från våra egna och andras skogar.

SCA har en effektiv och integrerad värdekedja med ett miljöcertifierat skogsbruk och moderna produktionsanläggningar belägna i anslutning till skogsinnehavet. SCAs snabbt växande skog binder koldioxid, bolagets produkter bidrar till ett mer hållbart samhälle och den förnybara fibern kan ersätta produkter baserade på fossila råvaror.

För mer information, besök sca.com SCA - Vi drivs av skogens kraft

Läs mer om SCA som arbetsplats på SCA som arbetsgivare – SCA

Följ våra ambassadörer på SCA (@wearescasweden)

  • Foton och videor på Instagram

Lyssna på några av våra medarbetare på Spotify: SCA

  • Vi drivs av skogens kraft
  • Jobcast (spotify.com)

companyDescription:
We are Three. We are the underdog that challenges the whole Swedish telecommarket. Since the start in 2003 our main priority have been to develop our products and our network so that our two million customers can have the best mobile experience as possible.

We are convinced that our culture is the main ingredient in our recipe to success. A culture created by everyone working at Three.
companySlogan: Det finaste man kan ge är lite mer
descriptionHtml:
Har du jobbat något år med redovisning och vill fortsätta bygga din erfarenhet i en trygg och utvecklande miljö? Vi söker nu en Accountant till vårt General Ledger-team. Teamet ansvarar för löpande redovisning, bokslut och rapportering. Vi letar efter dig som gillar struktur, uppskattar teamwork och vill bidra till att våra siffror landar korrekt, i tid och på ett tydligt sätt. Här får du en roll där din noggrannhet gör skillnad och där du kan utvecklas vidare tillsammans med engagerade kollegor

Om rollen

I den här rollen kommer du framför allt att arbeta med löpande bokföring, kontoavstämningar, månadsbokslut, rapportering och betalningar. Du är med och ser till att allt blir korrekt, komplett och levererat i tid. Du blir också en viktig länk till resten av organisationen när det handlar om att förklara, stötta och komma med förslag på hur vi kan göra saker lite bättre.

Du kan se fram emot att

  • få möjlighet att bygga din expertis inom redovisning och bokslut
  • vara med och utveckla våra processer och system inom redovisning
  • vara en del av att leverera kritisk infrastruktur och bidra direkt till samhällsnyttan
  • vara en del av Sveriges bästa arbetsplatser enligt Great Place To Work samt att vi är stolt samarbetspartner till Women in Tech och Tjejer Kodar

,

Vem är du?

Vi tror att du har en eftergymnasial utbildning inom ekonomi, kanske en KY-utbildning eller liknande, och att du hunnit jobba några år med redovisning. Det viktiga för oss är inte exakt vilken titel du haft tidigare, utan att du gillar redovisning och vill fortsätta utvecklas inom området.

Som person är du enkel att samarbeta med, nyfiken och tar ansvar för att saker blir gjorda. Du trivs med att ta ansvar för dina egna uppgifter men gillar också att samarbeta och bidra till att teamet fungerar bra. Du uppskattar en miljö där det ibland är högt tempo, särskilt vid bokslut, men där det alltid finns plats för att hjälpas åt.

Du behöver kunna svenska och engelska obehindrat i tal och skrift. Har du dessutom bra koll på Excel och erfarenhet av något större affärssystem (vi jobbar i Oracle) så är det ett plus.

Välkommen med din ansökan!

Vi tar emot ansökningar via denna annons och kommer hålla intervjuer löpande. Personligt brev behövs inte, men vi vill gärna att du bifogar CV och svarar på de frågor vi ställt nedan i samband med att du söker. Vi använder tester för att säkra objektiva bedömningar i samband med urval. Tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag. Bakgrundskontroll görs på slutkandidat.

companyDescription:
Our mission is clear: to become a lifelong mobility partner for our customers and communities by creating meaningful progress through clean energy, connected technology, and human-centered innovation.

Hyundai Motor Company is a global mobility leader committed to shaping a better future. With more than 120,000 employees across 200+ countries, we are redefining the way the world moves, not just through innovation in automotive design, but through bold investments in smart mobility, sustainability, and technology.

Each year, we bring over 4.6 million vehicles to drivers around the globe – from award-winning sedans to cutting-edge electric and hydrogen-powered solutions.
descriptionHtml:
Job description:

We want you to:

The job mission of this role as Financial Controller at Hyundai Motor Sweden is to Manage & ensure that the financial records of the company and the finance systems are maintained to a high standard. Identify associated risks within the business and proactively suggest / implement controls as necessary. To ensure internal and regulatory reporting is correct and timely. Perform to excellent standards and meet department objectives.
 

  • Manage and ensure that financial reporting is accurate and timely submitted.
  • Ensure accounting records are IFRS compliant for group reporting and monthly submissions are accurately produced and submitted in line with group reporting deadlines.
  • Ensure underlying financial records are kept to the highest standard and appropriate financial controls are implemented and adhered to.
  • Manage cash flow forecasts, weekly and monthly and monitor liquidity to ensure optimal cash management.
  • Ensure tax return submissions including corporation tax, VAT and PAYE returns.
  • Manage and ensure operations accounting processes are effective, accurate and well controlled including costing, pricing, parts, and warranty accounting.
  • Ensure balance sheet accounts are reconciled and reviewed monthly and controls are in place to minimize any risk and exposure.
  • Coordinate the external audit process and providing auditors with information and explanations.

 

About you:
  • Bachelor’s or master’s degree in accounting, finance, business, or economics.
  • 8+ years of relevant professional experience.
  • Good knowledge of IFRS Standards.
  • Good knowledge of Swedish laws and regulations.
  • Good knowledge of finance and accounting best practices.
  • Experience in preparing and maintaining finance and tax documents.
  • Experienced on large ERP systems. SAP knowledge desirable.
  • Fluent in Swedish and English, both written and verbal.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office, including Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Experience from the automotive industry is advantageous.
  • Excellent analytical, mathematical, and problem-solving skills.
  • Well-organized and detail oriented.
  • Ability to operate under pressure and meet time-critical deadlines.

 

We offer:
  • Culture: A role in an international comapany with great collueges.
  • Wellness Allowance: An annual wellness contribution to promote health and well-being.
  • Additional Paid Leave: Five extra vacation days per year, in addition to the statutory holiday entitlement.
  • Annual Bonus: Eligibility for a yearly performance-based bonus.
  • Occupational Pension: A high-quality pension solution designed to ensure long-term financial stability.
  • Enhanced Insurance Coverage: Comprehensive insurance that exceeds standard offerings.
  • Office Refreshments: Enjoy fresh fruit, snacks, coffee, and tea available daily at the office.

companyDescription:
We provide employee, financial and legal administration so that firms can invest and operate safely around the world. 

TMF Group is a single global team with over 11,000 colleagues in more than 125 offices across 87 jurisdictions, covering 92% of world GDP and 95% of FDI inflow.
 
We bring common culture and ways of working, investing heavily in our people and platform to provide a high level of quality and security to our clients. We exist to give clients a global solution to what otherwise requires many local providers, each with their individual operational complexity and risk.

Our clients include the majority of the Fortune Global 500, FTSE 100 and top 300 private equity firms. We see ourselves as a partner to them, keeping them on top of complex rules and regulations in the countries where they are active. 

We recognise that what we do is critical to our clients’ reputation and integrity. That is why we have made flawless service our single obsession. Great service starts with our people, so colleague and client engagement are the two measures we care most about, driving our management agenda and investment.
companySlogan: Global provider of critical business administration services. We make a complex world simple.
descriptionHtml:
Disclaimer

We never ask for payment as part of our selection process, and we always contact candidates via our corporate accounts and platforms. If you are approached for payment, this is likely to be fraudulent. Please check to see whether the role you are interested in is posted on our career website.

About TMF

TMF Group is a leading provider of critical administrative services, helping clients invest and operate safely around the world. Our 11,000 experts and 125 offices in 87 jurisdictions worldwide serve corporates, financial institutions, asset managers, private clients, and family offices, providing the combination of accounting, tax, payroll, fund administration, compliance, and entity management services essential to global business success. Please visit our website to read more about our business: https://www.tmf-group.com

Discover the role

As a Junior Accountant you will be responsible to support the team with efficient and qualitative service to portfolio of assigned clients in Accounting and Tax department. You will work in accordance with local Accounting and Tax legislation and in line with internal client’s regulations, as well in compliance with all internal procedures and standards.

You will report directly to Team Leader Accounting and Tax.

Key Responsibilities

You will work alongside the Senior Accountant to support these tasks,

  • Making entries into accounting system in accordance with accounting and tax regulations
  • Reconciliation works relating to various accounts
  • Responsible for preparation of financial statements (standalone and consolidated) for statutory purposes together with the Senior Accountant
  • Responsible for preparation of group reporting in the format required by the client together with the Senior Accountant
  • Preparing tax calculations / returns in accordance with the tax regulations together with the Senior Accountant

Key Requirements

  • At least 2 years of experience in full accounting area
  • Up to one year of experience in preparing tax returns and financial statements
  • Experience in Visma Business, Fortnox, and/or SAP is an advantage
  • Ability to prioritize client works and keen to learn new things on a daily basis
  • Bachelor or Master’s degree (preferably in finance and accounting, economics or related field)
  • Full proficiency of English and Swedish is a must

What’s In It For You

  • Opportunity to grow and succeed in an international environment: Learning opportunities: you will work with highly experienced colleagues who are ready to share their knowledge and will receive the opportunity to work on diverse and complex client requests. You will also get extensive individual further training opportunities, supported by our TMF Business Academy
  • Value-driven work environment: you can expect integrity and respect from all your colleagues and your leadership team.
  • Flexibility: We offer a hybrid working model
  • Ambition: we are a growing and forward-thinking company that values continuous improvement, embraces the last industry trends, and where innovative ideas are always welcome. Our team of high-level experts ensures we stay at the cutting edge of our field.

We’re looking forward to getting to know you!

companyDescription:
John Bean Technologies Corporation (JBT) is a leading global technology solutions provider to high-value segments of the food and beverage industry with a focus on proteins, liquid foods, and automated guided vehicles (AGVs).

Food and Beverage Solutions – Our equipment offerings include primary, secondary and further value-added processing, including chilling, mixing/grinding, injecting, blending, marinating, tumbling, flattening, forming, portioning, coating, cooking, frying, freezing, extracting, pasteurizing, sterilizing, concentrating, high pressure processing, weighing, inspecting, filling, closing, sealing, and packaging, which support a large and growing portfolio of food, beverage, and health end markets.

Automated Guided Vehicle Systems – Our Automated Guided Vehicle Systems offerings include stand-alone, fully integrated, and dual-mode robotic systems for material movement requirements with a wide variety of applications including automotive manufacturing, warehousing, and medical facilities.
companySlogan: A global leader that designs, develops and delivers solutions for high-value segments of the food & beverage industries.
descriptionHtml:
Overview ...

At JBT Marel, what we do matters. As one of the global leaders within food and beverage technology and automated systems, we know that the contribution of our employees, ultimately leads to the success of our business fortifying the future of food.

We are committed to our employees and will provide you with development and opportunities to allow you to be the best version of yourself at work, at home, and in your community.

We foster a genuine inclusive team culture enjoying collaborative working across our global teams to deliver world-class projects.

We encourage development ensuring new experiences and challenges at JBT Marel in order to feed your growth!

We’re looking for an Accountant to our Finance department in Helsingborg, Sweden!

The Opportunity ...

Are you an Accountant with a solid knowledge of K3, ready for a next and exciting step in your career? Look no further, we have a fantastic opportunity waiting for you in JBT Marel!

Responsibilities

We are looking for an Accountant to join our team in Helsingborg. The main areas of responsibilities will be:

  • Timely and accurate reporting of JBT AB on a monthly basis, both from Swedish and US Gaap perspective including preparation and review of journal entries and account reconciliations
  • Actively participate in monthly balance sheet reviews working with the business and SSC (Shared Service Centre)
  • VAT reporting and support to the orginization with VAT, TAX and other legal enquiries
  • Support internal and external audit requests and participate in efforts to remediate audit and control findings
  • Document standard operating procedures and instructions surrounding accounting procedures
  • Corporate governance for JBT AB including support with Annual report, work with external service providers on the Income Tax declaration and assist on board changes
  • Intercompany compliance such as monthly reconciliations and transfer pricing agreements
  • Share best practices in accounting practices with others in the Accounting CoE and support the Accounting Manager to implement them within the team


Besides this, included in the areas of responsibilities lays communication with Tax Authorities and Tax Advisors as well as Internal and External Auditors. You will also work with internal compliance and drive improvement projects. This requires a sharp eye and genuine interest for accounting both from a legal and managerial standpoint!

As JBT AB is owned by JBT Marel, a US listed company, it requires a strict internal control environment. JBT has complied with the SOx program (Sarbanes Oxley Act) since the beginning of 2002. This role will give you the opportunity to learn and to broaden your knowledge within internal control and processes as well as the accounting differences between the Swedish and US Gaap.

Qualifications

Our focus is to find the right person who can continue to grow together with us and develop the company. However, it is preferrable if you have a solid knowledge of the Swedish Financial reporting standard K3.

As a person, you are used to work independently with a clear structure and an attention to details. You meet deadlines and like to work within an environment that constantly change. We see that you have a high integrity as well as a flexible team player. The role includes several social touchpoints (both internally and externally) and therefore you must like to cooperate and have good social skills.

To be successful in this role, we believe that you have the following profile:

  • An academic degree within relevant field, for example in Business Administration - Accounting or similar education
  • 3 to 5 years’ experience of qualified accounting or similar
  • Strong knowledge in the Swedish Financial reporting standard K3
  • Very good knowledge in Excel
  • Fluent in both Swedish and English, written and verbally
  • Knowledge of US Gaap as well as SOx program would be the cherry on top and seen as a strong merit, but it is not a requirement - so don’t let this stop you from applying!


We are looking forward to reviewing your application!

In JBT, we welcome a diverse and inclusive environment. This is key when it comes to innovation and finding new solutions.

Why work at JBT ...

We offer you the opportunity to work in an established international and fast-growing company. It is important for us to create opportunities for our colleagues for personal development and a work environment where individual initiatives are valued. We have strong focus on continuous development and improvement, and always together as a team. The role is located in our office in Helsingborg, with the possibility to work from home two days a week.

ABOUT US

IsaDora is Sweden’s no.1 make up brand and since 1983 we have brought honest makeup to the many. We want to empower everyone to set their own beauty standards by offering affordable, high-quality, and easy-to-use products, without compromise. Guided by Swedish timeless values, our no-nonsense approach means that our premium products that do what they say, are safe in every way and accessible to everyone, no matter the amount of time at hand or size of wallet. Our Swedish roots are the moral compass that helps us stay inclusive, transparent, and responsible in everything we do. With IsaDora you don’t have to compromise on being you. That’s what honest beauty means to us.
descriptionHtml:
Join Our Team as Senior Accountant!

Are you a detail-driven, analytical finance professional with a passion for structure and precision? Then maybe you are our new colleague!

Isadora is a Swedish founded makeup brand. We are based in Malmö were we also have our production facilities. The brand has existed for more than 40 years and remains the no 1 makeup brand in Sweden and is also present in both Nordic, European and International markets.

We are now looking for someone who thrives in operational work and takes pride in delivering high-quality financial reporting.

Key Responsibilites:


  • Manage day-to-day accounting operations
  • Lead monthly, quarterly, and annual closing processes, including VAT-reporting and tax declarations
  • Ownership of the external audit process, including coordination and communication with external auditors
  • Ensure compliance with internal and external financial regulations
  • Drive efficiencies and improvement projects within the Finance organization
  • Collaborate across departments to support financial transparency and accuracy


The role is based in our head office in Malmö.

Qualifications/Profile:


  • Master's degree in Business Administration or equivalent
  • Broad experience in accounting and financial reporting
  • Strong knowledge of closing procedures, audit processes and tax declarations
  • A proactive, independent mindset with a hands-on approach
  • Excellent analytical skills and attention to detail
  • Strong ability to manage Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central or solid knowledge of similar systems
  • Ability to learn new programs and drive improvement through technology
  • Fluent in English


What We Offer:


  • A chance to join Sweden’s no.1 makeup brand!
  • A flexible working place where your opinion matters, and you can make real impact
  • The opportunity to be part of a growth journey within an established company with strong values
  • You'll be a part of a dedicated Finance team that values accuracy, teamwork, and continuous improvement while we are having fun along the way!


At IsaDora, we believe it is all about mindset, great skills and attitude. If you are aligned to our core values; to be honest and empowering, without compromise, are excited about the opportunity and great at what you do; driven by high targets and thrive in a fast-paced environment, we would love to hear from you. Apply now to join our team!

We review applications and send invitations for interviews on an ongoing basis, so do not wait to apply.

descriptionHtml:
About Us

Soundtrack is a B2B scale-up company providing music streaming services for businesses. We serve small customers like the café around the corner, and much bigger brands like Joe & the Juice, Toni & Guy and TAG Heuer. On the inside, we're a bunch of talented, motivated and humble designers, engineers and music experts. We believe in product-led growth, where the product is the primary driver of customer acquisition, conversion and expansion.

The Role

As part of the Accounts Receivables Team, you'll have an important role within our Accounting team. You will be working with our billing operations, ensuring invoices and payments are accurate and timely recorded.

This is a hands-on role in a small but global team, where you’ll have daily contact with our Sales and Customer Support teams as well as our customers worldwide

Responsibilities

  • Handle daily Accounts Receivable operations, including invoicing, credit notes, refunds, and tax exemptions.
  • Maintain accurate customer data and process vendor forms for new collaborations.
  • Respond to customer and internal inquiries.
  • Follow up on overdue balances, manage reminders, and coordinate with collection agencies
  • Actively participate in the month-end closing procedures thorough reconciliations and reporting
  • Collaborate closely with internal teams and external customer finance contacts.

About You

You're driven, solution-oriented, and thrive in a role where structure meets service. You can work independently but enjoy collaborating across teams, and you’re comfortable handling multiple ongoing tasks in an international environment.

Requirements

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in Accounts Receivable or similar finance administration role
  • Strong skills in both Swedish and English (written and spoken)
  • Proficiency in Excel and Google Sheets

Beneficial Requirements

  • Experience with NetSite and/or ADRA (used at Soundtrack) or other large ERP systems (SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, Oracle etc.)
  • Experience from a global finance environment
  • Education in Economics, Finance, or Accounting

Employee Benefits

  • We offer a flexible workplace with the possibility to work remotely
  • Generous pension plan
  • Yearly budget for health, wellness and personal development
  • Laptop and mobile phone of your choice with included cellular subscription
  • Regular social activities such as team events, meet ups, after works and off-sites
  • Daily breakfast served at the office

At Soundtrack, we believe that diversity fuels creativity and innovation. We're committed to building a team that reflects a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We welcome applications from individuals of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and identities.

We reserve the right to close this vacancy early if we identify a suitable candidate before the application deadline. To ensure consideration, we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.

Please note that we only accept applications submitted via our career page and do not accept applications by e-mail.

descriptionHtml:
Are you detail-oriented and analytical with a strong interest in retail and data-driven work? We are now looking for an AR Accountant to take ownership of securing accurate sales and payment transactions in a dynamic, cross-functional environment.


As our new AR Accountant, you will be responsible for ensuring the completeness and accuracy of sales and card payment transactions across GANT’s e-commerce, stores, and outlets for several of the Gant entities. Your work will focus on reconciling sales between our accounting system (SAP) and retail systems (Cegid Retail Y2 and Salesforce) and payment transactions through Adyen.

This role plays a vital part in securing accurate financial reporting and supporting internal and external audit requirements. You will collaborate closely with teams across Finance, Customer Service and IT.


Key Responsibilities

  • Perform daily reconciliation of e-commerce and retail sales and card payment transactions
  • Investigate and resolve incorrect or incomplete data in collaboration with relevant departments
  • Account for customer compensations related to delayed deliveries
  • Take ownership of relevant balance sheet accounts and associated reconciliations
  • Ensure compliance with internal and external audit requirements and implement improvements as needed
  • Support ad-hoc finance and reporting tasks in a dynamic and cross-functional environment


Profile

  • University degree in Finance
  • 2–4 years of experience in accounting, auditing, or finance, preferably within retail
  • Strong analytical skills and understanding of accounting principles
  • Advanced Excel skills; experience with SAP and retail/e-commerce systems is a plus
  • Fluent in English
  • Detail-oriented, structured, and able to meet tight deadlines
  • Proactive team player with an interest in data, automation, and process improvement


Please submit your CV in English by October 31. We encourage you to apply early, as applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Please note that the position may be filled before the set end date.


About GANT

GANT stands as an emblem of American Sportswear, symbolizing timeless style and innovation across 70+ global markets. As a company, we blend heritage with contemporary flair, crafting apparel for the bold, the curious, and the imaginative. Our brand represents more than fashion; it's a tradition of excellence and authenticity. As an employer, we foster a culture of continuous learning and creativity, inviting high performers to join our diverse team of innovators. At GANT, we're not just shaping the future of fashion; we're redefining what it means to dress with purpose and passion.

companyDescription:
The all-in-one sports platform for modern clubs.

360Player enables club directors, coaches, players, and parents to do more, using less time. We have developed an extensive but simple platform, gathering everything the club, team and player need in one app. 

360Player includes: 

Payments & Memberships
Manage payments and memberships for your organisation automatically, creating new revenue opportunities for the club while lowering the administrative workload.

Communication
Communicate seamlessly throughout your organisation. With the push of a button club directors can send information to thousands of people at once, while coaches and players can discuss exactly what they need to with integrated chat tools.

Performance Analysis
Ensure that everyone in the club has the right tools to develop. With 360Player's video analytics tools all team members can collaborate in creating clips and participate in match analysis. Combined with statistical reports, fact based insights can drive team development even further.

Training & Methodology
Easily distribute methodology including drills and sessions throughout your organisation to ensure everyone has access to high quality training content and resources. With personal, team, and club libraries, 360Player makes it easy to stay organised.

Management Tools
Managing the club or team should be easy. With 360Player’s club and team management tools you can handle all calendars and scheduling in one place and spend less time on administrative tasks.

Player Development
Make sure that every player has the resources they need to develop. With individual development plans and goal setting, players always know exactly what they can work on to reach the next level.
companySlogan: The sports platform for modern clubs
descriptionHtml:
About Us

At 360Player, we’re on a mission to change the game for sports organizations worldwide. From grassroots clubs to professional clubs, our platform helps them streamline operations, develop players, and manage everything in one place. Today, we support thousands of teams across the globe — but we’re just getting started.


Behind our success is a passionate, collaborative team. Now, we’re looking for a Junior Accountant to join our Finance & People team in Stockholm. This role is perfect for someone who is self-driven, prestigeless, outgoing and enjoys the variety that comes with working in a fast-growing company.


What You’ll Do

In this role, you’ll have a mix of responsibilities that keep things interesting — from handling accounts payable, billing, and expense management, to supporting payroll administration and sales commission reporting. You’ll also get involved in HR-related tasks like scheduling interviews, managing candidate communication, and drafting employee contracts. On top of that, you’ll help make sure our office runs like clockwork, creating a space that’s both productive and fun.


Qualifications

  • 3+ years of experience in accounting
  • Higher education in Business Administration
  • Proficiency in accounting systems, HR tools, and CRMs (bonus points for Deel, Teamtailor, and multi-entity accounting experience)
  • Used to work in Excel.
  • Fluent in English and Swedish
  • Highly organized and detail-oriented
  • Hands-on, unpretentious, and ready to pitch in where needed
  • Able to take ownership and work independently


What We Offer

At 360Player, you’ll join a global company that values collaboration, transparency, and having fun while building something great. You’ll work from our centrally located Stockholm office and have plenty of opportunities for professional development.


We move fast, we celebrate our wins, and we support each other every step of the way. If you’re ready to be part of an ambitious international team where your work truly matters — apply now!


Start date: As soon as possible (interviews ongoing)

descriptionHtml:
Aceve is in business to back the builders of society. We are the SaaS powerhouse, built by engineers, developers, salespeople, administrators, marketeers, controllers, and much mor. With over 500 employees, more than 950 million SEK in revenue and extensive experience in the field, we combine tradition and tech to provide a pioneering platform that helps our customers to achieve more. 

About the role

We're looking for a detail-oriented and proactive Billing Accountant to support our finance operations. In this key role, you’ll ensure accurate billing, maintain customer master data, and collaborate with Sales and Finance teams to drive efficiency and data integrity—contributing to a seamless and scalable billing process.


What will you do? 

As our Billing Accountant, you will be responsible for the end-to-end billing process, including:

  • Registering and processing customer sales orders in the billing system.
  • Preparing and managing accurate invoices in line with contractual and accounting standards
  • Executing billing activities on time and resolving issues proactively.
  • Maintaining and enriching customer master data in the ERP system in line with governance standards.
  • Managing credit note issuance and processing.
  • Supporting the Accounts Receivable and Billing team with reporting and analysis.

You will work closely with our Sales and Finance teams to ensure a seamless and efficient billing experience for our customers. You will also be part of the finance team operating within a Shared Services Center (SSC) environment.

Qualifications & Skills:

You are organized, detail-oriented, and enjoy working in a dynamic environment. You bring a proactive mindset and are always seeking opportunities for improvement. You’re also comfortable using ERP tools and understand the importance of accurate data and process control.

To thrive in this role, you’ll need to have:

  • 1–3 years of experience in a billing, finance, or accounting role.
  • High school or secondary education, with a Bachelor's degree in Accounting (or similar) preferred.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Excellent organizational and quantitative abilities.
  • A proactive approach and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively.

Bonus if you have:

  • Experience using NetSuite or Younium.
  • Familiarity with billing best practices, automation tools, and data governance processes.

Not ticking every box? No worries! We value potential and drive, so apply anyway or connect with us- you might be just what we're looking for in our growing organisation! Join Aceve and take your expertise to the next level, together we are redefining what’s possible in craft and construction solutions!


Aceve is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our selection process is conducted continuously and without discrimination, ensuring an equal opportunity for all candidates.

descriptionHtml:
We never ask for payment as part of our selection process, and we always contact candidates via our corporate accounts and platforms. If you are approached for payment, this is likely to be fraudulent. Please check to see whether the role you are interested in is posted on our career website.

About TMF Group

TMF Group is a leading provider of critical administrative services, helping clients invest and operate safely around the world. Our 11,000 experts and 125 offices in 87 jurisdictions worldwide serve corporates, financial institutions, asset managers, private clients, and family offices, providing the combination of accounting, tax, payroll, fund administration, compliance, and entity management services essential to global business success. Please visit our website to read more about our business: https://www.tmf-group.com

Discover the Role

As a Junior Accountant you will be responsible to support the team with efficient and qualitative service to portfolio of assigned clients in Accounting and Tax department. You will work in accordance with local Accounting and Tax legislation and in line with internal client’s regulations, as well in compliance with all internal procedures and standards.

You will report directly to Team Leader Accounting and Tax.

Key Responsibilities

You will work alongside the Senior Accountant to support these tasks,

  • Making entries into accounting system in accordance with accounting and tax regulations
  • Reconciliation works relating to various accounts
  • Responsible for preparation of financial statements (standalone and consolidated) for statutory purposes together with the Senior Accountant
  • Responsible for preparation of group reporting in the format required by the client together with the Senior Accountant
  • Preparing tax calculations/returns in accordance with the tax regulations together with the Senior Accountant

Key Requirements

  • At least 2 years of experience in full accounting area
  • Up to one year of experience in preparing tax returns and financial statements
  • Experience in Visma Business, Fortnox, and/or SAP is an advantage
  • Ability to prioritize client works and keen to learn new things on a daily basis
  • Bachelor or Master’s degree (preferably in finance and accounting, economics or related field)
  • Full proficiency of English and Swedish is a must

What’s in it for you?

  • Opportunity to grow and succeed in an international environment: Learning opportunities: you will work with highly experienced colleagues who are ready to share their knowledge and will receive the opportunity to work on diverse and complex client requests. You will also get extensive individual further training opportunities, supported by our TMF Business Academy
  • Value-driven work environment: you can expect integrity and respect from all your colleagues and your leadership team.
  • Flexibility: We offer a hybrid working model
  • Ambition: we are a growing and forward-thinking company that values continuous improvement, embraces the last industry trends, and where innovative ideas are always welcome. Our team of high-level experts ensures we stay at the cutting edge of our field.

We’re looking forward to getting to know you!

For further information, and to apply, please visit our website via the “Apply” button below.

companyDescription:
Vindex works with interim consulting and recruiting within the Finance Department. Each day we interview CFOs, Accounting Managers, Group Accountants, Financial Controllers, Heads of Business Control and Business Controllers for roles in our client's organizations and to be ready for a quick process for our next client. 

We only work with the finance department and in Vindex each recruiter is specialized in a part of the finance department, e.g. Accounting Managers. In this way we get better in the search and placement of candidates within our field day by day, both regarding interim and full-time positions.

Welcome with applications or your needs of new co-workers to the Finance Department.

Contact details:
Peter Bergmark || peter.bergmark@vindex.se || +46 735 218 318

descriptionHtml:
Vindex is currently recruiting a Financial Accountant to our client Einride. This is a full-time position where you will be employed directly by Einride, while Vindex manages the entire recruitment process. The role offers a broad scope of responsibilities within accounting, with a focus on both day-to-day operations and monthly closing activities.



About Einride


Einride is an industry-disrupting freight technology company providing end-to-end solutions for electric and autonomous shipping.

By deploying technologies that are integrated using its data-driven operating system, Saga, it enables customers to decarbonize their operations by making an immediate shift towards digitalized, electric road freight.

In 2019, Einride became the world’s first company to operate an autonomous, electric freight vehicle on a public road. Its vision is to make Earth a better place through intelligent movement.



About the role

Einride is now looking to strengthen its finance team with a skilled and structured Financial Accountant. In this role, you will be responsible for managing the overall accounting for multiple entities and take part in the month-end close process. You will also support the organization in financial matters related to accounts payable, accounts receivable, and general bookkeeping. The position is based in Stockholm.



Responsibilities


  • Manage day-to-day accounting for several entities
  • Oversee accounts payable and be a back-up for accounts payable
  • Customer billing and follow-up
  • Ensure timely execution and accurate recording of bank transactions
  • Payroll accounting
  • Reconcile bank accounts, payables, and receivables, including aging analysis
  • Prepare and execute monthly closing activities (e.g., balance sheet reconciliations, P&L review)
  • Support internal stakeholders with AP/AR/expense-related inquiries
  • Assist with VAT reporting and compliance
  • Provide documentation and support during audits



Your profile


To thrive in this role, we believe you are structured, analytical, and have a strong sense of responsibility. You are comfortable working independently and take initiative to drive your tasks forward. Accuracy is important to you, and you enjoy working in a fast-paced environment where attention to detail and reliability are key. You also have strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate across functions.



Required qualifications:


  • Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
  • Several years of experience in a similar accounting position
  • Solid understanding of accounting principles and financial processes
  • Proficiency in Excel and familiarity with ERP systems
  • Fluency in English, both spoken and written
  • Previous experience from a Big 4 company is considered a merit



Application

This recruitment is taking place in exclusive collaboration with Vindex AB. Are you the one we are looking for? Apply via the advertisement or send your CV in Word format, including reference number JKFC2956, to the responsible recruiter Josefine Kolm (josefine.kolm@vindex.se, 070-811 32 32) Interviews are taking place continuously so don’t hesitate to send in your application today!



About Vindex


Vindex is a recruitment agency that works with interim solutions and recruitment niched towards the finance department. We are specialists in appointing consultants and candidates within the finance function. This means that we appoint CFOs, finance managers, controller managers, Business Controllers, accounting managers, group accountants, accounting economists and payroll specialists.

companyDescription:
Go North Medical is a Medical Device Distribution company in Gothenburg, Sweden. Established in 2014, the company was founded by Glen Fotland to provide specialized software consultation for medical device companies looking for application development. After having successfully completed several software projects for a handful of medtech companies, it was time to expand the scope of the company and focus on medical device distribution and new product development. Today, Go North Medical represents multiple brands in both Neurology and Dermatology spaces.
With its roots in the Nordics (Norway, Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Iceland) Go North Medical has its primary focus, network and track record in these markets. With the cultural, linguistic and procedural expertise, Go North Medical purposefully positions itself as the leading partner for medical device representation in the Nordic market.
With a clear vision and ambitious goals, Go North Medical is expanding its portfolio within current business areas as well as evaluating potential for new products and new markets.

Services:
 
Agent
Products that are difficult to move or that have an established customer base are candidates for this model. Go North Medical offers agency model contracts whereby a percentage of the sale value is awarded as commission.

 Distribution
Go North Medical offers distribution model contracts. These are best suited when the goal of the contract is entry to market or growth. Normally, goods will be held by the distributor for local delivery.

Partnership
Our knowledge of specialty medical areas enable us to help and support the development and commercialization of new products to specific markets. These products carry a high risk profile, so picking the right partner is important.

Consultancy
For smaller engagements we offer short term consultancy agreements aimed to help with targeted tasks or time sensitive tasks.
descriptionHtml:
Bokföringsansvarig / Administratör till växande medicinteknikbolag i Göteborg


Plats: Mölndal

Omfattning: Deltid

Start: Enligt överenskommelse


Om oss


Go North Medical är ett specialiserat medicinteknikföretag med fokus på avancerad behandling av epilepsi genom vagusnervstimulering (VNS). Tillsammans med vårt systerbolag GNM Derma, som arbetar inom dermatologi med försäljning av estetiska och medicinska lasrar och andra medicintekniska produkter, bygger vi en stark närvaro på den nordiska marknaden.


Vi är inne i en tillväxtfas och söker nu en ny kollega som vill ta ansvar för bokföring och administration i våra bolag. Du kommer att vara en viktig del av vårt team och bidra till att skapa struktur och kontroll i vår ekonomi- och rapporteringsfunktion.


Om rollen


Som bokföringsansvarig/administratör hos oss kommer du att arbeta självständigt och brett med ekonomi och kontorsadministration. Du blir en viktig person i vår verksamhet med ansvar för både operativa och administrativa uppgifter kopplade till bokföring, rapportering och daglig verksamhetsstöd.


Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter

• Löpande bokföring i Fortnox för våra bolag

• Löneadministration och utbetalningar

• Månatliga finansiella rapporter och ekonomisk uppföljning

• Moms- och arbetsgivardeklarationer

• Kund- och leverantörsreskontra

• Avstämningar och förberedelser inför bokslut

• Ansvar för interna och externa rapporter (till exempel Bolagsverket, styrelse, myndigheter)

• Koordinering av interna och externa möten, resor och konferenser


Vi söker dig som har

• Eftergymnasial utbildning inom ekonomi eller redovisning

• Minst 3 års erfarenhet av liknande arbetsuppgifter

• Mycket goda kunskaper i Fortnox och Excel med starkt intresse för IT

• Flytande svenska i tal och skrift

• God professionell engelska

• Ett strukturerat, självständigt och lösningsorienterat arbetssätt

• Erfarenhet av arbete i mindre eller entreprenöriella bolag är meriterande


Du är noggrann, organiserad och har god förmåga att prioritera. Du trivs med ansvar och ser till att inget faller mellan stolarna. Du är van vid att arbeta metodiskt, följa upp dina uppgifter och kommunicera tydligt.


Vi erbjuder

• En möjlighet att växa tillsammans med oss som bolag i snabb utveckling

• Ett engagerat team med korta beslutsvägar och högt förtroende

• Möjlighet att påverka och utveckla rutiner och system

• Arbetsplats i Mölndal


Ansökan


Skicka din ansökan med CV och ett kort personligt brev till jonas@gonorthmedical.com så snart som möjligt. Urval sker löpande.


Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!
