companyDescription:
n8n is a workflow automation platform that uniquely combines AI capabilities with business process automation. The platform enables connection to any app or API while maintaining the flexibility of code with the speed of no-code.

Released under a fair-code license, n8n can be self-hosted and is supported by a vibrant community of developers and builders. Users can start simple and layer complexity as needed - utilizing the visual builder for quick wins, and adding custom Javascript or Python code where more control is required, enabling the connection of anything to everything.

n8n is privately held, with funding from Sequoia, Felicis Ventures, Firstminute Capital, Harpoon Ventures, and others. Individual investors include Eventbrite's Kevin Hartz and Supercell's IIkka Paananen. n8n was founded in 2019 and is headquartered in Berlin.
companySlogan: Workflow automation platform combining AI with business processes. The flexibility of code with the speed of no-code.
descriptionHtml:
n8n is a workflow automation platform that uniquely combines AI capabilities with business process automation. We give technical teams the flexibility of code with the speed of no-code, backed by a passionate community of builders. With 500+ integrations and fair-code principles, we're revolutionizing how businesses connect their systems and processes.

We were founded end of 2019 and currently:

  • 🧑‍🤝‍🧑 We’re a diverse team of + 140 talented people
  • 🚀 Our annual recurring revenue is growing over 9x year-over-year
  • ⭐️ With +130k GitHub stars, we are in the top 50 most popular projects of all time on GitHub
  • 🍾 We were the 25th fastest growing startup last year and 4th BtoB SaaS Rising 100 this year in Europe according to Sifted
  • 🌱 We were Sequoia's first seed investment in Germany, and most recently secured our $60M Series B (February '25, led by Highland)

🌟 Your main goal will be to ensure accurate, timely, and scalable accounting operations that support n8n’s hyper-growth while improving financial compliance and decision-making.

To do so, here are your responsibilities:

Accounting & Financial Reporting

  • Organize and execute preparatory bookkeeping for payables, receivables, and asset accounting
  • Coordinate and own monthly closings and annual financial statements, with or without external tax office support
  • Potentially insource accounting processes and implement efficient internal procedures

Invoicing & Dunning

  • Prepare and send accurate, sales-enabled customer invoices
  • Monitor payment statuses and manage the dunning process

Reimbursements, Payroll & Payments

  • Review and approve employee reimbursement requests
  • Coordinate payroll with our external provider and internal People Ops
  • Prepare and execute weekly payment runs

Strategic Projects & Automation

  • Set up and implement scalable accounting processes for international expansion
  • Automate finance and accounting workflows to enhance operational efficiency
  • Support documentation and continuous improvement of finance operations

Requirements

Must-haves

  • 🎓 Experience in Accounting: You bring 5+ years in accounting, including 3+ years in a high-growth tech or B2B SaaS company
  • 🔨 Accounting Tools Proficiency: You have hands-on experience with systems like Datev, NetSuite, Odoo, and modern AP/AR tools (e.g. Moss, Payhawk, Ramp)
  • 🔢 German GAAP & Tax Law: You possess solid knowledge of HGB and German tax regulations
  • 📊 Process Improvement Mindset: You identify and implement process enhancements with autonomy
  • 📝 Communication Skills: You're confident communicating clearly across levels and functions in English
  • 🔍 Detail-Oriented & Reliable: You're known for your precision and reliability in financial operations

Nice-to-haves

  • 🎓 Educational Background: Degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field, or certified training (e.g. Tax Clerk)
  • 💼 Audit or Firm Experience: You've worked at an accounting or audit firm
  • 🇬🇩 German Language Skills: Basic fluency in German is a plus
  • 🌐 International Exposure: You understand accounting standards like IFRS or US-GAAP and are familiar with financial consolidation
  • ✨ Tech Savvy: You're eager to explore new tools and automation for improved efficiency
  • 📖 Compliance Experience: You're familiar with VAT, transfer pricing, and international compliance topics
  • 🚴 Async Work Proficiency: You're comfortable working in remote, distributed, async-first teams

Why join us?

At n8n, your work won’t disappear into a slide deck. You'll play a key role in building a scalable, tech-forward finance function from the ground up during our hyper-growth phase. You’ll have ownership from day one, shape our financial operations for scale, and potentially grow into a team lead role in the future. Join a remote-first, autonomous team that values impact, experimentation, and continuous learning.

Sound like a challenge you’re excited to take on?

Apply now — and help us build the future of automation.

n8n is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

We can sponsor visas to Germany; for any other country, you need to have existing right to work.

Our company language is English.

You care about diversity and inclusion? We do too! Check out our Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging initiatives at n8n (https://www.notion.so/n8n/Diversity-inclusion-and-belonging-n8n-c1bec2fff536422d868b1a438d990e35).

Location disclaimer: If you see multiple job postings for the same role, it is most likely because we're hiring remotely for this role and posting in different locations to make sure every potential candidate can see the role. Please apply to the location you're the most likely to work from in the future.

Benefits

  • Competitive compensation 💸 – We offer fair and attractive pay.
  • Ownership 💪 – Our core value is to “empower others,” and we mean it—you’ll get a slice of n8n with equity.
  • Work/life balance 🏖️ – We work hard but ensure you have time to recharge:
    • Europe: 30 days of vacation, plus public holidays wherever you are.
    • US: 15 vacation days, 8 sick days, plus public holidays wherever you are.
  • Health & wellness 🩺 –
    • Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*

  • Future planning 💰 –
    • Europe: We provide pension contributions according to local country norms.*
    • US: 401(k) retirement plan.
  • Financial security 🛡️ –
    • Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
    • US: Short-term & long-term disability insurance, life & AD&D coverage, and additional hospital coverage.
  • Career growth 📈 – We hire rising stars who grow with us! You’ll get €1K (or equivalent) per year to spend on courses, books, events, or coaching to level up your skills.

descriptionHtml:
Vindex is recruiting an Accounts Receivable (AR) Accountant to our client Einride. This is a full-time position where you will be employed directly by Einride, while Vindex manages the entire recruitment process. The role requires the ability to work independently in a structured and detail-oriented manner.


About Einride

Einride is an industry-disrupting freight technology company providing end-to-end solutions for electric and autonomous shipping.


By deploying technologies that are integrated using its data-driven operating system, Saga, it enables customers to decarbonize their operations by making an immediate shift towards digitalized, electric road freight.


In 2019, Einride became the world’s first company to operate an autonomous, electric freight vehicle on a public road. Its vision is to make Earth a better place through intelligent movement.


About the role

As AR Accountant at Einride, you will play an important part in the daily accounts receivable operations. You will be responsible for ensuring timely and accurate processing of customer data, invoicing, and reconciliation tasks. The role also involves follow-up on outstanding receivables, reporting of aging, and working with factoring processes. You will work closely with other members of the finance team and support ongoing improvements within AR.


Responsibilities:

  • Register and maintain customer information in the finance system
  • Post accounts receivable entries across business segments
  • Reconcile customer accounts and ensure data accuracy
  • Monitor and report on AR aging
  • Manage factoring processes and related documentation
  • Collaborate with internal teams to support system and process improvements


Your profile

You are structured, detail-oriented, and comfortable working independently. You communicate clearly, handle recurring tasks with precision, and contribute to ongoing development of processes.


Required qualifications:

  • Degree in Finance, Accounting, or equivalent
  • Several years of experience in an AR or similar finance role in Sweden or within a Swedish company
  • Proficient in Excel and finance systems
  • Experience with factoring or AR reporting is an advantage
  • Experience from a system implementation or finance system transition is considered a merit
  • Fluent in English, both written and spoken


Application

This recruitment is taking place in exclusive collaboration with Vindex AB. Are you the one we are looking for? Apply via the advertisement or send your CV to the responsible recruiter Johanna Stenmark (johanna.stenmark@vindex.se, 073-441 44 65).


Interviews are taking place continuously so don’t hesitate to send in your application today!


About Vindex

Vindex is a recruitment agency that works with interim solutions and recruitment niched towards the finance department. We are specialists in appointing consultants and candidates within the finance function. This means that we appoint CFOs, finance managers, controller managers, Business Controllers, accounting managers, group accountants, accounting economists and payroll specialists.

descriptionHtml:
We need someone who can close the books and kick them open to see the bigger picture.  At Karma, your days start with classic accounting chops: journal entries, reconciliations, and really balanced checklists. By lunch you’re in the war room with our CEO Hjalmar, shaping budgets, sparring with forecasts, and pitching insights to the board over good coffee.

Why the "+", what's that sparkle?

👑 Board-level spotlight: sit at the table, speak your truth, change minds

📊 Financial storytelling: turn spreadsheets into strategy gems

🚀 Growth projects: VAT mastery, process optimization, expansion planning

🌱 Your career path to hack: Accountant → Finance Manager → ??

You'll Absolutely CRUSH This If You

  • Love both tidy ledgers AND messy whiteboard sessions
  • Translate GAAP into plain Swedish
  • Monthly salary, KPI, VAT reporting
  • Get a thrill when Plan meets Actual (and you explain the delta like a TED talk at our All Hands)
  • Strive at the office 5 days a week together with amazing colleagues

The Basics We Need

  • Finance/accounting degree
  • 2+ years experience working with accounting and month-end close processes
  • Swedish & English fluenc
  • That special "roll-up-your-sleeves" DNA and comfort with beautiful chaos

Your mission is to maintain and constantly improve an in-house finance function. Your goal is to ensure high-quality accounting and financial reporting. You will also take an active role in improvement projects.

companyDescription:
Cint is a global leader in research and measurement technology. We connect brands, researchers, academics, or anyone with a question, to the world’s largest network of over 800 suppliers, representing millions of engaged respondents, in 130+ countries. The Cint Exchange allows users to get their surveys answered and gather insights at scale to build business strategies, develop research-enabled solutions, publish credible research, and more.  Lucid Measurement by Cint, our advanced set of media measurement solutions, gives advertisers, media owners, and agencies the tools to measure the effectiveness and brand lift of cross-channel advertising campaigns in real time to optimize media performance while campaigns are live.  Both products leverage Cint’s global network of suppliers including panel providers, mobile apps, loyalty programs, and other online communities. These companies use our audience monetization tools to monetize their communities by matching them to survey opportunities.  

At Cint, we’re feeding the world’s curiosity. 

Cint Group AB (publ), listed on Nasdaq Stockholm (STO: CINT), has a global workforce of over 800. Cint has offices in Stockholm, London, New York, New Orleans, Singapore, Gurgaon, and Sydney, among other locations.
companySlogan: Feeding the world’s curiosity. https://www.cint.com/
descriptionHtml:
Who We Are

Cint is a pioneer in research technology (ResTech). Our customers use the Cint platform to post questions and get answers from real people to build business strategies, confidently publish research, accurately measure the impact of digital advertising, and more. The Cint platform is built on a programmatic marketplace, which is the world’s largest, with nearly 300 million respondents in over 150 countries who consent to sharing their opinions, motivations, and behaviours.

We are feeding the world’s curiosity!



Job Description

Oppurtunity

We are looking for a motivated and knowledgeable professional to join us in a dynamic position that plays a vital role in both local Swedish financial operations and global reporting efforts. This cross-functional role offers the opportunity to collaborate closely with teams focused on compliance, accounting, and external reporting.

The ideal candidate will have a strong track record in preparing K3 statutory annual reports with a desire to develop into group reporting. This role reports directly to the Director of Accounting, with a dotted-line reporting relationship to the SVP of Finance.

Responsibilities:

  • Take ownership of preparing and filing local financial statements (K3) and related disclosures, in collaboration with auditors and external consultants.
  • Support the tax team with annual tax filings for Swedish entities, working closely with designated external advisors.
  • Oversee and review the monthly close process, contributing where needed to ensure accuracy and timeliness.
  • Perform thorough reviews of balance sheet notes, providing input on key accounts as necessary.
  • Contribute to interim and year-end audits, ensuring timely and accurate delivery of assigned materials.
  • Manage, support, and develop the local accountant to ensure all accounting tasks are completed on time and regulatory compliance is maintained.
  • Assist with the execution of the major parts of interim - monthly and quarterly as well as the annual external reporting including any preliminary announcements
  • Assist the reporting team with the multi-currency IFRS consolidation process and monthly management reporting for the Group
  • Handle non-complex technical aspects of the Group's financial statements
  • Ensure all reporting aligns with statutory and regulatory requirements, including audits and disclosures.
  • Collaboration: Work closely with local finance teams, auditors, and external advisors to gather, analyse, and consolidate financial data.
  • Participate in ad hoc projects, providing support to both the Group finance and business unit finance teams





Qualifications



  • Qualified Public accountant/economist. Post-qualification experience in a Group function or a listed company being a clear advantage.
  • Proven experience in Swedish company reporting, with a strong background in leadership by expertise and example.
  • Exceptional attention to detail and the ability to prioritise tasks effectively and meet tight deadlines
  • Advanced proficiency in finance systems – NetSuite and Aaro a clear advantage - and advanced Excel, with the ability to manipulate and present complex financial data.
  • Knowledge of Swedish statutory accounts and relevant disclosure requirements, with a solid understanding of IFRS being an advantage.
  • Confident and effective communicator, capable of building relationships and presenting financial information in Swedish and English clearly and concisely
  • Proactive and self-motivated with a keen interest in taking ownership of projects and responsibilities

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or related field
  • Professional qualification in accounting/finance
  • Min. 5 year’s experience in qualified accounting roles
  • Native Swedish language, and fluent in English, spoken and written

Additional Information

Please submit your application in English.





Our Values

Collaboration is our superpower

  • We uncover rich perspectives across the world
  • Success happens together
  • We deliver across borders.

Innovation is in our blood

  • We’re pioneers in our industry
  • Our curiosity is insatiable
  • We bring the best ideas to life.

We do what we say

  • We’re accountable for our work and actions
  • Excellence comes as standard
  • We’re open, honest and kind, always.

We are caring

  • We learn from each other’s experiences
  • Stop and listen; every opinion matters
  • We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion.



More About Cint

We’re proud to be recognised in Newsweek’s 2025 Global Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces®, reflecting our commitment to a culture of trust, respect, and employee growth.



In June 2021, Cint acquired Berlin-based GapFish – the world’s largest ISO certified online panel community in the DACH region – and in January 2022, completed the acquisition of US-based Lucid – a programmatic research technology platform that provides access to first-party survey data in over 110 countries.



Cint Group AB (publ), listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, this growth has made Cint a strong global platform with teams across its many global offices, including Stockholm, London, New York, New Orleans, Singapore, Tokyo and Sydney. (www.cint.com)

Enthusiasm, team spirit and always looking to the future. These are the foundations underpinning the success of a great company.

Mapei is now among the world's leading manufacturers of chemical products for the building industry: with 98 subsidiaries operating in 59 countries and more than 13,200 employees worldwide, the consolidated revenue amounted to about €4.4 billion in 2024.

We offer long-lasting high-quality products that are mindful of both the planet and people. We draw on this same expertise to contribute to the most important architectural and infrastructural works globally, including projects in the housing industry and renovation of historic buildings.

Every day we are focused on increasingly sustainable building in the name of innovation.
companySlogan: Building a present designed to have a future. 🌿🏢
descriptionHtml:
🌍 We’re hiring: Regional Controller - Nordics



Are you ready to grow your international career in Controlling with a global industry leader? Join Mapei, where innovation, excellence, and global impact meet.


Why Mapei?

Founded in Milan in 1937, Mapei is a world leader in chemical products for the building industry, known for supporting some of the most iconic projects worldwide. We’re now strengthening our Corporate Finance & Controlling function and are looking for a motivated Controller to be part of this exciting journey.


🎯 Your mission

As Controller, you will:

  • Ensure the accuracy of financial records (P&L, Balance Sheet)
  • Analyze performance and highlight areas for improvement
  • Contribute to financial reporting, margin analysis, and forecasting
  • Help implement Group financial procedures locally
  • Collaborate on capex tracking and cash flow forecasting
  • Support budgeting at both local and Group level


💡 What we’re looking for

  • Degree in Finance, Accounting, or Business Administration
  • 3–4 years of experience in Controlling/Finance, preferably in multinational environments
  • Solid knowledge of business analysis and financial reporting
  • Fluent in English and Norwegian
  • Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
  • Team player with great communication skills
  • Initial training period in Milan


🚀 What we offer

  • International career path in a fast-growing, global company
  • Full-time role with future relocation to Norway / Sweden
  • Dynamic, collaborative environment with cross-functional exposure
  • Real impact and professional development within the Finance area


📩 Ready to take the next step?

Apply now and start your journey with Mapei.

📌 Please remember to include your consent to data processing (GDPR) in your CV.

companyDescription:
Valneva is a specialty vaccine company that develops, manufactures, and commercializes prophylactic vaccines for infectious diseases addressing unmet medical needs. 

We take a highly specialized and targeted approach, applying our deep expertise across multiple vaccine modalities, focused on providing either first-, best- or only-in-class vaccine solutions.

We have a strong track record, having advanced multiple vaccines from early R&D to approvals, and currently market three proprietary travel vaccines, including the world’s first and only chikungunya vaccine, as well as certain third-party vaccines.

Revenues from our growing commercial business help fuel the continued advancement of our vaccine pipeline. This includes the only Lyme disease vaccine candidate in advanced clinical development, which is partnered with Pfizer, as well as vaccine candidates against the Zika virus and other global public health threats.

Valneva has operations in Austria, Sweden, the United Kingdom, France, Canada and the U.S. with approximately 700 employees.
companySlogan: Specialty vaccine company focused on prevention of infectious diseases with major unmet need, such as Lyme & chikungunya
descriptionHtml:
We are a specialty vaccine company that develops, manufactures, and commercializes prophylactic vaccines for infectious diseases addressing unmet medical needs.

We have leveraged our expertise and capabilities both to commercialize three vaccines, including the world's first and only chikungunya vaccine, and to rapidly advance a broad range of vaccine candidates into and through the clinic, including candidates against Lyme disease and Zika.

With operations in Austria, Canada, France, Sweden, the United Kingdom, and the U.S., we are committed to making a positive impact on global public health.

Together, we are a global Company which is open to people of all backgrounds. Our diverse and inclusive workforce makes Valneva a great place to work.

We are recruiting for a maternity position as an Accountant

This role will support the Accounting Manager and the Finance Team by having responsibility for day to day management of several key financial systems and processes ensuring adherence to external and internal regulations for Valneva Sweden.  The role will also support the business growth through ad hoc project work from time to time. 

Some travel to our Solna office will be required as part of the responsibilities, likewise we will also consider applications directly from Solna.

Please note this is a maternity cover position for 12 months.

Requirements

For Valneva Sweden: 

  • Responsibility for a number of month-end close, balance sheet and profit and loss account processes 
  • Assisting with the maintenance and control of the purchase and sales ledger  
  • Responsibility for the timely payment of all suppliers in accordance with Valneva Sweden AB terms of business  
  • Deal with any escalated supplier queries and resolve promptly 
  • Fixed asset register management 
  • Maintain and reconcile cash 
  • Ensure accuracy and maintenance of Accruals, Intercompany and Prepayments 
  • Out of hours work as required by the business from time to time to support the accounting calendar 
  • Assist with liaising with auditors and preparation of year-end statutory financial statements 
  • Assist with preparation and information gathering for ad hoc financial modelling 
  • Assist with completion of tax documentation in relation to VAT including submission of return 
  • To undertake any other duties as requested by the line manager in accordance with company requirements 

Experience

Essential 

  • Either holder of a recognised Accounting Qualification, part qualified or be qualified by experience  

 

Desirable 

  • Have knowledge and experience of working in or alongside small and medium enterprises  
  • Have knowledge and experience of working in the pharmaceutical or healthcare industries 
  • Have knowledge and experience of standard costing 
  • Have knowledge and experience of internal controls and SOX 
  • Have experience of working with colleagues internationally 

  

Required Skills

  • Intellectually curious, with a desire to learn about their chosen industry / sector 
  • High level of consistency, accuracy and numeracy 
  • Proactive and positive mind set, which embraces change 
  • Excellent Microsoft Office skills 
  • Knowledge of ERP accounting and Microsoft AX 
  • Ability to be flexible and collaborate to support the success of a small team  


Benefits

  • Private Healthcare Plan
  • Pension Plans
  • Life Assurance
  • Training & Development
  • Employee Assistance Programme - Counseling
  • Childcare Voucher Scheme

companyDescription:
Bolageriet erbjuder en unik tjänst, där du slipper all administration med ditt företagande. Du fokuserar på det du gör bäst och Bolageriet sköter allt ifrån fakturering till färdig deklaration.

Att driva företag är inte alltid enkelt. Det ska reserveras rätt moms och skatter ska betalas i tid. Det här kan vara krångligt, det vet vi på Bolageriet. Därför har vi utvecklat vårt system som alltid reserverar rätt skatt och moms åt dig. Dessutom sköter vi löpande din bokföring. Allt för att säkra och underlätta ditt företagande.
companySlogan: Bolageriet erbjuder en unik tjänst där du slipper all administration för ditt företag. Tillstånd från Finansinspektionen
descriptionHtml:
Redovisningskonsult & Teamleader – Forma framtidens redovisning med oss på Bolageriet!

Är du en driven redovisningskonsult som är redo att ta nästa kliv - inte bara i karriären utan även i hur redovisning faktiskt kan fungera? Vill du leda andra, forma processer och arbeta med något helt annat än den traditionella byråvärlden? Då är Bolageriet AB din nästa destination.


🌟 Varför Bolageriet?

Vi är inte som andra byråer - och det är precis det som gör det roligt att jobba här. På Bolageriet har vi skapat en tjänst som utmanar det klassiska sättet att tänka redovisning. Istället för att fastna i timmar, pärmar och tröga system, har vi utvecklat en egen digital plattform som gör det enkelt för företagare att hantera bokföring, fakturor och betalningar - allt på ett och samma ställe.

Det betyder mindre administration, mer kontroll för kunderna och mer tid för oss att göra det vi är bäst på - skapa värde, både internt och externt.


💼 Om rollen

Som redovisningskonsult hos oss kommer du att få mer än ett standarduppdrag. Du blir en nyckelperson i vårt team och kommer även att ha ett teamleader-ansvar för en mindre grupp kollegor. Det innebär att du:


- Är ett stöd för teamet i det dagliga arbetet och coachar dem mot gemensamma mål

- Leder och utvecklar interna processer tillsammans med vår ledning


- Arbetar nära våra kunder och hjälper dem att förstå och använda vår plattform på bästa sätt

Självklart arbetar du också med:


- Löpande bokföring, månads- och årsbokslut

- Rådgivning och affärsstöd

- Utbildning och stöd för kunder och kollegor i vårt eget system


👤 Vem är du?

Du har några års erfarenhet som redovisningskonsult och har kanske jobbat på en traditionell byrå - men känner nu att du vill göra mer. Du är inte rädd för teknik, gillar att ta ansvar och tycker om att utveckla både andra och dig själv.

Vi tror att du har:


- Gedigen erfarenhet av redovisning

- Ledaregenskaper och förmåga att inspirera andra

- Teknisk nyfikenhet - du tycker att nya verktyg är spännande, inte skrämmande

- Stark känsla för service och en vilja att skapa kundvärde


🚀 Vad får du hos oss?


- En arbetsplats där dina idéer tas på allvar - du får påverka både vardagen och framtiden

- Ett modernt kontor mitt i Stockholm, precis vid Hötorget

- Ett starkt team där vi hjälper varandra att lyckas

- Flexibla arbetstider och balans mellan jobb och fritid

- Möjlighet att vara med och forma framtidens redovisningstjänster


Vill du ha mer än bara ett jobb? Vill du vara med och bygga något som förändrar en hel bransch?

Skicka din ansökan idag - vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!

title: Senior Accountant

companyDescription:
Overal van betekenis, dát is aaff. Geboren uit ABAB en Alfa, kom je ons met ruim 50 vestigingen overal tegen. We zijn graag dichtbij én werken het liefst mét mensen en vóór mensen. We zijn ambitieus en willen jou helpen om het beste uit je organisatie te halen. Dat doen we onder meer op het gebied van accountancy, audit & assurance, fiscaal advies, bedrijfsadvies én juridisch advies. aaff is lokaal betrokken en wij delen onze kennis graag, daar staan én gaan we voor. 

Als werkgever maken we het graag persoonlijk. Daarom hebben we geen partnerstructuur. Bij aaff zijn we met alle 2000 collega’s voor 100% eigenaar. We zorgen voor een inclusieve werkomgeving en zetten in op werkplezier en een goede work-life balance. Daarnaast investeren we volop in opleiding, ontwikkeling, kennis en innovatie. Tegelijkertijd willen we op allerlei manieren bijdragen aan een betere wereld. Echt impact maken! Het zit diepgeworteld in onze genen. 

Kom je werken bij aaff en ga je ook voor échte betekenis? Ontdek alle mogelijkheden op www.aaff.nl.
companySlogan: B Corp I Overal van betekenis
descriptionHtml:
Wat krijg je van ons?

  • Salaris: tot €4309.
  • Co-eigenaarschap vanaf dag één (bij vast contract): je krijgt tien certificaten cadeau en kunt meerdere keren per jaar extra certificaten bijkopen.
  • Regie over je werktijden: werk thuis of op kantoor, in overleg met je team.
  • Keuzevrijheid in verlof: tot 38 vrije dagen per jaar.

Wat vragen we van je?

  • Kennis en ervaring: je hebt net een hbo-opleiding afgerond (of bent hier mee bezig) in Accountancy, Finance & Control of een vergelijkbare richting.
  • Skills en vaardigheden: je kunt zelfstandig werken en taken snel oppakken, terwijl jouw eerlijkheid en openheid je communicatief sterk maken.
  • Mentaliteit: je hebt een klantgerichte en proactieve houding, je denkt graag mee over de bedrijfsvoering van onze klanten én hebt de mentaliteit van een co-eigenaar.

Assistent accountant mkb

Locatie aaff Ede

Tijd 24 - 40 uren per week

Level Young professional

Solliciteer direct!

Assistent accountant in Ede

Aangenaam, wij zijn aaff. De nieuwe naam in accountancy en advies met maar liefst 180 jaar ervaring. Bij ons ben je meer dan een werknemer; je bent één van de 2000 co-eigenaren. Met onze participatieregeling kun jij elk jaar investeren en zo meedelen in onze winst. Winst die er zonder jou niet was geweest. Maar co-eigenaarschap is meer dan certificaten alleen. Het is een houding, een gevoel. Ze zeggen ook wel: ‘run it, like you own it’. En dat is wat wij doen. Wij zoeken een assistent accountant die binnen aaff het verschil gaat maken voor onze klanten. Iemand die zich wil ontwikkelen in een dynamisch team en wil bijdragen aan zowel zijn eigen groei als die van aaff. Word jij éen van onze 2000 co-eigenaren?

Wat ga je doen als assistent accountant?

Als assistent accountant bij aaff werk je in een dynamische omgeving waar je veel kunt leren en groeien. Je ondersteunt je collega's bij het op orde houden van administraties en het voorbereiden van jaarrekeningen. Daarnaast help je ondernemers met fiscale aangiftes, tussentijdse cijfers, het opstellen van jaarrekeningen en optimaliseer je de administratie. Je krijgt veel verantwoordelijkheid en vrijheid in je werk en je werkt zelfstandig met klanten. Vanaf dag één krijg je een vaste begeleider die je meeneemt en inwerkt. Je komt in aanraking met diverse sectoren, zoals transport, horeca, hightech/maakindustrie en de bouw, wat het werk gevarieerd en uitdagend maakt.

De sfeer bij aaff is open, informeel en betrokken. Bij aaff bouwen we samen aan een betekenisvolle toekomst en duurzame relaties. Of je nu werkt met start-ups, familiebedrijven of gevestigde ondernemingen. Bij aaff krijg je meer dan een baan: je bouwt mee als co-eigenaar. 

Je Belangrijkste Verantwoordelijkheden Zijn

  • Controleren en verwerken van administraties dmv onze automatisering of onze scan en herkensoftware.
  • Opstellen van fiscale aangiften.
  • Optimaliseren van financiële administraties en automatisering.
  • Opstellen van jaarrekeningen en tussentijdse cijfers en overige administratieve stukken.
  • Jezelf ontwikkelen in het vak en bij aaff de mogelijkheid krijgen om door te groeien naar een meer ervaren functie óf door te groeien naar de functie van Accountant, als je die wens hebt, via het Young Professional Program. Er is altijd een passend opleidingsprogramma binnen aaff.

Jouw Arbeidsvoorwaarden

  • Salaris: tot € 4309 op fulltime basis, afhankelijk van jouw ervaring.
  • Keuzevrijheid in verlof: jij bepaalt hoeveel vrije dagen je opneemt, tot 38 per jaar. Kies uit regulier verlof, het aaff-budget of extra dagen via je salaris. Wat je van het aaff-budget niet gebruikt, keren we uit.
  • Ruimte om te leren tijdens werktijd: via onze aaff academie of een externe opleiding.
  • Duidelijk groeipad: met volop mogelijkheden om je te specialiseren of door te stromen.
  • Duurzaam onderweg: 0,30 per auto-km voor woon-werkritten, € 0,40 (€ 0,23 netto) per fiets-/wandelkilometer. Daarbovenop een volledige OV-vergoeding of – bij meer dan 7.500 zakelijke km/jaar – een elektrische leaseauto of mobiliteitsbudget. Bij elektrisch rijden ontvang je € 0,40 per kilometer.
  • Vergoeding voor thuiswerken: € 2,40 per thuiswerkdag én € 40 netto internetvergoeding per maand.
  • Thuiswerkbudget van € 550: om je werkplek thuis prettig en ergonomisch in te richten.
  • Jaarlijks € 200 vitaliteitsbudget: voor jouw mentale en fysieke gezondheid én altijd toegang tot het online platform OpenUp.
  • Flexibel verlof sparen: voor een sabbatical, langere reis of tijd voor jezelf.
  • Feestdagen omruilen: kies twee dagen die passen bij jouw religie of cultuur.
  • Laptop en telefoon: alles wat je nodig hebt om jouw werk goed te doen.

Is deze vacature iets voor jou?

We zijn benieuwd naar jouw verhaal en horen graag hoe jij ons als co-eigenaar komt versterken. Heb je nog vragen? Schroom niet om contact op te nemen met Alissa Vogelsang, onze regio recruiter, via alissa.vogelsang@aaff.nl of 06 39 23 48 71. Of solliciteer direct.

Solliciteer nu

Is deze vacature iets voor jou?

Word onderdeel van ons team en solliciteer nu! We leren je graag kennen.

Sollicitatieformulier (externe link, opent in nieuw venster)

Solliciteer direct!

Aangenaam, aaff.

Hét gezicht in accountancy en advies met 2.000 co-eigenaren. Samen ondersteunen we bedrijven in financiën, fiscaliteit, juridische advisering en bedrijfsvoering. Als co-eigenaar bouw je vanaf dag één mee aan ons gezamenlijke succes. Je denkt mee, ziet kansen en voelt je verbonden met aaff omdat je er zelf onderdeel van bent. Die mentaliteit merk je overal: geen hiërarchie of dichte deuren, maar korte lijnen, vertrouwen en samenwerking. Met 50 vestigingen door heel Nederland zijn we altijd dichtbij. Betrokkenheid om de hoek, maar met impact die verder reikt. Zo bouwen we lokaal aan iets groots – voor ondernemers, voor elkaar en voor de toekomst.

Aangenaam

Aangenaam

Sezgin - relatiemanager / bedrijfsadviseur - Ede / Arnhem

We spreken met één van de 2.000 co-eigenaren van aaff: Sezgin. Hij werkt bijna twee jaar bij ons op kantoor in Ede en Arnhem. Na 11 jaar ondernemerschap heeft hij gekozen voor een vast dienstverband als relatiemanager / bedrijfsadviseur bij aaff, en dit bevalt hem uitstekend. Sezgin beheert klantrelaties, geeft ze strategische adviezen en werft nieuwe klanten. In dit interview vertelt hij over zijn overstap en de flexibiliteit die hij ervaart om werk te combineren met zijn vele hobby's.

Lees verder

Twijfels over de functie, maar wel interesse in werken bij aaff?

Vraag dan een vrijblijvend kennismakingsgesprek aan! Of laat een open sollicitatie achter.

Vrijblijvend kennismaken Open sollicitatie

Zo bouwen we lokaal aan iets groots – voor ondernemers, voor elkaar en voor de toekomst.AangenaamAangenaamSezgin - relatiemanager / bedrijfsadviseur - Ede / ArnhemWe spreken met één van de 2.000 co-eigenaren van aaff: Sezgin. Hij werkt bijna twee jaar bij ons op kantoor in Ede en Arnhem. Na 11 jaar ondernemerschap heeft hij gekozen voor een vast dienstverband als relatiemanager / bedrijfsadviseur bij aaff, en dit bevalt hem uitstekend. Sezgin beheert klantrelaties, geeft ze strategische adviezen en werft nieuwe klanten. In dit interview vertelt hij over zijn overstap en de flexibiliteit die hij ervaart om werk te combineren met zijn vele hobby's. Lees verder Twijfels over de functie, maar wel interesse in werken bij aaff?Vraag dan een vrijblijvend kennismakingsgesprek aan! companyDescription:
IDEMIA Group is a global technology leader with more than 12 500 employees worldwide. Its two market-leading divisions deliver mission-critical solutions that simplify and secure interactions in the physical and digital realms:
 
•	IDEMIA Secure Transactions (IST) is the leading technology provider making it safer and easier to pay and connect. With unmatched expertise in cryptography and credential issuance, IST is trusted by over 2000 financial institutions, mobile operators, automotive manufacturers, and IoT providers worldwide. Every day, IST secures billions of essential transactions, ensuring the highest levels of data protection and convenience.
•	IDEMIA Public Security is a trusted provider of government-grade and convenient biometric-based solutions for 600 government, state and federal agencies. With decades of experience in biometric technologies, IPS enables safer, frictionless and fairer ways to secure travel, access and citizen protection.

For more information, visit www.idemia.com and follow @IDEMIAGroup on X.

descriptionHtml:
Job Description

Currently we are looking for Finance Controller, who will join our team in Sweden. The Team is doing their best to assure we are prepared for the audits. Moreover, no one understands Swedish accounting rules as well as they (maybe only accounting team ;)). If you feel that annual reports and analyzing data corectness in pivots it's your cup of tea, let's talk - both in English and Swedish!

A Our New Financial Controller You Will

  • Create the official yearly financial report, following Swedish accounting rules (local GAAP)
  • Coordinate both internal and external audit reviews and supports internal control annual self-assessment
  • Record different types of financial activities like salaries, taxes, and other costs in the General Ledger
  • Make sure the company’s accounts are correct by comparing different records and checking that all assets and debts are included.
  • Prepare monthly financial statements per entity, including Balance sheet, Profit and Loss, Trial balance, and any relevant analysis
  • Upload financial reports into the company’s system for the other teams to access
  • Provide tax schedules to support Value Added Tax and Corporate Income Tax returns
  • Ensure correctness and validity of the Fixed Asset Ledger, managing the fixed assets purchase orders, booking appropriate entries, monitoring activation, depreciation, and disposal of the assets
  • Ensure that the company’s stock and inventory records are correct
  • Handles GL accounting and reporting for medium-sized entities or contributes to accounting sub-processes

To Succeed On This Position You Should Have Following

  • Fluent English and Swedish - important for global communication internally and communication with local agencies
  • At least 3 years of experience in working with annual financial reports, coordinating internal and external audits and
  • Knowledge in Swedish taxes field, especially VAT
  • Excel skills - pivot tables level, managing data, analyze it and visualize in readable way
  • ERP experience - perfectly AX
  • Preferably studies in a field related to finance

The role may be located in both Malmö and Strängnäs. For both locations there is a hybrid model of working with at least 2 days from the office per week.

Ready to make a move?

Hit Apply And Share Your CV With Us. Our Recruitment Team Will Contact You Soon If It’s a Match. Then, We Will Be Happy To Know You Better Through

  • A HR screen interview to present the company and check your motivations and expectations for the role.
  • A second interview with the hiring manager to know more about the position and what will be expecting from you, the first knowledge check
  • A third interview with our regional CFO, who will talk with you more about your experience in coordinating audits, annual reports and, of course, get to know you better :)

We Offer

  • Wellness allowance
  • Voluntary insurance
  • Employee Assistance Program - professional support and counselling covering issues related to work, health or personal life
  • Bicycle leave
  • Access to Convini-kylar offering chilled and froxen meals, salads, sandwiches, drinks, snacks
  • Time off to medical purposes

Monika Brodowska

companySlogan: Making a Material Difference
descriptionHtml:
Vill du vara med och bidra till framtidens redovisning i ett bolag med historia, innovationstakt och samhällsviktiga uppdrag? Hos Heidelberg Materials får du både arbeta med kvalificerade redovisningsfrågor och vara del av ett team som vill utvecklas framåt. Vi söker nu en trygg och noggrann redovisningsansvarig som trivs i en analytisk roll och gillar att ha koll på både detaljer och helhet.


Rollen som Redovisningsansvarig

Du får ett helhetsgrepp kring redovisningen för bolaget – från det dagliga till årsbokslut och årsredovisning. Det innebär allt från att säkra månads-, kvartals- och årsbokslut enligt K3 och IFRS, till att fördjupa dig i koncernrapportering. Rollen är bred och kräver både självständighet, flexibilitet och ett analytiskt sinne.

  • Månads- och årsbokslut enligt K3 och IFRS
  • Rapportering till koncern (inkl. forecast och budget, balansräkning)
  • Moms, punktskatter och övriga deklarationer
  • Hantering av skatt och skatteberäkningar
  • Redovisning av anläggningstillgångar och investeringsprojekt
  • Leasingredovisning enligt IFRS 16
  • Upprättande av årsredovisningar enligt K3
  • Ansvara och driva förbättringar samt dokumentera och upprätta manualer
  • Intern kontroll och compliance


Vad söker vi?

Vi tror att du har minst fem års erfarenhet av kvalificerat redovisningsarbete, gärna i en större koncern med internationell närvaro. Du är bokslutssjälvständig, har erfarenhet av CAPEX, är trygg i att hantera skatte- och momsfrågor och har upprättat årsredovisning enligt K3. Du har även hanterat komplex koncernrapportering i större bolag. I grunden har du en ekonomiexamen eller motsvarande och du talar och skriver svenska och engelska obehindrat. Det är meriterande om du dessutom har erfarenhet av IFRS och erfarenhet av bolag med Shared Service Center.

Men erfarenhet är bara en del av den person vi söker. Du jobbar även strukturerat, ser detaljer, men har också förmågan att förstå och kommunicera helheten både till kollegor och externa parter. Du gillar att knäcka redovisningstekniska frågor, är lösningsorienterad, prestigelös och vågar ifrågasätta och föreslå förbättringar. Det är dessutom viktigt att du är självgående men samtidigt en lagspelare – här hjälps vi åt!

Varför Heidelberg Materials?

Hos Heidelberg Materials blir du en del av en global koncern med stark lokal närvaro och tydlig framtidsambition. Här kombineras stabilitet med förändringsvilja. Här finns goda möjligheter för utveckling, både för dig personligen och för bolaget. Vi sitter i ljusa och moderna lokaler vid Mälaren i Marievik, med närhet till både vatten, stad och kollegor. Arbetsmiljön präglas av öppenhet, engagemang och respekt – och vi jobbar aktivt med hållbarhet, innovation och utveckling.


Har vi väckt ditt intresse?

I denna rekrytering samarbetar Heidelbergs Materials med Mpya Finance. Urval och intervjuer sker löpande så skicka gärna in din ansökan snarast möjligt. Har du frågor gällande rekryteringsprocessen eller kring tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta ansvariga rekryteringskonsulter Elin Allskog, elin.allskog@mpya.se eller Maria Brisheim maria.brisheim@mpya.se. Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!


Heidelberg Materials är en del av Sveriges samhällsviktiga verksamhet. En del av våra tjänster är säkerhetsklassade. Är denna tjänst säkerhetsklassad kommer säkerhetsprövning genomföras innan anställning, i enlighet med Säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585)

companySlogan: Värden för välfärden
descriptionHtml:
Moderbolagsansvarig till Fastighets AB Stenvalvet


Bakgrund

Fastighets AB Stenvalvet är ett av de ledande bolagen i Sverige inom ägande, förvaltning och utveckling av samhällsfastigheter. Totalt äger Stenvalvet ett hundratal fastigheter med en area av ca 594 000 kvm till ett sammanlagt marknadsvärde om ca 16 miljarder kronor. I våra fastigheter bedrivs samhällsservice inom områden som utbildning, vård & omsorg, rättsväsende och offentliga kontor för myndigheter.

Stenvalvets ägare är långsiktiga institutionella investerare; Kyrkans Pension och Stiftelsen för Strategisk Forskning.


Om rollen

Som moderbolagsansvarig har du ett stort ägarskap för att säkerställa en korrekt och kvalitativ redovisning i moderbolaget. Du bidrar i bokslutsprocessen i samverkan med redovisningschef och bolagsansvariga. Rollen innebär även ett aktivt deltagande i det löpande förbättringsarbetet inom Stenvalvet, exempelvis genom att utveckla arbetssätt.


I rollen ingår även ansvar för att hantera och följa upp personalkostnader i moderbolaget. Det innebär att du arbetar med frågor kopplade till lön, pensioner och andra personalrelaterade kostnader samt utför analyser inom området. För att lyckas i rollen ser vi gärna att du har tidigare erfarenhet av personalekonomi.


I din roll rapporterar du till redovisningschefen, som beskriver arbetsmiljön på Stenvalvet så här:

”Vi har en öppen och hjälpande kultur där vi samarbetar och stöttar varandra. Jag ger medarbetarna stort handlingsutrymme, vilket också innebär ett tydligt ansvar. Hos oss arbetar du nära verksamheten och har korta beslutsvägar till hela organisationen.”

I dina arbetsuppgifter kommer du bland annat att ansvara för och arbeta med:


  • Löpande redovisning, avstämningar och periodiseringar
  • Månads-, kvartals- och årsbokslut
  • Årsredovisningar och deklarationer
  • Resultatuppföljning, analys och prognos
  • Uppdatering och ansvar för lånereskontra i Nordkap
  • Avstämning och analys av räntekostnader
  • Hantering av frågor kopplade till lön, pensioner och andra personalrelaterade kostnader
  • Löpande rapportering till externa parter samt kontakt med revisorer



För att trivas i rollen bör du vara en självgående och framåtlutad person som uppskattar struktur och analys samt tar ansvar för dina uppgifter.

För att lyckas i rollen tror vi att du har en eftergymnasial utbildning inom ekonomi samt erfarenhet av redovisning, gärna från arbete i moderbolag eller koncern.


Det är meriterande med erfarenhet av systemet Insikt, och vi värdesätter ett intresse för system och digitala verktyg. Hos oss finns goda möjligheter att utvecklas i rollen, så länge du har rätt personlighet och engagemang.


Varmt välkommen med din ansökan.

Du kan läsa mer om AB Stenvalvet här:https://stenvalvet.se/


Har du några frågor är du varmt välkommen att kontakta:

Jeanette Elm, 070-353 55 73 jeanette.elm@financefamily.se

Magnus Åberg, 070-981 77 51 magnus.aberg@financefamily.se


P.S. Vi jobbar kompetensbaserat och önskar därför inget personligt brev. Urval sker löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag.

descriptionHtml:
My Beat AB (MYBEAT) is a fast growing company in a scale-up phase, listed on the Spotlight Market, Stockholm. We are a mobile operator that delivers low-cost, high-quality subscriptions and personal service. The services are extended to power supply and TV/Streaming services.

The ideal candidate will be responsible for daily handling of our accounting and reporting. Also delivery of financial strategy, financial planning and analysis, financial reports, company audits, compliance, and bank relationships. You will collaborate with the CEO and the executive team to make decisions regarding company's financial strategy and operations. The candidate will possess a strong background in accounting and finance. Knowledge of handling legal issues in connection with a listed company is a pluss.


Responsibilities

  • Accounting precision, follow-up and reporting
  • Create and present financial and tax strategy recommendations to CEO
  • Support the financial planning and analysis efforts
  • Oversee cash flow, cash management, working capital, and company audits
  • Prepare financial statements and reports
  • Ensure legal compliance on all financial functions
  • Manage relationships with banks
  • Handle the relationship to the stock exchange, and related legal processes


Qualifications


  • Solid practical accounting experience.
  • Strong finance-based analytical skills
  • Solid finance experience
  • Some understanding/Insight into legal aspects and handling of stock exchange issues

companyDescription:
We are a single-source financial solutions provider dedicated to driving positive change while supporting the growth and ambitions of asset managers, allocators, financial institutions, and family offices around the world. 

Established in Bermuda in 2003, we have continually disrupted the industry through our investment in innovation and talent. Today, we set the pace in fund and asset servicing and stand out for our unique single-source solution and unified cross asset-class platform which supports the entire value chain, harnesses leading innovative technology, and benefits from cross-jurisdictional expertise delivered by a long-standing management team and over 13,000 highly integrated professionals.   

As a pioneering data and fintech-enabled company, we are a disruptor driving digital tools into fund and asset servicing. However, our vision to drive positive change extends beyond the industry. The Apex Foundation, a not-for-profit entity, is our passionate commitment to empower sustainable change.
descriptionHtml:
The Apex Group was established in Bermuda in 2003 and is now one of the world’s largest fund administration and middle office solutions providers.

Our business is unique in its ability to reach globally, service locally and provide cross-jurisdictional services. With our clients at the heart of everything we do, our hard-working team has successfully delivered on an unprecedented growth and transformation journey, and we are now represented by over circa 13,000 employees across 112 offices worldwide.Your career with us should reflect your energy and passion.

That’s why, at Apex Group, we will do more than simply ‘empower’ you. We will work to supercharge your unique skills and experience.

Take the lead and we’ll give you the support you need to be at the top of your game. And we offer you the freedom to be a positive disrupter and turn big ideas into bold, industry-changing realities.

For our business, for clients, and for you

Accountant

Role Location: Sweden and Denmark (Sweden Based)

Department: Finance

Employment Type: Fulltime

The Role & Key Responsibilities:

  • Processing monthly accruals and prepayments
  • Ensuring monthly costs are inline with prior month.
  • Identifying any big expenses that need to be taken into consideration
  • Explaining monthly variances with the reporting manager
  • Ad hoc requests from the reporting manager
  • Maintaining of aged receivables and related matters
  • Working with the accounts receivable and operations teams to ensure invoices are raised and posted to the financial system.
  • Ensuring month end invoice cut off dates are communicated to the AR team.
  • Processing deferred revenue on an annual, or quarterly basis
  • Preparing and posting of all accrued income as well as ensuring relevant monthly reversals are completed in a timely manner
  • Assisting in the month end process leading to the submission of accurate monthly management accounts in a timely manner
  • Assisting the revenue specialist with the monthly analysis of variances
  • Preparation of reporting and reconciliations for submission to the group’s Financial Planning & Analysis team
  • Assisting with the annual audits and liaising with the Audit Specialist
  • Assisting the revenue specialist with any ad-hoc requests
  • Discussing the monthly results with the Finance Reporting Manager, with emphasis on monthly movements.

What you will get in return:

  • A genuinely unique opportunity to be part of an expanding large global business;
  • Working with a strong and dynamic Finance team
  • Training and development opportunities.
  • Exposure to all aspects of the business, cross-jurisdiction and to working with senior management directly.

Additional information:

We are an equal opportunity employer and ensure that no applicant is subject to less favourable treatment on the grounds of gender, gender identity, marital status, race, colour, nationality, ethnicity, age, sexual orientation, socio-economic, responsibilities for dependants, physical or mental disability. Any hiring decision are made on the basis of skills, qualifications and experiences.

We measure our success as a business, not only by delivering great products and services and continually increasing our assets under administration and market share, but also by how we positively impact people, society and the planet.

companyDescription: Logent är en oberoende logistikpartner, med nordisk bas i norra Europa och globala nätverk. Vi har ett brett utbud av tjänster och skapar värde för våra kunder genom garanterade kostnads- och kvalitetsförbättringar. Vårt tjänsteerbjudande inkluderar logistiktjänster såsom lagerdesign och drift, transportledning och tull, hamn- och terminaldrift, bemanningstjänster och konsulttjänster. Det innebär att Logent har vuxit till en omsättning på cirka 2,2 miljarder kronor från starten 2006 och sysselsätter cirka 3 800 personer i norra Europa.

descriptionHtml:
About Logent:

Logent is the market leader in contract logistics in the Nordics. We offer our customers a unique and broad range of services where we strive for continuous improvement through robust operational processes and a focus on strong leadership. We are on a strong growth journey with an increased interest in our services.

We take care of our employees and offer all employees safe employment conditions and market wages. The journey as an employee can begin in different locations, in different parts of our company. We have approximately 3,000 employees in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, the Netherlands and Belgium in 2025.

About the role:

Are you a Finnish-speaking Accountant and dream of working with accounting in an exciting company with a team spirit and forward-thinking? Do you want a role with wide responsibility where you get to work with the entire accounting flow for Finnish companies and at the same time be involved in developing the financial processes? Then the role as Accountant at Logent could be the right challenge for you!

Your new role:

As an Accountant at Logent, you are part of an accounting team of 6 people and you are responsible for the entire flow of accounting work for several companies in Finland. We are continuously working on the development and streamlining of our processes, you will have the opportunity to be responsible and run your own projects as well as be involved in the work of updating accounting rules, etc. Your work also involves a lot of contact with the operations of the various companies and with external suppliers. You report to the CFO. The position is located at our nice office in Hammarby Sjöstad in Stockholm.

Your main tasks will be:

  • Be responsible for the daily accounting activities of several companies (AP, AR, GL, Fixed Assets, bank transactions)
  • Meet group deadlines at each monthly closing for several companies
  • Perform account reconciliation on a monthly basis in the group reporting tool
  • Declare VAT for several companies and meet deadlines and ensure that payment takes place
  • Declare tax and social charges on the set date and ensure that payment takes place
  • Submit periodic compulsory reporting on time for relevant companies
  • Ensure that VAT codes are correctly set up in accounting software
  • Ensure that the internal invoicing takes place efficiently to ease the reconciliation process
  • Contribute to improvement / automation project for accounting processes
  • Primary contact person for the yearly audit
  • Primary contact person for the local administration (tax office, company house…)
  • Be involved in budget and forecast work
  • Be involved in the production of a financial handbook

Your profile:

We are looking for a person that has a relevant education in finance and at least 3 years of experience from qualified accounting work. You are fluent in Finnish and English, and we also see that you have good knowledge of Excel as it is used for analysis work and reconciliation. We would like to see that you have good system skills and an interest in developing processes more efficiently.

You like to take responsibility and are driven by challenges. As a person, you are open, down-to-earth and thrive in an environment where people help each other and take responsibility. The position itself requires a meticulous person with problem-solving skills who delivers quality and maintains good communication with those around them.

Application:

Selection is made on an ongoing basis and the position may be filled before the application deadline. We value your personal qualities, commitment and suitability. If you have questions about the role or the process, please contact Thérèse Grivérus, therese.griverus@logent.se. We do not process applications via email due to GDPR.

The position will be filled after summer.

Welcome with your application!

descriptionHtml:
AbbVie is looking for a Senior Accountant to take on a key role in our Nordic finance team. You will work both strategically and operationally, ensuring the highest quality in our financial processes.


This role involves close collaboration with centralized functions such as Financial Shared Services (FSS) and Centers of Excellence (CoE), including Tax and Treasury.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Ensure accurate and efficient reporting in compliance with Swedish regulations and US GAAP.
  • Drive improvement initiatives with FSS in areas such as asset management, account reconciliation and leasing.
  • Strengthen internal control systems in compliance with legal and internal policies while introducing innovative solutions to enhance efficiency.
  • Be a champion of continuous improvement. Lead efforts for ongoing process improvement, proactively identifying opportunities for optimization and ensuring adherence to AbbVie´s quality standards and policies.
  • Deliver insightful analysis of key metrics such as Gross To Net and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) to senior management.
  • Lead VAT and corporate income tax declarations and manage relationships with external banking partners.


We´re looking for someone who has:

  • A Bachelor´s degree in Business Administration, Accounting, or Finance
  • At least 5 years of experience in accounting, with solid knowledge of both Swedish GAAP and US GAAP
  • Strong analytical skills and a solution-oriented mindset with advanced Excel skills
  • Excellent communication skills and experience working with internal and external stakeholders
  • Fluency in English, both written and spoken


Why AbbVie?

At AbbVie, you´ll be part of a global company with a strong focus on innovation, quality, and sustainability. We offer a dynamic work environment where you can grow, make an impact, and contribute to something bigger – improving people´s lives. Apply today and become part of our dedicated team!

In this recruitment process, AbbVie are collaborating with Jurek Recruitment and Consulting. For questions regarding the position, please contact the responsible recruitment consultant Matilda Thelin at matilda.thelin@jurek.se. Due to GDPR regulations, we do not accept applications via

email.

companyDescription:
Founded in 2010, Bannerflow is a Creative Management Platform (CMP) that enables marketing teams to manage the entire campaign lifecycle, from start to finish, across languages, formats, and markets – with full transparency over cost and performance.

Bannerflow supports many digital ad formats such as Display, Social, Video, Mobile, CRM/onsite advertising and even new channels such as DOOH. Creating personalised (DCO) and channel-specific campaigns is also easily performed — resulting in higher engagement, greater impact, and increased ROI.

Bannerflow has been named as one of Europe's fastest-growing tech companies. It has been on the Deloitte Tech 50 for three years straight, on the Financial Times FT1000 for three years in a row, and been awarded Mäster Di Gasell status. 

Follow us on LinkedIn to learn more about the latest technology and trends shaping digital marketing.
companySlogan: Great ads start here. Automate your production workflow and create ads at scale – effortlessly.
descriptionHtml:
Ready for a new challenge? Here is what we offer.

Team spirit, built upon trust & ownership.

Together, we decide how to win. We trust each other and take great ownership in what we do. We respect the individual, assume the best in others and believe in close collaboration and communication, within and outside of the team.

New challenges and personal growth.

We live by our entrepreneurial spirit, this means taking risks, identifying opportunities and always seeking new solutions. Do you have ideas how we can scale even faster? Bring them on!

A passionate and fun culture.

We are passionate about our work and believe that having lots of fun is essential for both creativity and success. Welcome to a diverse and inclusive workplace, with a lot of fun and exciting activities going on- including Health Month, inspirational sessions, team activities, company trips and board game nights.

A stable and global industry-leader based in Stockholm, expanding and scaling to new markets!

We’re in a great position to drive success and seize new opportunities, our international customer base is constantly growing and so is our product. With the support of our new owners, we are making moves to expand into new markets, setting a clear course for growth in 2025.

Are you + Bannerflow = true?

As Financial Controller, you’ll be a key partner across the business, driving accurate reporting, analysis, and forecasting that enable smart decisions and sustainable growth. You’ll bring strong financial expertise, SaaS know-how, and business insight to help us scale faster.

Your Key Responsibilities

  • Deliver accurate monthly, quarterly & annual financial reports
  • Lead budgeting & forecasting with department heads
  • Build and maintain KPI dashboards (incl. SaaS metrics)
  • Support cash flow forecasting & scenario analysis
  • Ensure smooth, accurate month-end close with accounting
  • Continuously improve tools, processes & reporting
  • Support audits and ensure compliance with controls & policies

What We’re Looking For

To enjoy working at Bannerflow, you should be eager to learn new things and happy to take on challenges. To succeed at Bannerflow, you need to be passionate and have a black belt in collaboration. As the Bannerflow team is constantly growing, it’s important that you embrace change and thrive in an environment where the landscape is always evolving.

Qualifications

  • Degree in Finance, Business, Accounting, Economics, or related field
  • 3+ years in financial controlling, FP&A, or similar role
  • Experience with multi-entity setups, intercompany transactions & foreign exchange exposure
  • Solid grasp of accounting principles & reporting standards
  • Analytical, detail-oriented, and solutions-driven
  • Independent, proactive, and communicative
  • Skilled in financial modeling (esp. SaaS metrics)
  • Strong Excel and financial/reporting tool expertise
  • Tech-savvy and quick to learn new systems

At Bannerflow, we live by our values of passion, collaboration, and challenge. As an Financial Controller you’ll help us continue to revolutionize the Ad Tech industry through cutting-edge technology and product excellence. You’ll also get to enjoy our fun and engaging culture with activities like padel tournaments, webinars, Friday beers, and Level-up Hackathons. We offer a hybrid workplace and a competitive compensation package including pension according to ITP1, health allowance, parental leave top-up, and health care insurance. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team and welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds, experiences, and abilities.

companyDescription:
With annual sales of about CHF 10.5 billion in fiscal year 2023/24, the Zurich-based Barry Callebaut Group is the world’s leading manufacturer of chocolate and cocoa products – from sourcing and processing cocoa beans to producing the finest chocolates, including chocolate fillings, decorations and compounds. 

The Group runs more than 60 production facilities worldwide and employs a diverse and dedicated global workforce of more than 13,000 people. 

The Barry Callebaut Group serves the entire food industry, from industrial food manufacturers to artisanal and professional users of chocolate, such as chocolatiers, pastry chefs, bakers, hotels, restaurants or caterers. The global brands catering to the specific needs of these Gourmet customers are Callebaut® and Cacao Barry®, Carma® and the decorations specialist Mona Lisa®. 

The Barry Callebaut Group is committed to make sustainable chocolate the norm to help ensure future supplies of cocoa and improve farmer livelihoods. It supports the Cocoa Horizons Foundation in its goal to shape a sustainable cocoa and chocolate future.
companySlogan: We make sustainable chocolate the norm!
descriptionHtml:
About The Role

The Chief Accountant is responsible for end-to-end IFRS and statutory reporting for the assigned country or legal entity. The role ensures timely execution of accounting activities in accordance with group-wide principles and standards. It includes documenting and reviewing financial statements to ensure compliance with relevant regulations and contributes to the implementation of standardized processes and the Finance Target Operating Model.

This position works closely with the Global Business Services (GBS) team and receives guidance from the Manager Accounting & Tax Compliance EMEA on complex activities and ad hoc requests.

Scope

This role covers the beverage site in Kågeröd, Sweden.

Key Responsibilities

  • Coordinate monthly, half-year, and year-end closings, including BOF reporting
  • Perform balance sheet reviews and statutory accounting
  • Oversee tax and pension accounting (IFRS 19), and manage technical accounting topics
  • Ensure governance of internal control systems (ICS)
  • Collaborate with GBS on payroll and inventory accounting
  • Provide guidance to GBS and other finance teams (Consolidation, Tax, Treasury, GAR)
  • Govern the quality, consistency, and integrity of financials
  • Share accounting expertise across EMEA
  • Manage service level agreements with Shared Services
  • Drive continuous improvement in accounting processes
  • Support finance-related projects
  • Maintain relationships with external stakeholders and the finance community

About You

You are a finance professional with strong technical expertise and a collaborative mindset. You thrive in a dynamic environment and are committed to delivering high-quality results.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field
  • Business proficiency in English (written and spoken)
  • Proficiency in SAP or other ERP systems
  • ACCA DipIFR or ACCA/CPA membership is a plus
  • Strong knowledge of IFRS and local GAAP (Sweden and Denmark)

Experience

  • Extensive experience in finance with a focus on accounting
  • Experience in a multinational company, preferably in B2B or food industries
  • Solid understanding of accounting, controlling, and financial reporting methodologies

Personal Competencies

  • Analytical, hands-on, and solution-oriented
  • Strong communication skills and high integrity
  • Team player with the ability to work in a multi-tasking environment
  • Systematic problem-solver with a proactive mindset
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks efficiently and with urgency

At Barry Callebaut, we are committed to Diversity & Inclusion. United by our strong values, we thrive on the diversity of who we are, where we come from, what we’ve experienced and how we think. We are committed to nurturing an inclusive environment where people can truly be themselves, grow to their full potential and feel they belong. #oneBC - Diverse People, Sustainable Growth.

You thrive in a dynamic environment and are committed to delivering high-quality results.QualificationsBachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or related fieldBusiness proficiency in English (written and spoken)Proficiency in SAP or other ERP systemsACCA DipIFR or ACCA/CPA membership is a plusStrong knowledge of IFRS and local GAAP (Sweden and Denmark)ExperienceExtensive experience in finance with a focus on accountingExperience in a multinational company, preferably in B2B or food industriesSolid understanding of accounting, controlling, and financial reporting methodologiesPersonal CompetenciesAnalytical, hands-on, and solution-orientedStrong communication skills and high integrityTeam player with the ability to work in a multi-tasking environmentSystematic problem-solver with a proactive mindsetAbility to manage multiple tasks efficiently and with urgencyAt Barry Callebaut, we are committed to Diversity & Inclusion. United by our strong values, we thrive on the diversity of who we are, where we come from, what we’ve experienced and how we think. We are committed to nurturing an inclusive environment where people can truly be themselves, grow to their full potential and feel they belong. #oneBC - Diverse People, Sustainable Growth.

companyDescription:
Varför krångla till det när det inte behöver vara svårt?
Det var frågan vi ställde oss och bestämde oss för att göra något åt saken.

Tillsammans har vi många års erfarenhet av både konsultlösningar, rekrytering och ledarskap inom ekonomiområdet. Genom att förenkla processerna från start till mål med digitala verktyg, kan vi jobba smartare och erbjuda våra tjänster snabbare och mer kostnadseffektivt än vad andra branschkollegor gör.

Den tid vi frigör vill vi istället använda till att fördjupa relationerna med våra kunder och konsulter. För vi vet av erfarenhet att det är just engagemang, glädje och tillit som gör skillnaden.

Välkommen att utmana oss!

descriptionHtml:
Vill du utvecklas i en internationell miljö i ett bolag inom medicinteknik och bidra till att förbättra livskvaliteten för människor världen över? Vi söker nu dig som är redo att ta nästa steg som Accountant och vill bli en viktig del av ett växande företag med passion för innovation, samarbete och hållbarhet. Företaget är en ledande global leverantör av avancerade lösningar inom medicinteknik.


I denna roll stöttar du såväl säljbolagen i EMEA som holdingbolaget i Sverige. Du arbetar tätt ihop med säljorganisationen och den övriga ekonomifunktionen, där flera av kollegorna utgår från andra orter och länder. Din arbetsplats blir i Uppsala tillsammans med den nordiska säljorganisationen och holdingbolaget. Här möter du en vardag i förändring, där nya utmaningar och möjligheter är en del av arbetsglädjen.


Vi tror att du är initiativrik, tar ansvar och drivs av att lösa problem på ett prestigelöst sätt. Har du frågor vågar du ställa dem, och du arbetar självständigt tills uppgifterna är lösta, samtidigt som du bidrar till att stärka teamet. I den här organisationen litar man på varandra – därför är det viktigt att du tar ansvar för ditt arbete och att du är trygg i din bokföring.


Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer bland annat bestå av:

  • Att hantera leverantörs- och kundflöden
  • Löpande bokföring
  • Att bidra i bokslutsarbetet
  • Att vara delaktig i likviditetsanalyser
  • Ansvar för reseräkningar


Vi söker dig som tycker om att arbeta i ett högt tempo och vill vara med och utveckla rutiner och skapa ordning och reda. Du trivs med att bygga relationer både internt och externt, och du är bekväm med att kommunicera på svenska och engelska.


Här får du vara med och skapa en arbetsplats där nyfikenhet, glädje och specialistkunskap går hand i hand. Bolaget värdesätter rätt inställning och ett stort driv i kombination med gedigen ekonomikompetens. Personlig utveckling är viktigt liksom att alla ska kunna växa och trivas tillsammans.


The Finance Family har fått den äran hitta rätt person till detta fantastiska bolag och har du frågor är du varmt välkommen att kontakta någon av oss!


Maria Dahl, 072-039 38 40, maria.dahl@financefamily.se

Jeanette Elm, 070-353 55 73, jeanette.elm@financefamily.se

companyDescription: Modern banking. Real-time control. Juni provides business banking, spend management, accounting and financing, on one powerful platform and app.
companySlogan: Modern business banking. Real-time control.
descriptionHtml:
Juni who? 👋
At Juni, we’re busy building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.
How & Why We Do It 🚀
We give our people the same thing we’re building for our customers: freedom. Freedom to be 100% yourself. Freedom to explore your potential and what’s more – make the most of it. We truly believe we’re making the world a better place for online businesses, and we want you to be a part of that mission.
Summary 🤸‍♀️
The Finance team is an integral part of Juni. We enable Juni to release new products, assess financial implications and ensure swift, compliant internal and external reporting. Juni is built for Finance teams which is why our internal finance processes are the backbone of the business.
We are now looking for a financial superstar to join our growing Finance team. If you are looking for a new adventure as an accountant, and to make a difference in a business with constant changes and great growth potential, then this is the role for you. We have many exciting & challenging projects which we would like you to be involved in, including the development of our features and new products. As Juni is a product for finance teams, The below responsibilities are only a small sample of what you will be working on.
Responsibilities 👏
In this role, you’ll:
  • Consolidate GL accounts for EU & UK and its subsidiaries.
  • Coordinate transfer pricing agreements.
  • Ensure accurate and timely closing of the books for monthly and annual reporting.
  • Assist in managing the accounting policy, updating as required and ensuring regulatory financial reporting requirements are adhered to in accordance with Juni’s EMI-license.
  • Support with annual statutory as well as interim audits.
  • Assisting in maintaining a hard close month end structure under set accounting policies.
  • Manage the IFRS9 reporting for the loan book and update the ECL model on a monthly basis. Translate and analyse the results to the business in a clear, concise way to identify operational drivers for change working closely with the Credit Team.
  • Deliver process improvements to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of the monthly, quarterly and annual reporting process; both via automation and control.
  • Coaching junior colleagues within the accounting team.
Tooling ⚙️
  • Slack
  • G-suite
  • NetSuite
  • Excel
Qualifications 👑
What we need to see
  • University degree in Finance, Business or Economics, preferably with a major in accounting.
  • Minimum 5 years experience in similar roles.
  • Strong accounting knowledge, with experience in consolidated financial reporting.
  • Ability to take initiative and work with minimal supervision in a rapidly changing environment, influencing the business departments and driving improvements in the end-to-end financial reporting process.
  • A problem solving, hands on approach, not afraid to challenge the status quo and push for changes.
  • Eager to jump in where support is needed
  • Keen to be involved in different areas of the business and work in projects outside your comfort zone.
What we’d love to see
  • Experience from a regulated environment.
  • Experience in automating procedures.
  • Highly numerate with advanced excel skills.
  • Experience with ERP system NetSuite.
Your people 👥
Our team is as ambitious as our amazing customers. We aim high and we move with speed to make our vision a reality. We care deeply about building a better future for our customers and each other. Here, you can work with people at the top of their game and who didn’t get there by playing games. You can help us create a whole new category in financial services.
Your Benefits 😍
We’re freedom-first. Transparent. Caring. Empowering. So our benefits are too.
  • We work hybrid. We'll see you in one of our offices in Stockholm or Gothenburg at least two days per week.
  • Swap 2D for 3D. Meet all Junis IRL at the company onsite each year.
  • Diversity is at our core. We're part Swedish. Part Canadian. Part French. Part Indian. Part Italian. Part British. Part Portuguese. You get the idea.
  • Great players can stay great players. Progress your career whether you choose to manage people or not.
  • Stock options. We can’t promise you’ll make a fortune. But we’ll give it our very best shot.
  • Vacation days. 30 days. Awesome.
  • Private Health insurance. You know. Just in case
  • Beautiful offices in central Gothenburg and Stockholm, front row sea view!
At Juni, we’re building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.
