Freedom to be 100% yourself. Freedom to explore your potential and what’s more – make the most of it. We truly believe we’re making the world a better place for online businesses, and we want you to be a part of that mission.Summary 🤸‍♀️The Finance team is an integral part of Juni. We enable Juni to release new products, assess financial implications and ensure swift, compliant internal and external reporting. Juni is built for Finance teams which is why our internal finance processes are the backbone of the business.We are now looking for a financial superstar to join our growing Finance team. If you are looking for a new adventure as an accountant, and to make a difference in a business with constant changes and great growth potential, then this is the role for you. We have many exciting & challenging projects which we would like you to be involved in, including the development of our features and new products. As Juni is a product for finance teams, The below responsibilities are only a small sample of what you will be working on.Responsibilities 👏In this role, you’ll:Consolidate GL accounts for EU & UK and its subsidiaries.Coordinate transfer pricing agreements.Ensure accurate and timely closing of the books for monthly and annual reporting.Assist in managing the accounting policy, updating as required and ensuring regulatory financial reporting requirements are adhered to in accordance with Juni’s EMI-license.Support with annual statutory as well as interim audits.Assisting in maintaining a hard close month end structure under set accounting policies.Manage the IFRS9 reporting for the loan book and update the ECL model on a monthly basis. Translate and analyse the results to the business in a clear, concise way to identify operational drivers for change working closely with the Credit Team.Deliver process improvements to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of the monthly, quarterly and annual reporting process; both via automation and control.Coaching junior colleagues within the accounting team.Tooling ⚙️SlackG-suiteNetSuiteExcelQualifications 👑What we need to seeUniversity degree in Finance, Business or Economics, preferably with a major in accounting.Minimum 5 years experience in similar roles.Strong accounting knowledge, with experience in consolidated financial reporting.Ability to take initiative and work with minimal supervision in a rapidly changing environment, influencing the business departments and driving improvements in the end-to-end financial reporting process.A problem solving, hands on approach, not afraid to challenge the status quo and push for changes.Eager to jump in where support is neededKeen to be involved in different areas of the business and work in projects outside your comfort zone.What we’d love to seeExperience from a regulated environment.Experience in automating procedures.Highly numerate with advanced excel skills.Experience with ERP system NetSuite.Your people 👥Our team is as ambitious as our amazing customers. We aim high and we move with speed to make our vision a reality. We care deeply about building a better future for our customers and each other. Here, you can work with people at the top of their game and who didn’t get there by playing games. You can help us create a whole new category in financial services.Your Benefits 😍We’re freedom-first. Transparent. Caring. Empowering. So our benefits are too.We work hybrid. We'll see you in one of our offices in Stockholm or Gothenburg at least two days per week.Swap 2D for 3D. Meet all Junis IRL at the company onsite each year.Diversity is at our core. We're part Swedish. Part Canadian. Part French. Part Indian. Part Italian. Part British. Part Portuguese. You get the idea.Great players can stay great players. Progress your career whether you choose to manage people or not.Stock options. We can’t promise you’ll make a fortune. But we’ll give it our very best shot.Vacation days. 30 days. Awesome.Private Health insurance. You know. Just in caseBeautiful offices in central Gothenburg and Stockholm, front row sea view!At Juni, we’re building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.
title: Senior Accountant

companyDescription:
Hear now. And always 

As the global leader in implantable hearing solutions, at Cochlear (ASX: COH) we are committed to our mission to help people hear and be heard. Our story started more than four decades ago when Professor Graeme Clark pioneered the world's first multi-channel cochlear implant and created an entirely new treatment for hearing loss.  

Since our formation in 1981, we continue Professor Clark’s work to help people with moderate to profound hearing loss experience a life full of hearing. 

We have provided more than 650,000 implantable devices. Each recipient helps form a global community of millions, through families, friends, colleagues, teachers and more. And they’re not just connected to their own community — each shares a link with each other and to Professor Clark’s childhood desire to help people hear. 

We aim to give people the best lifelong hearing experience and access to innovative future technologies. We understand the privilege of connecting people to a life lived with hearing. And we listen, respond and move with the times – to continue to bring hearing within reach of all those who need it.
 
That's how we live our mission every day. Our global workforce of more than 4,000 people shares a collective determination to give more people the opportunity to enjoy a life of hearing.

Cochlear’s global headquarters are on the campus of Macquarie University in Sydney, Australia with regional headquarters in Asia Pacific, Europe and the Americas. Through our offices in over 180 countries we help people of all ages to hear. 

Social Media Terms of Use http://bit.ly/2qRMEvY

descriptionHtml:
Make a difference with Cochlear.

Cochlear is the global leader in implantable hearing solutions. In this role, you'll manage day‑to‑day accounting processes across our Nordic entities (Sweden, Finland, Norway, and Denmark), ensuring accurate reporting, strong financial control, and great service to our internal and external stakeholders. You'll work closely with a Senior Accountant while partnering across Order‑to‑Cash and Purchase‑to‑Pay processes to drive performance and continuous improvement.



What you'll do
Accounts Receivable (AR)

- Achieve debt reduction and cash collection targets across assigned countries

- Accurately and timely process incoming customer payments and maintain customer master data

- Produce AR reports and insights for stakeholders (e.g., DSO, aging, cash collections)

- Ensure OTC processes comply with company policies and relevant legislation

- Lead/participate in continuous improvement initiatives to enhance AR efficiency

Accounts Payable (AP)

- Process supplier invoices and payments in line with agreed terms

- Maintain supplier master data and controls

- Ensure P2P processes align with policies and legal requirements

- Identify and implement AP efficiency improvements

Accounting & Financial Reporting

- Prepare monthly Income Statement and Balance Sheet in line with local GAAP and Cochlear group standards (IFRS)

- Ensure accurate accruals, prepayments, and restatements transactions

- Calculate, review, and post transfer pricing and tax accruals

- Prepare annual financial statements, VAT returns, and other statutory filings for Nordic entities

Financial Control & Audit

- Perform monthly account reconciliations and maintain effective financial controls

- Ensure robust controls over payables, expense processing, capex, and intercompany

- Support internal/external audits, including year‑end

Business Partnering

- Provide reporting and analytical support that helps stakeholders make better decisions


About you
- 3+ years' experience in accounting/finance within a multinational environment

- Knowledge of local GAAP (Nordics) and IFRS

- Hands‑on experience with AR and AP processes

- Proficient in ERP (e.g., SAP or Oracle) and very strong Excel skills

- Degree in Accounting/Finance

- Fluent in English (spoken and written); fluency in a Nordic language is an advantage

- Proactive, structured, collaborative, with a continuous improvement mindset and a natural service orientation



What we offer
- Purpose-driven work that changes lives

- Learning and development with real career pathways

- Inclusive, collaborative culture

- Hybrid work with international exposure

- Competitive benefits package

How to apply: Submit your CV (and brief cover note) via [Company Careers Page/ATS link].

Inclusion: We welcome applications from all qualified candidates and are committed to an inclusive workplace.

descriptionHtml:
Nyexaminerad Revisorsassistent – Stockholm

Kickstarta din karriär hos Go Revision & Consulting

Är du nyutexaminerad och redo att växa inom revision och rådgivning? Hos oss får du tidigt ansvar, nära stöd av erfarna kollegor och möjlighet att arbeta med allt från ägarledda bolag till noterade kunder – i en kultur där det är högt i tak och nära till beslut.

Om rollen

Som Revisorsassistent blir du en viktig del av våra uppdrag och kommer att:

• Medverka i hela revisionsprocessen – planering, riskbedömning, genomförande och rapportering.

• Göra analyser, granskningsåtgärder och dokumentation i våra revisionsverktyg.

• Ha kundkontakt tillsammans med ansvarig revisor och bidra i löpande rådgivning.

• Arbeta både självständigt och i team i ett varierat uppdragsflöde.

Vem är du?

• Har examen inom ekonomi, gärna inriktning redovisning/revision.

• Uttrycker dig väl på svenska och engelska, i tal och skrift.

• Är nyfiken, driven och noggrann med ett professionellt förhållningssätt.

• Trivs i team och tar ansvar när du arbetar självständigt.


Meriterande: praktik/extraarbete inom revision eller redovisning under studietiden.

Därför trivs du hos oss

• Brett kundspann & omväxling: från ägarledda till börsnoterade bolag.

• Lärande varje dag: mentorskap, intern kunskapsdelning och tydlig utvecklingsplan mot auktorisation.

• Nära kultur: korta beslutsvägar, prestigelös stämning och stort kollegialt stöd.

• Starkt nätverk: vi är en del av LR Revision & Redovisning Sverige med gemensamma resurser och kompetensutbyte.

• Balans & förmåner: marknadsmässig lön, friskvård och centralt kontor.

Praktiskt

• Plats: Holländargatan, Stockholm

• Omfattning: Heltid

• Start: Under hösten (enligt överenskommelse)

Ansökan

Skicka CV och personligt brev till go-rekrytering@lr-revision.se. Urval sker löpande – vänta inte med din ansökan.

companyDescription:
Assessio exists because humans cannot make completely objective decisions – in recruitment processes, we can be our own worst enemy. Today, your chances of getting a job are higher if you are European, tall, look healthy and do not have children. We want to change that.

Assessio is a SaaS company specialising in strategic HR and developing tests and tools in occupational psychology. Our tools can help organisations predict who has the best potential to succeed in a role, find future leaders within the organisation, identify toxic behaviours in leadership, and match candidates to the organisational culture through values.

We provide our customers with data-driven insights about both current and potential employees. By making decisions based on reliable data rather than gut feeling, we can discover the potential that exists and ensure that no talent goes to waste.

To teach organisations how to make better decisions based on data, our consultants hold both customer-specific and open training courses in our tools. Among other things, the focus is on our digital platform, where we have gathered all our tools for matching, selection, development and business-driven HR. The platform makes it easy to combine multiple tools, create ranking lists in screening processes, compare candidates with each other or compare a candidate with a group.
descriptionHtml:
At Assessio, we have a bold ambition: to become the leading HR Tech provider in Europe, eliminating talent waste by helping people thrive in roles where they can perform at their best.

Through our transformation into a scalable, AI-powered people data platform, we deliver solutions that span recruitment and development. By combining over 70 years of scientific heritage with a modern, intuitive product experience, Assessio serves global enterprises and public organizations with high HR tech maturity. Our mission? To lead the transformation toward a future with Zero Talent Waste by empowering individuals to unleash their full potential in roles aligned with their strengths.

With offices in Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Germany, Estonia, France, Romaina and the Netherlands- and more on the way- we’re building a strong international presence. Join us in shaping the future of talent management in Europe. We are 200+ dedicated co-workers who share our vision of Zero Talent Waste.

About The Role

We are now looking for an experienced Group Chief Accountant to join our growing Finance team at HQ in Stockholm. This is a high-impact role with end-to-end responsibility for group consolidation, and statutory reporting across our international SaaS group. As we continue our rapid expansion- including the integration of our newly acquired French SaaS business. This role will take ownership of our accounting and financial reporting across the group. This is a key role in ensuring financial integrity, accurate reporting, and effective financial operations across multiple entities in a growing international business.

Main Responsibilities

  • Full responsibility for accounting of multiple Group holding companies
  • Monthly consolidation of Group companies in accordance with applicable accounting standards (K3 or IFRS, as relevant)
  • Lead and develop the Swedish accounting team, ensuring timely and accurate delivery of monthly closes and reporting
  • Oversee the Nordic treasury function, including cash flow management, internal funding, and liquidity planning
  • Serve as primary contact for auditors and banks, coordinating audit processes and maintaining banking relationships
  • Ensure compliance with local and group accounting policies, tax regulations, and statutory reporting requirements
  • Drive process improvements and support automation and efficiency across accounting functions
  • Support preparation of annual reports and Board materials
  • Work closely with Group CFO and other finance leaders on group-wide initiatives and projects

Who are you?

At Assessio, we believe in potential. While experience matters, your drive, curiosity, and collaborative mindset are what truly set you apart.

You are a structured, energetic, and pragmatic team player- someone who thrives in a dynamic international setting. You're hands-on when needed, but also capable of leading and inspiring remote teams. You enjoy simplifying complexity, taking ownership of strategic processes, and are comfortable managing cross-border collaboration.

We believe you’ll thrive in this role if you:

  • Hold a university degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • Have 5–8 years of experience in international finance roles, preferably in SaaS or audit (Big 4 experience a plus).
  • Possess strong technical accounting knowledge (IFRS/local GAAPs).
  • Have hands-on experience with ERP systems — Visma experience is highly valued.
  • Are confident leading consolidation, audits, and reporting in an international environment.
  • Excel at stakeholder communication — from CFOs to local finance staff to auditors.
  • Communicate fluently in English, both spoken and written.

Nice to have:

  • A background in scaling financial operations during periods of high growth.
  • Familiarity with Swedish financial and regulatory context is a plus.

A fair and data-driven recruitment process

We practice what we preach. That’s why you will be asked to complete three short assessments (personality, cognitive ability, and motivation) - a process that takes around 50 minutes and ensures a fair, unbiased selection based on potential rather than background alone.

You can read more about our hiring approach on our Career Site.

We look forward to getting in touch!

Questions?

Please contact CFO Bryan Bailey at Bryan.Bailey@assessio.se

companyDescription: Prevent cyber breach and minimize impact
companySlogan: Prevent cyber breach and minimize impact
descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? Are you a seasoned Senior Accountant looking to take your career to the next level? Do you thrive in a dynamic environment where your expertise can make a real impact? If so, we have the perfect opportunity for you!

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 300 employees across several entities such as Sweden, Germany, Denmark, Finland & the US, and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Senior Accountant who wants to make a difference in our finance team.

Group Functions

Truesec consists of several domains who act as partners towards our customers within various different areas of cyber security. The group function supports with e.g. Marketing, People & Culture, Internal IT and Finance and do so both locally and globally. All functions act and collaborate with the organization with a business objective in mind as the tasks are crucial for Truesec to continue to prevent cyber breach and minimize impact.

A key role for a successful Finance Team

We are looking for an enthusiastic, proactive and competent senior accountant to our finance team. Truesec is a fast-growing company with international ambitions so the role has plenty of opportunities for growth. You will assist with the development of our systems and processes, be a key player in the monthly financial close and reporting to ensure operational success.

You will gain increasing experience as the company grows utilizing technology to develop processes for a lean mean finance function. You will be responsible for ensuring accurate and timely financial reporting, maintaining compliance with regulations and providing strategic financial guidance to senior management.

If you have an accounting background, love a challenges and want to be part of a fast-growing business and amazing team, we’d like to meet you.

In this position you report directly to Finance Manager and the role is based at the Office in central Stockholm.

What You Will Do

  • Prepare and analyze financial statements (K3) including group consolidation and cash flow statements.
  • Prepare monthly, quarterly, and year-end closing processes, ensuring accuracy and completeness of financial data
  • Perform full balance sheet reconciliations and Inter-company re-invoicing and reconciliations between entities
  • Ensure accurate Tax and VAT reporting
  • Manage and drive compliance and statutory reporting and the external audit process
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams and accounting firms across our entities (Finland, Denmark, Germany, USA)
  • Leading initiatives to ensure process improvement, system development and always strive to improve
  • Any other ad hoc tasks as and when required

Your Potential Background

You have a strong foundation in finance, with experience in monthly closing and tax. You’re detail-oriented, organized and capable of managing multiple tasks simultaneously. Your proactive approach allows you to identify areas for improvement while maintaining compliance with financial standards. You are a team player and have a service-minded attitude which makes you a natural collaborator who thrives in a team-oriented environment.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (CPA or CMA preferred)
  • 5+ years of experience in accounting and ownership of month-end closing.
  • Strong knowledge of local GAAP and financial reporting standards
  • Experience with ERP systems (e.g., Visma Net, Severa) and Excel proficiency
  • Strong analytical, organizational and communication skills

Other Requirements:

  • Fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken
  • Willingness to travel occasionally, as required

Due to the sensitivity of our work, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Why Join Us? As a Senior Accountant you will play a pivotal role in driving financial excellence and ensuring regulatory compliance in one of our key markets. You’ll have the opportunity to work in a dynamic environment, contribute to strategic initiatives, and be part of a global team dedicated to achieving organizational success.

Apply now to become a cornerstone of our financial operations and help shape the future of our organization!

Strong knowledge of local GAAP and financial reporting standards Experience with ERP systems (e.g., Visma Net, Severa) and Excel proficiency Strong analytical, organizational and communication skills Other Requirements: Fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken Willingness to travel occasionally, as required Due to the sensitivity of our work, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit. Why Join Us? As a Senior Accountant you will play a pivotal role in driving financial excellence and ensuring regulatory compliance in one of our key markets. You’ll have the opportunity to work in a dynamic environment, contribute to strategic initiatives, and be part of a global team dedicated to achieving organizational success.Apply now to become a cornerstone of our financial operations and help shape the future of our organization!
title: Senior Accountant

companyDescription:
Nestled in Sweden's life sciences heartland, NorthX Biologics has been at the forefront of biologics manufacturing and process development since 1992. Our rich heritage in Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) is not just a testament to our enduring commitment to quality but also to the trust that the biotech community has placed in us for more than three decades.
Our expertise within bioprocessing spans a variety of advanced modalities, including recombinant proteins, viral vaccines and cell therapy, supporting projects from concept through to commercial manufacturing offering a full spectrum of GMP-compliant services. It is our relentless pursuit of excellence and innovation that led to our recognition in 2021 as a national innovation hub for advanced therapeutics and vaccines by the Swedish Government, a milestone that underscores our role in shaping the future of healthcare.
We are more than a CDMO; we are pioneers and partners in the truest sense. At NorthX Biologics, we believe in embarking on a collaborative journey with our clients, navigating the complexities of biologics and growing along the way. Let's journey together to turn visionary science into life-changing treatments.

We specialise in:
Process development
Analytical development
Recombinant proteins
Virus & Vectors
Cell Therapy
Extracellular Vesicles
Aseptic fill & finish
Microbial & mammalian production
QC, QA, QP
companySlogan: Beyond CDMO
descriptionHtml:
NorthX Biologics är en kontraktsutvecklings- och tillverkningsorganisation (CDMO) som erbjuder tjänster och stöd inom utveckling och tillverkning av biologiska läkemedel som används för vacciner, genterapi och andra avancerade tillämpningar. Vi har en lång erfarenhet av läkemedelstillverkning och har tillverkat biologiska läkemedel enligt GMP sedan 1992.

Vi söker nu en Accounting Manager som värdesätter ordning och smarta lösningar. Du kommer att vara en viktig del i att skapa effektiva och hållbara ekonomiprocesser.


Dina arbetsuppgifter i huvudsak

I rollen som Accounting Manager kommer du bland annat att:

  • Ansvara för månads- och årsbokslut
  • Ta fram rapporter och underlag till ledning
  • Hantera intäktsredovisning, anläggningsregister och lagerredovisning
  • Säkerställa kvalitet och korrekthet i ekonomiska rapporter
  • Delta i förbättringsarbete kring rutiner och processer


Vem är du?

Vi söker dig som trivs i en bred roll där du får kombinera operativt redovisningsarbete med ansvar för struktur, förbättringar och rapportering. Du är trygg i hela redovisningsprocessen och gillar att arbeta nära verksamheten samt har ett intresse för produktion och verksamhet

Som person är du självgående, analytisk, samarbetsinriktad och har en god kommunikativ förmåga. Du trivs i en mindre organisation där du får vara med och påverka – och där samspel och arbetsglädje är viktiga delar av vardagen.


Kvalifikationer

Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av kvalificerad redovisning och en eftergymnasial utbildning inom redovisning och ekonomi. Du har god vana av att arbeta i ERP-system och i Microsoft Office och du behärskar engelska väl. Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av att arbeta i en internationell organisation.


I denna rekryteringsprocess samarbetar NorthX Biologics med Poolia. Vid frågor om tjänsten eller processen är du varmt välkommen att höra av dig till Poolias rekryteringskonsult Pär Jonsson på par.jonsson@poolia.se, +46 768 694 756.

companyDescription:
Zurich Insurance Group (Zurich) is a leading global multi-line insurer founded more than 150 years ago, which has grown into a business serving more than 75 million customers in more than 200 countries and territories, while delivering industry-leading total shareholder returns. Our customers include individuals, small businesses, and mid-sized and large companies, as well as multinational corporations. The Group is headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, where it was founded in 1872. 

ONE TEAM, ONE PURPOSE

We are Zurich, one global company, with one mission, one ambition, one set of shared values and a clear commitment to our stakeholders: our customers, our people, our shareholders, and the communities in which we live and work.

You can find our community guidelines on: https://www.zurich.com/services/social-media
descriptionHtml:
Accountant to Zurich Nordic

If you are a customer centric, agile and engaging person that would like to join an innovative Finance team, then we are happy to receive your application!

Accountant in Stockholm

Zurich Nordic offers local and global insurance programs both standardized as well as individually tailored. Our customers are mainly international and large industrial companies. We are now looking for a positive person to join our Finance team as an Accountant.

As an Accountant, you will have a variety of smaller and bigger tasks, e.g. incoming and outgoing payments for one of our Nordic countries. Support timely monthly, quarterly, and year-end financial close, including journal entries, balance sheet account reconciliations, accruals, and overall maintenance of all operational accounts. You can also be expected to support the broader accounts payable process including intercompany invoices, and reinsurance process.

You will also be exposed to the broader Zurich network when handling international taxes and payments with other offices.

Qualifications:

  • Academic degree in Economics or equivalent
  • 1-3 years of experience within accounting
  • Relevant insurance related work experience is a plus
  • Verbal and written communication skills in Swedish and English, equivalent to C2 level, are required

Skills and competencies:

  • Service minded, communicative and flexible
  • High motivation, drive and energy
  • Responsible and loyal, without prestige
  • Strong driving force to deliver on deadline
  • Prior experience with SAP and FSCD considered beneficial
  • Strong Excel skills

As a person, you are a team player and accurate in your work. You have a problem-solving mind-set and are eager to resolve challenging tasks. You like working independently but with good abilities to co-operate with colleagues within Nordic and Internationally.

Please refer your questions to Jonas Ersblad, CFO Zurich Nordic, jonas.ersblad@zurich.com or Carl-Arvid Nilsson, Nordic Talent Acquisition Specialist, carlarvid.nilsson@zurich.com

You are welcome to submit your application online on 28 September 2025, at latest. The selection process will run continuously, and we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.

Who We Are

Zurich Nordic is the local arm of Zurich Insurance Group, one of the world’s leading insurance companies with over 63 000 employees. Zurich has been operating in the Nordic market for 150 years with offices in Stockholm, Oslo, Copenhagen and Helsinki, where we also serve the Baltic market.

Zurich Nordic is a leading provider of commercial insurance solutions, as well as a wide range of risk management products and services, for businesses in the large corporate and middle market segment. We support companies with local and global presence, in more than 200 countries and territories.

Reflecting its purpose to ‘create a brighter future together,’ Zurich aspires to be one of the most responsible and impactful businesses in the world. Its core values Optimism, Caring, Reliability, Togetherness, Forward-Thinking and Results Orientation guides its employees and informs every action they take. With Zurich, you can expect to work on challenges that will help you grow and to collaborate with a diverse and inclusive global team.

descriptionHtml:
Zurich is one of the most specialized insurance companies within non-life insurance for local and international business in the Nordic market. We are now looking for a customer centric and agile person, that would like to join an innovative Finance team, then we are happy to receive your application!

Financial Controller

You will be part of the Nordic Finance team, which operates collaboratively. You will report directly to the Head of Planning & Performance Management (PPM). As a Financial Controller you split your time between analyzing data and being a finance business partner to our stakeholders; helping them understand how their portfolio’s are performing. You will be responsible for one of our countries and several products, allowing you to gradually learn about our Nordic portfolio. PPM is a small team with a broad range of responsibilities, therefore your ability to adapt, take ownership of your work, and work seamlessly with your colleagues will be crucial to our collective success.

Job description:

  • Operationally support our closing process where we generate our actual results and forecast how the year will end. Afterwards you make sure your stakeholders understand these numbers so they can drive business decisions based on it.
  • Contribute to the development and execution of our business strategy by generating actionable insights from analysing our financials.
  • Design, create, and improve financial models and processes.
  • Working together with the business to drive our annual financial planning cycle.
  • Provide regular support as a subject matter expert (on Premium, Expenses, or Claims) to projects and internal initiatives.
  • Take an active role in ensuring accuracy of numbers through our control process ICFR (Internal Control over Financial Reporting)

Qualifications:

  • An academic degree, preferably a Master of Science in Economics or Finance
  • Preferably you have 2-3 years of work experience in controlling in the insurance industry specifically. However, we are also open to recent graduates in a more junior role. In this case we would prefer you have some relevant experience from an internship or side-job.
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills in English, equivalent to C2 level, are required

Skills and competencies:

  • High motivation, looking to learn and grow.
  • Attention to details
  • A Team-player, who can also work independently
  • Takes responsibility and delivers on deadlines
  • Service minded, flexible, and communicative
  • Analytical and problem solving skills
  • Interest in understanding the story behind the numbers
  • Advanced Excel proficiency
  • PowerBI experience is plus.

What we can offer you:

  • Competitive compensation package
  • Benefit package
  • Hybrid working
  • Occupational pension
  • Wellbeing initiatives
  • Comprehensive insurance package
  • Global Career development
  • A diverse and inclusive workplace
  • Great location in the central part of the city

Please refer your questions to Lennart van Loo, Nordic Head of Planning & Performance Management, lennart.vanloo@zurich.com or Carl-Arvid Nilsson, Nordic Talent Acquisition Specialist, carlarvid.nilsson@zurich.com

You are welcome to submit your application until 28 September 2025. Please note, the selection process will run continuously, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

We look forward to receiving your application!

Who We Are

Zurich is one of the world’s leading insurance groups, and one of the few to operate on a global basis. Our mission is to help our customers understand and protect themselves from risk. With about 63,000 employees serving customers in more than 215 countries, we aspire to become the best global insurer as measured by our shareholders, customers and employees. We help individuals, small and medium sized companies and global corporations around the world understand and protect themselves from risk by offering a wide range of insurance products, solutions and advisory services.

Our people are the heart & soul of Zurich. Together we are committed to delivering on our purpose - Let’s Create a Brighter Future Together!

Our ambition is to become one of the most responsible and impactful businesses in the world. To get there we hire and develop the best talent available. With Zurich, you can expect to work on challenges that will help you grow and to collaborate with a diverse and inclusive global team.

descriptionHtml:
Group International Financial Controller- Global Production Agency -Stockholm/Hybrid

The Role:
As Group International Financial Controller, you’ll oversee the agency’s financial operations in Sweden and the USA, reporting directly into the 2 Founders as well as the Group CFO. You’ll be a key player in shaping financial strategy, improving reporting processes, ensuring compliance, and driving commercial performance. You’ll work closely with cross-functional teams, including Client Services, Commercial, and Operations, supporting decision-making and helping the business scale effectively.

Key Responsibilities:
  • Manage the consolidation of financial results from international subsidiaries
  • Manage the audit process for the companies under your portfolio.
  • Lead accurate and timely monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reporting.
  • Manage budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis processes.
  • Supervise and develop the finance team across management accounting, AP, and AR.
  • Ensure compliance with group policies and external statutory requirements.
  • Manage external accountants statutory reporting.
  • Partner with client teams to monitor profitability, improve margins, and optimise project delivery. Including billing and job costing.
  • Maintain strong internal controls and drive process improvements across finance functions.
  • Manage cash flow, working capital, and balance sheet reporting.
  • Deliver insightful financial analysis to support senior leadership.
  • Collaborate on system and process upgrades to improve automation and efficiency.
  • Act as a point of contact for external auditors, tax authorities, and group finance.
 
Requirements:
  • ACA, ACCA, or CIMA qualified, with experience reporting under IFRS standards.
  • Fluent in English and Swedish
  • Experience within a Media, Creative, Production or Marketing agency preferred.
  • Exposure to audits, either as an auditor or in an audited business.
  • Strong understanding of project-based accounting and client profitability.
  • Confident communicator, able to build trust with senior stakeholders.
  • Advanced Excel skills and experience with finance/ERP systems (e.g., Oracle, Sage)
  • Analytical, commercially aware, and highly organised, with a strong eye for detail.
  • Collaborative team player, comfortable in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.
  • Experience incorporating AI into finance processes is a plus, but not required.
 
 
Location: Stockholm/Sweden – Hybrid
Salary: €90K- €100K  Per Annum + Benefits
Reference: AW11776
 
Please note: This role is based in Stockholm. Candidates should either be currently located in Stockholm or willing to relocate from the UK, with the legal right to live and work in Sweden.

DNA is committed to promoting a diverse and inclusive community and welcomes applications from candidates of all backgrounds.
Although we try to respond to all applications, unfortunately, due to the high number we receive, we are unable to respond to all of those who are unsuccessful. 


  • We offer up to £250 in vouchers for any referrals who pass their probationary period, so if you know anyone who might be interested please forward the link to the job advert to them. 

  • Be the first to know about our latest roles by following us on LinkedIn, Twitter and Facebook.



DNA Recruit deliver dynamic recruitment, search and talent solutions and provide insight, knowledge, and credible introductions necessary for client-side and agency roles. Areas we specialise in New Business, Account and Project Management, Strategy and Planning, Events, Experiential, Creative and User Experience Design, Technology, Product, Marketing and Data roles in permanent, contract and freelance recruitment, search and selection, talent advisory and consulting across the UK and globally.

companyDescription:
Randstad är ett av världens största HR-serviceföretag med över 600 000 anställda i fler än 39 länder. Vi erbjuder lösningar inom bemanning, rekrytering och omställning . För dig som söker jobb erbjuder vi lediga tjänster inom i princip alla branscher och yrkesområden med spännande karriärmöjligheter.

2016 förvärvade Randstad det nordiska bemanningsföretaget Proffice Group. Samma höst bytte Proffice namn till Randstad. 

Randstad-koncernen har sitt säte i Amsterdam, Holland. Det var här Randstad grundades 1960 och i princip uppfann den flexibla arbetsmarknaden i Holland. Sedan dess har mycket hänt. Storleken gör Randstad till en stabil partner. Men det är den nära, personliga kontakten med våra konsulter och kunder på lokal nivå som är nyckeln till vår framgång. 

Randstad Sverige är auktoriserat av Bemanningsföretagen. Det innebär att vi uppfyller högt ställda krav som bl a omfattar etiska regler, kollektivavtal, jämställdhetsplan, ansvarsförsäkring och allmänna leveransvillkor. Bemanningsföretagen ingår i Almega och är medlem i Svenskt Näringsliv.
companySlogan: Vi vill påverka vårt samhälle positivt, framför allt när det handlar om mångfald, jämställdhet och ett tryggt arbetsliv.
descriptionHtml:
Arbetsbeskrivning

Är du en ambitiös och framåtriktad Financial Controller som brinner för redovisning och ekonomisk kontroll? Har du dessutom ett stort intresse för att driva projekt och lösa diverse ekonomirelaterade ad hoc-frågor? Då kan det här vara nästa steg i din karriär!

Vi söker en erfaren och engagerad Financial Controller för ett konsultuppdrag på 14 månader med start i november. Uppdraget är en föräldraledighetsvikarie på heltid hos vår kund, ett dynamiskt företag i norra Stockholm.

Ansvarsområde

Som Financial Controller kommer du att ha en central roll i kundens ekonomiteam. Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter inkluderar:

  • Löpande redovisning
  • Skatteberäkning och -rapportering
  • Månadsrapportering till koncernen (IFRS)
  • Internkontroll och kvalitetssäkring
  • Kontoavstämningar
  • Stötta organisationen i redovisningsrelaterade frågor
  • Driva och delta i olika typer av projekt och förbättringsinitiativ
  • Ansvara för revisioner och agera kontaktperson för externa revisorer

Kvalifikationer

Vi söker dig som är en engagerad och erfaren financial controller med en stark analytisk förmåga. Du är duktig på att bryta ner komplexa problem och har ett genuint intresse av att driva förändring och utveckla processer.

För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att du har:

  • Relevant akademisk examen inom ekonomi/finans/revision och flera års erfarenhet av kvalificerade redovisningsuppgifter, inklusive bokslutsarbete. Det är meriterande om du även har arbetat på en revisionsbyrå.
  • Gedigen erfarenhet av att arbeta med svenska redovisningsstandarder och principer.
  • Ett ifrågasättande mindset och en stark vilja att driva förbättringar.
  • Förmågan att arbeta både självständigt och i grupp samt god kommunikationsförmåga för att bygga relationer och nätverk.
  • Ett starkt fokus på kvalitet, är nyfiken och positiv samt hjälpsam och prestigelös.
  • Mycket goda kunskaper i Excel samt god systemvana. Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av Visma Business eller SAP Group Reporting samt IFRS.
  • Flytande kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.

descriptionHtml:
About the Company


Our client is a leading Payroll Consultancy. Their product is a cloud-based payroll calculation engine that continuously recalculates payroll, delivering updated results instantly in response to changes. Featuring SOC1 compliance controls and a comprehensive dashboard, it eliminates high pressure processing, minimizes processing downtime and maximizes data input time. Built-in defensive queries and anomaly detection ensure increased payroll accuracy by proactively identifying potential payroll infringement. The intelligent solution reimagines the payroll process and is designed with the needs of today and the reality of tomorrow in mind.


About the Role


A Functional Consultant is needed on a 6 month project to implement the product. You will provide functional consultancy on implementations in Sweden. You'll be involved in the whole project lifecycle, including client liaison, confirming requirements & guidance aligned with best practices, completing configuration documents and questionnaires, providing effective configuration & testing of the solution, and supporting other go-live activities.


Responsibilities


  • Client-facing functional lead supporting kick-off and leading functional requirements discussions.
  • Working with the client to capture payroll configuration information.
  • Identify deviations from product standards.
  • Working closely with the Product team to complete the configuration of the payroll solution.
  • Validate payroll solution & integrations as part of the project testing cycles.
  • Perform data validations in the payroll system.
  • Coordinate defect resolution with relevant teams.
  • Knowledge transfer to ongoing application services for payroll solution maintenance.
  • Input to and updates the project plan.
  • Support data mapping and data validations across all systems and issue resolutions.
  • Support integration issues resolution.
  • Support UAT and parallel testing.
  • Support data validations across all systems and issue resolutions.
  • Responsible for workaround definitions and resolutions.
  • Support and oversee knowledge transfer to Delivery and Operational Payroll teams.



Qualifications


  • Experience in a functional consulting/analytical capacity within a payroll outsourcing or payroll software environment.
  • Experience and strong knowledge of the Swedish payroll and compliance (e.g. tax, legislation etc.).
  • Experience of the Irish payroll implementation project lifecycle.


Required Skills


  • Strong client-facing communication skills.
  • Ability to articulate and document client requirements.
  • Find creative and simple ways to address client issues or risks.
  • Strong stakeholder management skills and an ability to interact with all levels of business.
  • Propose alternative solutions assessing feasibility and costs.
  • Ability to be proactive and problem-solve.
  • Strong knowledge of MS Office tools including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
  • Flexibility to support a global and fast paced environment.
  • Attention to detail.
  • Excellent written and verbal skills.

companyDescription:
Nammo is an international aerospace and defense company headquartered in Norway. With more than 3 600 employees, 27 production sites and a presence in 11 countries, Nammo is today one of the world’s leading providers of specialty ammunition and rocket motors for both military and civilian customers.

Nammo Karlsborg is located next to the historical Karlsborg fortress at the western side of lake Vättern in Sweden. The ammunition production activities started in 1870 in the old Karlsborg fortress. Already in 1887 the factory was moved outside the fortress compound, where they remain to this day. Karlsborg employs around 240 people, and serves as Nammo’s primary hub for small caliber ammunition for use by assault rifles and other light weapons, including armor piercing and non-toxic/lead free ammunition.

Nammo Karlskoga is located in Björkborn, 45 km west of Örebro in Sweden. The industrial history in Björkborn dates back to the late 19th century, when a chemicals factory was established at the site. What is today Nammo Karlskoga was established in the 1970s. The facility today employs about 150 people, and is mainly focused on explosive filling and final assembly of 40 mm, 57 mm and 155 mm ammunition.

Nammo Vingåker is a demilitarization facility close to Vingåker outside Katrineholm, approx. 45 km south-east of Örebro in Sweden. The Vingåker facility has been engaged in demilitarization of ammunition for more than 40 years. The plant was established in 1967 as an assembly and explosives casting plant for the famous Swedish Carl-Gustaf system. Demilitarization operations started in early 1970’s and have from then grown to be the main activity. Today approx. 70 employees have their work at Nammo in Vingåker.
companySlogan: Nammo Sweden is a defence company located in Karlsborg, Karlskoga, Lindesberg and Vingåker and employs 500 people.
descriptionHtml:
Nammo-koncernen har ca 3.500 anställda och finns representerade i 11 länder världen över. Verksamheten omfattar utveckling, produktion och försäljning av högteknologiska försvarsprodukter samt miljövänlig demilitarisering. Nammo Sweden AB har verksamhet på fyra orter i Sverige och har ca 500 anställda. Nammo är ett värderingsstyrt företag och våra värderingar Engagemang, Precision och Omtanke genomsyrar hela vår verksamhet.


Hos Nammo får du chansen att tillhöra ett härligt team och framgångsrikt företag som bidrar till nationers och individers säkerhet i en orolig värld. Vi kan nu erbjuda en samarbetsinriktad team-player en variationsrik och viktig roll i en kraftigt växande verksamhet med korta beslutsvägar och där ingen dag är den andra lik. Hos oss får du ett stort ansvar och möjlighet till både professionell och personlig utveckling.


Om rollen

Avdelningen Finance/IT är en central funktion inom Nammo Sweden AB som arbetar brett över samtliga Nammos enheter i Sverige. Som Finance Controller blir du en nyckelperson i vårt controllerteam som ansvarar för att hålla ihop vår boksluts- och rapporteringsprocess. Du säkerställer att bolaget följer både Nammos koncernövergripande instruktioner och policies för finansiell redovisning och rapportering, samt att nationella och internationella regelverk efterlevs.


Ditt arbete sker nära verksamheten och du har ett tätt samarbete med våra Financial Specialists som förser dig med relevanta underlag. Ditt huvudsakliga ansvar innebär att:

  • Sammanställa, granska och leverera periodbokslut i tid och med kvalitet
  • Rapportera periodiska utfall i gruppens konsolideringsverktyg
  • Analysera och kommunicera periodiska resultat till interna stakeholders
  • Koordinera processen för att upprätta och rapportera budget
  • Rapportera till Skatteverket, Bolagsverket samt andra myndigheter och externa parter
  • Verkställa intern kontroll och assistera i extern revision
  • Bevaka kassaflöden, likviditetsplanering och värdesäkring av valutaflöden
  • Ansvara för processer för huvudbok, anläggningsregister, kund- och leverantörsreskontra
  • Underhålla och utveckla affärssystemets finansiella uppsättning


Rollen Finance Controller arbetar också nära Business Controllers för respektive site. En Business Controller arbetar med lokala frågor som stöd för produkten, affären och operationen. Ditt ansvar gäller de övergripande och gemensamma frågorna inom det svenska bolaget. Det handlar om frågor för den juridiska personen, och för delade strukturer och koordinerade processer mellan de svenska siterna. Här finns också ett nära samarbete med gruppens olika Finance funktioner.


Placeringsort: Karlskoga/Lindesberg alt Karlsborg


Vår kandidat

Vi söker dig som har en högskoleutbildning i företagsekonomi med inriktning redovisning och som har minst fem års erfarenhet av liknande arbete, gärna i tillverkningsindustri. Du har vana av att arbeta i större affärssystem och är en fena på Excel. Dessutom har du kunskap kring skatterätt samt mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska i både tal och skrift.


Som person drivs du av en vilja att utveckla verksamheten. Du kombinerar ambition och driv med ödmjukhet och tålamod. Därutöver är du kommunikativ och duktig på att skapa goda relationer med medarbetare och intressenter. Dessutom är du nyfiken och vetgirig och brinner för att hitta nya smarta och effektiva lösningar.


Kontakt

Vi samarbetar med Consensus i den här rekryteringen. För mer information är du välkommen att kontakta Hanna Malmborg 073-048 68 40 eller Lena Ottaway 070-547 36 00. Vi ser fram emot din ansökan på www.consensus.nu senast 17 september.

companyDescription: Nordic Capital is a leading sector-specialist private equity investor with a resolute commitment to creating stronger, sustainable businesses through operational improvement and transformative growth. Nordic Capital focuses on selected regions and sectors where it has deep experience and a long history. Focus sectors are Healthcare, Technology & Payments, Financial Services, and Service & Industrial Tech. Key regions are Europe and globally for Healthcare and Technology & Payments investments. Since inception in 1989, Nordic Capital has invested c. EUR 26 billion in close to 150 investments. The most recent entities are Nordic Capital XI with EUR 9.0 billion in committed capital and Nordic Capital Evolution II with EUR 2 billion in committed capital, principally provided by international institutional investors such as pension funds. Nordic Capital Advisors have local offices in Sweden, the UK, the US, Germany, Denmark, Finland, Norway, and South Korea. www.nordiccapital.com.
companySlogan: Driving transformative sustainable growth with deep sector knowledge
descriptionHtml:
Nordic Capital is looking for a Senior Accountant to join our high-performing Finance team at the firm’s headquarters in central Stockholm. This is a unique opportunity to take on a broad and impactful role in a dynamic, international finance environment within one of Europe’s leading private equity firms.


About the Role

As a Senior Accountant, you will be responsible for the accounting and financial reporting of several Swedish and international legal entities within the group. You will work closely with experienced colleagues across the Finance function to ensure accurate, timely, and compliant reporting. You will also act as a key point of contact for external stakeholders, including auditors and local accounting partners, and contribute actively to the ongoing development of processes and controls.


Key Responsibilities

  • Lead month-end closings, account reconciliations, and financial reviews of P&L and balance sheets for Swedish entities
  • Prepare VAT filings, tax calculations, annual reports, and statutory declarations
  • Coordinate with international accounting firms to ensure timely and accurate monthly reporting
  • Ensure compliance with group accounting policies and internal controls
  • Serve as the main point of contact for auditors both in Sweden and abroad
  • Oversee intercompany transactions, invoicing, and cost allocations
  • Support year-end reporting and consolidation for selected group entities
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives within accounting processes and team collaboration


Who You Are

We’re seeking a confident and experienced accounting professional who thrives in a collaborative, service-oriented, and high-integrity environment. You take initiative, have a strong analytical mindset, and take pride in delivering high-quality results. You’re comfortable taking ownership and enjoy working with both Swedish and international entities.


Your Background

  • At least 5 years of experience in qualified accounting roles, including financial reporting and tax
  • University degree in accounting, finance, or business administration
  • Experience from a group structure, ideally within financial services or professional services
  • Advanced proficiency in Excel and familiarity with accounting and reporting systems
  • Full professional fluency in both Swedish and English


Why Join Nordic Capital?

At Nordic Capital, you’ll be part of a high-performing, collaborative team where your work makes a real impact. We offer an opportunity to grow in an international, fast-paced environment with strong values, ambitious colleagues, and clear purpose.


How to Apply

Please submit your application, including your CV, no later than September 14, 2025.

In this recruitment, Nordic Capital is collaborating with Fand Rekrytering. For questions, please contact responsible recruiter Mikael Schmidt (mikael.schmidt@fand.se).


About Nordic Capital

Nordic Capital is a leading private equity investor committed to creating stronger, more sustainable businesses through operational improvements and transformative growth. Since its inception in 1989, Nordic Capital has invested over EUR 26 billion in more than 130 companies across Healthcare, Technology & Payments, Services & Industrial Tech and Financial Services. With offices in Sweden, the UK, the US, Germany, Denmark, Finland, Norway, and South Korea, Nordic Capital continues to drive long-term value creation in its portfolio companies.


For more information, visit www.nordiccapital.com.

companyDescription:
We fight friction to move the world forward.

20% of all energy consumed is spent overcoming friction.

At SKF, we’re constantly fighting that friction, to reduce energy waste and make the most of the resources we have. 

Since 1907, we’ve been making some of the world’s most innovative bearings, seals, lubrication systems to reduce friction.

But we sell something bigger. Less friction means more energy saved. 

By seeking out and reducing friction, we’re making industry smarter, more competitive, and more energy efficient. And in collaboration with other forward-thinking companies, actually tackling some of the most pressing challenges of our time. Ultimately, helping to build a more sustainable future where we can all do more with less.
descriptionHtml:
SKF has been around for more than a century and today we are one of the world’s largest global suppliers of bearings and supporting solutions for rotating equipment. Our products can be found literally everywhere in society. This means that we are an important part of the everyday lives of people and companies around the world.


In September of 2024, SKF announced the separation of its Automotive business, with the objective to build two world-leading companies. The role you are applying for will be a part of the new company created from the Automotive business. This means you will have the opportunity to be a part of shaping a new company aimed at meeting the needs of the transforming global automotive market.


Manager Group Accounting & Reporting - Automotive Business


We’re seeking a senior finance professional who will establish our new Group Accounting & Reporting department within the SKF Automotive business. In this high-impact role, you will work closely with the business, contribute to strategic decisions, and work in a challenging, international environment.


As our Manager Group Accounting & Reporting, you’ll take on a unique opportunity to shape a new function and team, responsible for group consolidation, quarterly and annual reporting. You will also be working on the preparation of the requirements and standards of a Nasdaq Stockholm listing. Together with our CFO, your highly skilled team, and the full finance organization, you will develop processes and structures that support the evolving needs of the business.


Key Responsibilities


  • Manage the process of creating group consolidated financial statements and external reports, which encompass quarterly and annual statements as well sustainability reports.
  • Develop and maintain group-wide accounting policies and reporting instructions.
  • Ensure compliance with external accounting standards and prepare for future listing requirements.
  • Empower, develop, and support a lean and skilled team within Group Accounting & Reporting.
  • Manage the parent company’s accounting and reporting.
  • Enhance competence development within the accounting and reporting area, in collaboration with the Finance Academy Lead.
  • Act as an expert and advisor in accounting matters and collaborate closely with auditors, IR, and other key stakeholders.
  • Represent Group Finance in strategic development projects.


Requirements


  • University degree in Business Administration or equivalent.
  • Solid experience in financial reporting and preparation of annual reports for a listed company, in either the role of Group accounting or from Auditing.
  • Deep understanding of accounting standards, regulations, and reporting requirements, including those relevant for listed companies.
  • Keen interest in and understanding of current and future finance processes, concepts, and systems.
  • Experienced leader, with the ability to support, inspire, and empower your team to thrive.
  • Fluent in English and in Swedish (verbal & written)
  • If you have experience in sustainability reporting, being part of preparing a company for public listing, and/or working at a manufacturing company, it’s a big plus.


You will enjoy working here if you


  • Thrive in a dynamic environment where collaboration, networking, and strategic discussions are part of everyday life.
  • Feel motivated by the opportunity to help shape a new finance organization within a newly formed company in a transforming global automotive market.
  • Enjoy working across functions and cultures and bring strong communication skills that help you build trust and drive improvements.
  • Appreciate a workplace that is both professional and fun, with supportive colleagues, global networks, and opportunities to grow through leadership and development programs.


Some Additional Information

The position will be located in Gothenburg, Sweden, hired on a local contract. You will report to the future CFO in SKF Automotive. For questions regarding the recruitment process, please contact Stina Scheller, Recruitment Expert Automotive, by email (stina.scheller@skf.com). Please note that we do not accept applications via email.


Is this you?

If the answer is yes, submit your application with your CV in English no later than 2025-09-28. We will screen candidates continuously throughout the application period, so make sure to submit your application as soon as possible.


At SKF, we are committed to promoting fairness and inclusivity throughout our recruitment process. To achieve this, we may include assessments and verify the information in your application in compliance with country-specific laws and regulations. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact the recruiter.
descriptionText: SKF has been around for more than a century and today we are one of the world’s largest global suppliers of bearings and supporting solutions for rotating equipment. Our products can be found literally everywhere in society. This means that we are an important part of the everyday lives of people and companies around the world.In September of 2024, SKF announced the separation of its Automotive business, with the objective to build two world-leading companies. The role you are applying for will be a part of the new company created from the Automotive business. This means you will have the opportunity to be a part of shaping a new company aimed at meeting the needs of the transforming global automotive market. Manager Group Accounting & Reporting - Automotive Business We’re seeking a senior finance professional who will establish our new Group Accounting & Reporting department within the SKF Automotive business. In this high-impact role, you will work closely with the business, contribute to strategic decisions, and work in a challenging, international environment.As our Manager Group Accounting & Reporting, you’ll take on a unique opportunity to shape a new function and team, responsible for group consolidation, quarterly and annual reporting. You will also be working on the preparation of the requirements and standards of a Nasdaq Stockholm listing. Together with our CFO, your highly skilled team, and the full finance organization, you will develop processes and structures that support the evolving needs of the business.Key ResponsibilitiesManage the process of creating group consolidated financial statements and external reports, which encompass quarterly and annual statements as well sustainability reports. Develop and maintain group-wide accounting policies and reporting instructions. Ensure compliance with external accounting standards and prepare for future listing requirements. Empower, develop, and support a lean and skilled team within Group Accounting & Reporting. Manage the parent company’s accounting and reporting. Enhance competence development within the accounting and reporting area, in collaboration with the Finance Academy Lead. Act as an expert and advisor in accounting matters and collaborate closely with auditors, IR, and other key stakeholders. Represent Group Finance in strategic development projects. RequirementsUniversity degree in Business Administration or equivalent. Solid experience in financial reporting and preparation of annual reports for a listed company, in either the role of Group accounting or from Auditing. Deep understanding of accounting standards, regulations, and reporting requirements, including those relevant for listed companies. Keen interest in and understanding of current and future finance processes, concepts, and systems. Experienced leader, with the ability to support, inspire, and empower your team to thrive. Fluent in English and in Swedish (verbal & written) If you have experience in sustainability reporting, being part of preparing a company for public listing, and/or working at a manufacturing company, it’s a big plus. You will enjoy working here if you Thrive in a dynamic environment where collaboration, networking, and strategic discussions are part of everyday life. Feel motivated by the opportunity to help shape a new finance organization within a newly formed company in a transforming global automotive market. Enjoy working across functions and cultures and bring strong communication skills that help you build trust and drive improvements. Appreciate a workplace that is both professional and fun, with supportive colleagues, global networks, and opportunities to grow through leadership and development programs. Some Additional Information The position will be located in Gothenburg, Sweden, hired on a local contract. You will report to the future CFO in SKF Automotive. For questions regarding the recruitment process, please contact Stina Scheller, Recruitment Expert Automotive, by email (stina.scheller@skf.com). Please note that we do not accept applications via email.Is this you? If the answer is yes, submit your application with your CV in English no later than 2025-09-28. We will screen candidates continuously throughout the application period, so make sure to submit your application as soon as possible. At SKF, we are committed to promoting fairness and inclusivity throughout our recruitment process. To achieve this, we may include assessments and verify the information in your application in compliance with country-specific laws and regulations. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact the recruiter.
title: Manager Group Accounting & Reporting - Automotive Business

companyDescription:
Assessio exists because humans cannot make completely objective decisions – in recruitment processes, we can be our own worst enemy. Today, your chances of getting a job are higher if you are European, tall, look healthy and do not have children. We want to change that.

Assessio is a SaaS company specialising in strategic HR and developing tests and tools in occupational psychology. Our tools can help organisations predict who has the best potential to succeed in a role, find future leaders within the organisation, identify toxic behaviours in leadership, and match candidates to the organisational culture through values.

We provide our customers with data-driven insights about both current and potential employees. By making decisions based on reliable data rather than gut feeling, we can discover the potential that exists and ensure that no talent goes to waste.

To teach organisations how to make better decisions based on data, our consultants hold both customer-specific and open training courses in our tools. Among other things, the focus is on our digital platform, where we have gathered all our tools for matching, selection, development and business-driven HR. The platform makes it easy to combine multiple tools, create ranking lists in screening processes, compare candidates with each other or compare a candidate with a group.
descriptionHtml:
About Assessio

At Assessio, we have a bold ambition: to become the leading HR Tech provider in Europe by 2028, eliminating talent waste by helping people thrive in roles where they can perform at their best.

Through our transformation into a scalable, AI-powered people data platform, we solutions that span recruitment and development. By combining +70 years of scientific heritage with a modern, intuitive product experience, Assessio serves global enterprises and public organizations with high HR tech maturity. Our mission? To lead the transformation toward a future with Zero Talent Waste by empowering individuals to unleash their full potential in roles aligned with their strengths.

With offices in Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Germany, Estonia, and the Netherlands, and more on the way, we’re building a strong international presence. Join us in shaping the future of talent management in Europe. We are 170+ dedicated co-workers who share our vision of Zero Talent Waste.

About The Role

We are now looking for an experienced Group Financial Controller to join our growing Finance team at HQ in Stockholm. This is a high-impact role with end-to-end responsibility for group financial control, consolidation, and statutory reporting across our international SaaS group. As we continue our rapid expansion — including the integration of our newly acquired French SaaS business — this role is instrumental in creating scalable and harmonized financial operations across the group.

You will work closely with our CFO and lead a small, distributed finance team across multiple European locations. Alongside core responsibilities, you’ll also take ownership of two strategic initiatives:

  • Driving standardized use and optimization of our ERP system (Visma) across all entities.
  • Leading the financial integration of our French acquisition.

Candidates who are available to start on short notice will be given priority.

Main Responsibilities

  • Lead the monthly group consolidation and internal reporting process (IFRS/local GAAP).
  • Oversee statutory reporting and annual audits across multiple jurisdictions.
  • Own financial control across subsidiaries: closing, reconciliations, intercompany processes.
  • Manage cash reporting and covenant reporting to our financial partners.
  • Drive ERP harmonization and ensure consistent system usage across local finance teams.
  • Lead financial integration of newly acquired subsidiaries, starting with France.
  • Coordinate with external advisors: auditors, tax authorities, legal counsel.
  • Manage a team of 8–10 finance professionals and external accounting partners across Europe.

Who are you?

At Assessio, we believe in potential. While experience matters, your drive, curiosity, and collaborative mindset are what truly set you apart.

You are a structured, energetic, and pragmatic team player- someone who thrives in a dynamic international setting. You're hands-on when needed, but also capable of leading and inspiring remote teams. You enjoy simplifying complexity, taking ownership of strategic processes, and are comfortable managing cross-border collaboration.

We believe you’ll thrive in this role if you:

  • Hold a university degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • Have 5–8 years of experience in international finance roles, preferably in SaaS or audit (Big 4 experience a plus).
  • Possess strong technical accounting knowledge (IFRS/local GAAPs).
  • Have hands-on experience with ERP systems — Visma experience is highly valued.
  • Are confident leading consolidation, audits, and reporting in an international environment.
  • Excel at stakeholder communication — from CFOs to local finance staff to auditors.
  • Communicate fluently in English, both spoken and written.

Nice to have:

  • Experience in M&A financial integration and project management.
  • A background in scaling financial operations during periods of high growth.
  • Familiarity with Swedish financial and regulatory context is a plus.

A fair and data-driven recruitment process

We practice what we preach. That’s why you probably will complete three short assessments (personality, cognitive ability, and motivation). It takes about 50 minutes and ensures a fair, unbiased selection process based on potential.

You will find more info about the process at our Career Site.  

We look forward to getting in touch! 

If you have any questions regarding the recruitment process, please contact Bryan Bailey at bryan.bailey@assessio.se.

descriptionHtml:
Är du en driven ekonom som vill mer än bara bokföra? Välkommen till oss!

Vi söker dig som vill arbeta brett med redovisning, koncernrapportering och affärssystemutveckling – samtidigt som du är en viktig kugge i vår vardag med allt från lager och inköp till e-handel och import. Låter det som en ovanligt varierad ekonomroll? Det är det.


Din roll i teamet

Som ekonom hos oss får du en bred och utvecklande roll där du är med och driver både dagligt ekonomiarbete och förbättringsinitiativ. Du får stort förtroende, arbetar nära verksamheten och är med och utvecklar hur vi jobbar – framför allt i vårt affärssystem Business Central.

Dina uppgifter innefattar bland annat:

  • Löpande redovisning, bokslut och rapportering till koncern
  • Leverantörsreskontra, kundreskontra, betalningar och utlägg
  • Tidrapportering och support till kollegor i ekonomiska frågor
  • Stöd i webshop- och orderhantering
  • Samarbete med inköp/logistik kring lagerflöden, tull och import
  • Delta i utveckling och effektivisering av ekonomiprocesser och system


Vi tror att du har:

  • Några års erfarenhet som ekonom, gärna i bolag med lager och/eller e-handel
  • Vana vid redovisning, rapportering och gärna koncernstruktur
  • Erfarenhet av affärssystem – gärna Business Central / Dynamics 365
  • Förståelse för inköp, import och grundläggande tullhantering
  • Goda kunskaper i Excel och ett systemintresse
  • Flytande svenska och god engelska

 

Vem är du?

Du är noggrann, lösningsorienterad och tycker om när det händer mycket. Du gillar att ha koll på detaljer – men också att förbättra och förenkla. Och du trivs i en miljö där du får både ansvar och frihet.


Vad erbjuder vi?

  • En bred och utvecklande ekonomroll med ansvar och variation
  • Möjlighet att påverka och utveckla våra arbetssätt
  • Kollegor med driv, hjärta och högt i tak
  • En plats i ett växande bolag där din insats märks


📍 Placering: Sollentuna

📆 Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse

📩 Ansök senast: 2025-09-15 eller så snart som möjligt – urval sker löpande!

Individuell lönesättning



👉 Skicka din ansökan redan idag och bli en del av vår resa!

companyDescription:
Azets is an international business advisory group, with 9,000 local experts in 190 locations across eight countries, backed by progressive technology. We are united by one clear purpose: to improve the lives of our clients, colleagues and communities in a sustainable way.

100,000+ clients on unique journeys trust us to meet their immediate and evolving needs, remove barriers, and deliver sustained outcomes so they can move forward with confidence.

Your goals, our people, a fresh mindset – solving problems so you can confidently focus on what you do best.
companySlogan: Move forward with confidence.
descriptionHtml:
Är du en erfaren och engagerad revisor som söker nya utmaningar? På Azets får du chansen att arbeta med kunder inom segmenten små- och medelstora företag, ideell sektor, offentlig verksamhet med fokus på kommuner och regioner. Tillsammans med härliga kollegor får du stora möjligheter att växa och påverka din karriärväg som auktoriserad revisor. Hos oss på Azets kan du verkligen göra skillnad.

Azets är en internationell aktör inom ekonomi, lön och HR. Azets  Sverige expanderar genom att förvärva KPMG Sveriges verksamhet för revision, rådgivning och skatt för små och medelstora företag, samt kommuner och regioner. Med detta förvärv etablerar vi oss i över 30 städer och välkomnar cirka 300 nya kollegor. Bli en del av vår tillväxtresa och bidra till att forma framtiden för vår verksamhet tillsammans med dina kollegor i Växjö! I Växjö är flera av våra tjänsteområden representerade, vilket innebär att du blir en del av ett härligt gäng med bred kompetens och god sammanhållning.

Din roll hos oss

Som erfaren revisor hos Azets kommer du att leda arbetet i skräddarsydda team med kvalificerade medarbetare, för att möta våra kunders specifika behov och förväntningar. Du leder revisioner vilket inkluderar allt från granskning av redovisningsposter och finansiella rapporter till revisionsnära rådgivning, relationsbyggande med kunder. Vi strävar efter att alltid ligga i framkant, vilket innebär att du får möjlighet att arbeta med den senaste tekniken och anpassa dig till förändringar i vår omvärld.

Med ökade krav på hållbarhetsrapportering blir granskning av dessa rapporter en naturlig och viktig del av vår revision. Detta ger dig möjlighet att utvecklas inom ett område som främjar ansvarsfullt företagande och stärker hållbarhetsaspekterna i företagsstyrningen.

Dina egenskaper och kvalifikationer

Vi söker dig som är:

  • Auktoriserad revisor eller har arbetat som revisor i 2-5 år och har siktet inställt på auktorisation.
  • Är flytande i både svenska och engelska.

Som person är du lyhörd, kommunikativ och har en stark vilja att hjälpa andra att hitta lösningar. Med fokus på kvalitet och resultat tar du initiativ, arbetar affärsinriktat och ser möjligheter i förändring. Ditt flexibla synsätt och din förmåga att se saker ur flera perspektiv gör att du anpassar dig snabbt och levererar med precision.

Vad vi erbjuder

Hos Azets får du goda utvecklingsmöjligheter och chansen att arbeta med den typ av kunder som du har ett brinnande intresse för. Du kommer att utmanas i spännande uppdrag hos våra kunder och erbjudas ett omväxlande och ansvarsfullt arbete med nära kundkontakter. Våra medarbetare är vårt ansikte utåt och våra kunder väljer Azets som samarbetspartner tack vare vår yrkesetik, vårt engagemang i uppdragen och hög kvalitet i våra leveranser. Hos oss får du arbeta proaktivt med att leverera branschanpassade tjänster som skapar mervärde för våra kunder.

Ansökan

Din ansökan består av CV och motivering till varför du är intresserad av rollen som erfaren revisor på Azets. Då vi arbetar med löpande urval ser vi gärna att du skickar in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.

Ansökningsprocessen består av intervjuer, kapacitetstester och referenstagning samt kredit-och bakgrundskontroll.

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

Kontaktperson:

Caroline Bergendahl, Talent Acquisition, caroline.bergendahl@azets.com
