companyDescription:
EP2C recruits talents looking for an international career... If you are a skilled engineer willing to work on challenging projects abroad, apply now!

We act as specialist consultants for major operating companies in the international market of Energy, Infrastructures, Environment & Process Industry, Water Treatment, Life Sciences & Nuclear Industry.

We provide a combination of project management support, project engineering support, construction supervision, third party/vendor inspection and recruitment selection. The scope of services is provided at various phases of a project ranging from exploration to production, engineering to construction, upstream and downstream.

More information on www.ep2c-energy.com
companySlogan: Bring on new talents
descriptionHtml:
Description Du Poste

Comptable Senior

Dans quelle structure serez-vous ?

Nous recherchons pour l’un de nos clients, acteur majeur du secteur du génie civil et de la construction, un Comptable Senior (H/F) afin de renforcer l’équipe finance locale dans le cadre du projet STEGRA, basé à Boden, Suède.

Quelles seront vos missions et responsabilités ?

Superviser les opérations comptables quotidiennes et garantir la fiabilité des écritures.

Assurer le suivi des comptes fournisseurs, clients et immobilisations.

Piloter la préparation des clôtures mensuelles, trimestrielles et annuelles.

Garantir la conformité fiscale et réglementaire (normes suédoises et internationales).

Effectuer les rapprochements bancaires et contrôler la trésorerie.

Fournir un support aux équipes projets dans le suivi des coûts et budgets.

Encadrer et accompagner des profils comptables plus juniors si nécessaire.

Profil recherché

Quel profil et quelles compétences pour ce poste ?

Diplôme en comptabilité, finance ou équivalent.

Minimum 7 à 10 ans d’expérience en comptabilité, dont une partie à un poste senior.

Solide maîtrise des normes comptables suédoises (Swedish GAAP) et procédures fiscales.

Bonnes connaissances en comptabilité internationale.

Anglais courant indispensable ; le suédois serait un atout.

Rigueur, organisation, autonomie et leadership.

Conditions De Travail

Localisation : Boden, Suède

Contrat long terme : 2 à 3 ans selon la durée du projet

Temps de travail : 5 jours/semaine

Salaires Et Avantages

Package attractif

Ce poste vous intéresse ?

Envoyez-nous votre candidature !

EP2C Energy s'engage en faveur de la diversité et de l'inclusion. Nous encourageons les candidatures de personnes reconnues travailleurs handicapés.

Dans le cadre de notre politique Qualité, Santé, Sécurité et Environnement (QSSE), nous attachons une importance capitale au respect des règles QSSE afin de garantir un environnement de travail sécurisé pour nos collaborateurs, avec l’ambition de contribuer positivement à l’environnement.
descriptionText: Description Du PosteComptable Senior Dans quelle structure serez-vous ?Nous recherchons pour l’un de nos clients, acteur majeur du secteur du génie civil et de la construction, un Comptable Senior (H/F) afin de renforcer l’équipe finance locale dans le cadre du projet STEGRA, basé à Boden, Suède.Quelles seront vos missions et responsabilités ?Superviser les opérations comptables quotidiennes et garantir la fiabilité des écritures.Assurer le suivi des comptes fournisseurs, clients et immobilisations.Piloter la préparation des clôtures mensuelles, trimestrielles et annuelles.Garantir la conformité fiscale et réglementaire (normes suédoises et internationales).Effectuer les rapprochements bancaires et contrôler la trésorerie.Fournir un support aux équipes projets dans le suivi des coûts et budgets.Encadrer et accompagner des profils comptables plus juniors si nécessaire.Profil recherchéQuel profil et quelles compétences pour ce poste ?Diplôme en comptabilité, finance ou équivalent.Minimum 7 à 10 ans d’expérience en comptabilité, dont une partie à un poste senior.Solide maîtrise des normes comptables suédoises (Swedish GAAP) et procédures fiscales.Bonnes connaissances en comptabilité internationale.Anglais courant indispensable ; le suédois serait un atout.Rigueur, organisation, autonomie et leadership.Conditions De TravailLocalisation : Boden, SuèdeContrat long terme : 2 à 3 ans selon la durée du projetTemps de travail : 5 jours/semaineSalaires Et AvantagesPackage attractifCe poste vous intéresse ?Envoyez-nous votre candidature !EP2C Energy s'engage en faveur de la diversité et de l'inclusion. companyDescription:
Candles Scandinavia is a fast growing company with many different services in the scented candle business.

CANDLES SCANDINAVIA PRODUCTION
In our candle factory in Sweden, we are producing scented and unscented jar candles in all shapes and sizes. In addition to several own brands, such as Blanche, Twin Candles and Olivia & Co we produce big volumes for most of the big brands, retail chains and department stores in Scandinavia. With our own in house design department we can help each customer to find jut the right type of candles suitable for there segment and brand. No volumes are to small or to big for us and the flexibility to change from one fragrance and jar size or from a small run production to bigger ones are one of our main advantages. Safety and quality is our main focus and all candles are carefully tested in our labb.

DESIGNING YOUR CANDLE
Designing and producing your own labeled candle is pretty straight forward. All you have to do is to decide what fragrance you desire to create the right atmosphere around your candle when lit. We have a wide, and continually expanding range of fragrances from top producers with a long experience.
The look and feel should of course follow your brand and send the right vibes to attract your audience. You might provide us with a complete design or hand it over to us, we have experience and are happy to take you through the whole process.

descriptionHtml:
Candles Scandinavia, med kunder som H&M, Åhléns, Lyko, Rituals med flera, är idag en ledande europeisk leverantör av doftljus producerade med 100% växtbaserat, biologiskt nedbrytbart vax, baserad på svenskodlad raps. Bolaget erbjuder ett hållbart och miljövänligt alternativ till den annars paraffindominerade ljusmarknaden där råvaran utvinns av fossilt bränsle. Bolagets styrka ligger främst i en kostnadseffektiv metod för storskalig produktion av kundanpassade doftljus, helt av naturligt och miljövänligt vax. Candles Scandinavia bedriver sin verksamhet i fräscha och ändamålsenliga lokaler centralt i Örebro och är idag ca 50 medarbetare med en omsättning om ca 200 miljoner kronor. Utöver detta ingår även ett nyförvärvat tyskt bolag, HashtagYou, i koncernen med ytterligare ca 55 medarbetare och en omsättning om ca 250 Mkr. Candles Scandinavia AB (publ) är sedan november 2021 noterade på Nasdaq First North Growth Market.

Våra värdeord är viktiga för oss och finns med oss i vårt dagliga arbete:

VI vågar och är innovativa

VI vet

VI gör det nu

VI är effektiva

VI är förändringsbenägna & arbetar annorlunda

VI kommunicerar & involverar

Vill du arbeta i ett börsnoterat tillväxtbolag i internationell miljö och vara en del av att ta koncernens ekonomifunktion till nästa nivå? Då kan detta vara rollen för dig!

Om rollen

Vi fortsätter att växa och behöver bli fler! Som Accountant, en nyinrättad roll, hos Candles Scandinavia AB kommer du arbeta nära Finance & HR Manager – som du också rapporterar till - och vara en viktig del av koncernens ekonomiavdelning. Vi söker dig som tycker redovisning är både spännande och utvecklande och har förmågan att på ett effektivt och korrekt sätt ta fram bokslut samt intern- och externrapporter. Genom ditt arbete säkerställer du inte bara att vi möter våra deadlines som noterat bolag, utan bidrar också med värdefulla beslutsunderlag till ledningsgruppen och övriga organisationen.

Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter

  • Löpande redovisning och bokföring
  • Avstämningar av balanskonton, inklusive bankkonton
  • Framtagande av månads-, kvartals- och årsbokslut i samarbete med Finance & HR Manager
  • Moms- och skattedeklarationer
  • Ansvara för anläggningsregister och avskrivningar
  • Medverkan i arbetet med årsredovisningar och revision
  • Stöd i intäkts- & kostnadsuppföljning och analyser
  • Medverkan i budget- och prognosarbete
  • Framtagande av koncernredovisning
  • Effektivisering av ekonomiprocesser och rapporteringsrutiner
  • Delta i utveckling och förbättring av vårt affärssystem, Microsoft Dynamics Business Central samt vårt BI verktyg Power BI
  • Back-up rörande hantering av kund- och leverantörsreskontra
  • Hålla sig uppdaterad om gällande regler inom redovisning och skatt

Kvalifikationer

  • Ekonomisk utbildning, minst på gymnasienivå – gärna högskoleexamen inom redovisning eller ekonomi
  • Mycket goda kunskaper inom redovisning, erfarenhet av koncernredovisning är meriterande
  • Erfarenhet från producerande och ägarledda företag är meriterande
  • Goda kunskaper i Excel och erfarenhet av affärssystem
  • God förståelse för lager-, produktions- och kostnadsflöden
  • Kunskap om gällande redovisningsprinciper och skattelagstiftning
  • Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift

Personliga egenskaper

  • Strukturerad och noggrann
  • Självgående
  • Samarbetsinriktad
  • Analytisk och lösningsorienterad
  • Trivs i en föränderlig och dynamisk miljö
  • Förståelse för helheten – från produktion till finansiell rapportering
  • Prestigelös, du trivs där alla hjälper till och bidrar – oavsett titel

Vi erbjuder

  • En dynamisk och innovativ arbetsmiljö i ett företag med stark tillväxt och fokus på hållbarhet.
  • Möjlighet att påverka och utveckla processer
  • Ett engagerat och stöttande team med hög kompetens.

Ansökan

Är du redo att ta nästa steg i din karriär? Skicka in din ansökan med CV och personligt brev senast den 17 september 2025. Urval sker löpande, så vänta inte med att söka! Vi ser fram emot att välkomna en ny kollega som delar vår passion för hållbarhet, kvalitet och innovation!

Har du frågor är du välkommen att maila till Martin Fredell mfr@candles.se alt 076-110 31 00

Tjänsten tillträds så snart som möjligt.

Mer information om oss hittar du på www.candles.se

Du ansöker senast 2025-09-17 genom att klicka på knappen nedan.

descriptionHtml:
Aceve is in business to back the builders of society. We are the SaaS powerhouse, built by engineers, developers, salespeople, administrators, marketeers, controllers, and much mor. With over 500 employees, more than 950 million SEK in revenue and extensive experience in the field, we combine tradition and tech to provide a pioneering platform that helps our customers to achieve more. 

About the role
We're looking for a driven and detail-oriented Group Financial Controller to support our international operations. This is a key role within our finance team, where you’ll contribute to statutory reporting, consolidation, and financial analysis—helping shape the financial strategy of a growing group.

🚀 What you’ll do

  • Assist in monthly, quarterly, and annual closings, including group consolidation.
  • Prepare Swedish statutory Group accounts in line with IFRS.
  • Support the development and implementation of the Group’s tax and treasury strategy.
  • Participate in the external audit process and collaborate with auditors.
  • Compile and analyze financial reports from subsidiaries across various countries.

🧠 Qualifications & Skills

  • Academic degree in Economics, Finance, Accounting, or similar.
  • 5+ years of relevant experience in finance, accounting, or auditing.
  • Strong experience in Swedish Group reporting and/or IFRS is a plus.
  • Excellent communication skills in Swedish and English, both spoken and written.
  • Strong analytical skills and a keen eye for detail.

💫 Who you are

  • Independent, proactive, and confident in managing complex tasks.
  • Hands-on with a structured, detail-oriented approach to work.
  • Accountable, deadline-driven, and solution-focused.
  • A team player who thrives under pressure and handles multiple tasks with ease.
  • Willing to travel for subsidiary meetings and training sessions.
  • Able to clearly communicate complex financial information to different stakeholders.

🌍 Why join us?

You'll be part of an ambitious, collaborative team with a strong sense of purpose. We value personal growth and encourage flexibility and innovation in everything we do. If you're passionate about finance and want to make an impact in a global organization—this is your opportunity.

Not ticking every box? No worries! We value potential and drive, so apply anyway or connect with us- you might be just what we're looking for in our growing organisation! Join Aceve and take your expertise to the next level, together we are redefining what’s possible in craft and construction solutions!

Aceve is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our selection process is conducted continuously and without discrimination, ensuring an equal opportunity for all candidates.

descriptionHtml:
About the job Advisory | Accounting | Audit | Tax | Payroll

About Us

ITC Worldwide Advisors, LLP is a rapidly growing, modern provider of professional services to the middle market, operating globally. Our mission is to instill confidence in a world of change. Enable Transformation while doing more with less! Our exceptional people are the cornerstone of our unique culture and talent experience.

Responsibilities

  • Understand clients' needs and expectations, their business and industry, accounting and control systems, employees, company values, and industry-related GAAP and GAAS issues.
  • Develop an understanding of the ITC audit automation approach and ERP tools.
  • Assess risks and evaluate the client's internal control structure.
  • Perform substantive tests and tests of internal controls to identify and resolve accounting or reporting issues.
  • Prepare financial statements under prescribed formats.

Required Skills And Qualifications

  • Completion of 90+ credit hours towards a B.A. / B.S. degree or equivalent from an accredited university in Accounting or Finance.
  • You must have passed your ACCA, CPA, CFA, or CIMA exam.

N.B: We kindly request you to complete a brief survey. Your feedback will help us enhance the candidate experience and ensure we attract the best talent.

companyDescription:
With annual sales of about CHF 10.5 billion in fiscal year 2023/24, the Zurich-based Barry Callebaut Group is the world’s leading manufacturer of chocolate and cocoa products – from sourcing and processing cocoa beans to producing the finest chocolates, including chocolate fillings, decorations and compounds. 

The Group runs more than 60 production facilities worldwide and employs a diverse and dedicated global workforce of more than 13,000 people. 

The Barry Callebaut Group serves the entire food industry, from industrial food manufacturers to artisanal and professional users of chocolate, such as chocolatiers, pastry chefs, bakers, hotels, restaurants or caterers. The global brands catering to the specific needs of these Gourmet customers are Callebaut® and Cacao Barry®, Carma® and the decorations specialist Mona Lisa®. 

The Barry Callebaut Group is committed to make sustainable chocolate the norm to help ensure future supplies of cocoa and improve farmer livelihoods. It supports the Cocoa Horizons Foundation in its goal to shape a sustainable cocoa and chocolate future.
companySlogan: We make sustainable chocolate the norm!
descriptionHtml:
Om jobbet

Är du en erfaren ekonom med ett öga för kvalitet? Trivs du i en dynamisk, internationell miljö och gillar att samarbeta över teamgränser? Då kan rollen som Huvudansvarig Redovisningsekonom på vår anläggning i Kågeröd som bl a tillverkar chokladdryckspulver vara rätt för dig.


Om rollen

Som Huvudansvarig Redovisningsekonom ansvarar du för helheten kring IFRS- och lagstadgad rapportering för Sverige. Du säkerställer att redovisningsaktiviteter genomförs korrekt och i tid, i enlighet med koncernens principer. Rollen är central för att upprätthålla finansiell integritet, stödja interna och externa intressenter samt driva förbättringar inom redovisningsprocesser.

Du samarbetar nära med vårt Global Business Services (GBS)-team och får vägledning från Manager Accounting & Tax Compliance EMEA vid komplexa frågor och ad hoc-uppdrag.


Ansvarsområden

· Leda månads-, halvårs- och årsbokslut, inklusive BOF-rapportering

· Utföra balansräkningsgranskningar och lagstadgad redovisning

· Hantera skatte- och pensionsredovisning (IFRS 19) samt tekniska redovisningsfrågor

· Säkerställa styrning av interna kontrollsystem (ICS)

· Samarbeta med GBS kring löne- och lagerredovisning

· Ge vägledning till GBS och andra finansfunktioner (Konsolidering, Skatt, Treasury, GAR)

· Säkerställa kvalitet, konsekvens och integritet i finansiella rapporter

· Dela redovisningsexpertis inom EMEA

· Hantera serviceavtal med Shared Services

· Driva kontinuerliga förbättringar inom redovisningsprocesser

· Stödja finansrelaterade projekt och upprätthålla relationer med externa intressenter


Om dig

Du är en lösningsorienterad ekonom med ett proaktivt arbetssätt och god samarbetsförmåga. Du har djup förståelse för IFRS och lokal redovisningspraxis, och trivs i en internationell miljö med högt tempo.


Kvalifikationer

· Kandidatexamen eller masterexamen inom ekonomi, redovisning eller liknande

· Mycket goda kunskaper i engelska, både i tal och skrift

· Erfarenhet av SAP eller andra ERP-system

· ACCA DipIFR eller medlemskap i ACCA/CPA är meriterande

· Stark kunskap om IFRS och svensk/dansk redovisningspraxis


Erfarenhet

· Gedigen erfarenhet inom ekonomi med fokus på redovisning

· Erfarenhet från multinationella företag, gärna inom B2B eller livsmedels-/dryckesindustrin

· God förståelse för redovisning, controlling och finansiella rapporteringsmetoder


Personliga egenskaper

· Analytisk och systematisk problemlösare

· God kommunikationsförmåga och hög integritet

· Lagspelare med förmåga att hantera flera uppgifter samtidigt

· Proaktiv och praktisk i ditt arbetssätt


Redo att ta nästa steg i din karriär?

Skicka in din ansökan och bli en del av ett team som värdesätter expertis, samarbete och ständig utveckling.

Du har djup förståelse för IFRS och lokal redovisningspraxis, och trivs i en internationell miljö med högt tempo.Kvalifikationer· Kandidatexamen eller masterexamen inom ekonomi, redovisning eller liknande· Mycket goda kunskaper i engelska, både i tal och skrift· Erfarenhet av SAP eller andra ERP-system· ACCA DipIFR eller medlemskap i ACCA/CPA är meriterande· Stark kunskap om IFRS och svensk/dansk redovisningspraxisErfarenhet· Gedigen erfarenhet inom ekonomi med fokus på redovisning· Erfarenhet från multinationella företag, gärna inom B2B eller livsmedels-/dryckesindustrin· God förståelse för redovisning, controlling och finansiella rapporteringsmetoderPersonliga egenskaper· Analytisk och systematisk problemlösare· God kommunikationsförmåga och hög integritet· Lagspelare med förmåga att hantera flera uppgifter samtidigt· Proaktiv och praktisk i ditt arbetssättRedo att ta nästa steg i din karriär?Skicka in din ansökan och bli en del av ett team som värdesätter expertis, samarbete och ständig utveckling.

companyDescription:
People around the world today feel lost, lonely, disconnected and excluded. The social climate crisis is fundamental and requires a radical shift. The Inner Foundation invests in innovative solutions that improve inner health and strengthen diversity, equity and inclusion in society – uniting public, private and non-profit efforts to make sustainable human development happen.

The Inner Foundation, founded by Annika Sten Pärson and Pär-Jörgen Pärson, aims to be a significant force for good by operating as an impact investor, grant-giver and thought leader, focusing on improving the state of mental health and fighting for diversity & inclusion. 

With a long-term commitment, The Inner Foundation has initially invested in for profit organisations such as Meela, an initiative that aims to personalize mental health care for women, Nudge Labs, which focuses on well-being and stress reduction in the workplace, and other NGOs such as StrongMinds, treating depression at scale in sub-Saharan Africa and 1825-Therapy Center for Young Adults, a Stockholm-based mental health clinic for young adults that the government health care system is failing to adequately support. Within the area of diversity, equity and inclusion, The Inner Foundation supports First Close Partners, a fund-of-funds investing in underrepresented venture fund managers on a global scale.
companySlogan: Investing in innovative solutions to improve inner health and strengthen diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging.
descriptionHtml:
Financial Controller


About The Inner Foundation

We are The Inner Foundation, an independent non-profit based in Stockholm, founded in 2022 by Annika Sten Pärson and Pär-Jörgen Pärson. With a €100 million, 20-year commitment, we invest in entrepreneurs and intrapreneurs building solutions that strengthen mental health and advance diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEIB)—with a particular focus on emerging adults (ages 18–30).


As an impact investor, grant-maker, and ecosystem builder, we operate with a catalytic hybrid financing model, combining grants and equity investments. Today, our portfolio spans more than 35 organizations across Sub-Saharan Africa, Europe, and North America.

Learn more at www.theinnerfoundation.org



The Role

We are now looking for a Financial Controller to serve as the operational backbone of our investment, grantmaking and platform activities and strengthen our internal capabilities and processes. 

The role will begin as a part-time (up to 50%) fixed-term contract until summer 2026, with a primary focus on building robust processes for data gathering, dashboard reporting, and financial planning. After that, there is the potential to grow into a full-time position.

This is an exciting and excellent opportunity for an experienced professional seeking part-time engagement, or for an early-career candidate eager to grow into a full-time role within social impact investing. You will report to the Founding Partner & Executive Chair.

 

 What You’ll Do

  • Coordinate portfolio companies’ financial and impact data for reporting and analysis.
  • Maintain portfolio dashboards (Looker Studio) covering capital deployed, commitments, and impact metrics.
  • Track investments in funds and blended finance vehicles.
  • Maintain portfolio cash flow forecasts and recommend timely capital allocation.
  • Collaborate with the accounting team on monthly financial reporting.
  • Prepare internal business reviews, board updates, and impact reports.
  • Act as liaison for banks, accountants, and service providers.
  • Improve internal operating procedures and reporting tools as the portfolio grows.



Who You Are

We are looking for a structured, proactive, and collaborative professional who thrives in a mission-driven environment. You combine analytical strength with a “can-do” mindset and are motivated to contribute both strategically and hands-on.

  • Background in investment operations, fund administration, management consulting, or similar.
  • Strong analytical and data management skills.
  • Comfortable with Excel, Google Workspace and reporting tools (Looker Studio); able to learn new systems quickly.
  • Strong communicator who builds trust with diverse stakeholders.
  • Experience from social enterprises or non-profits is meritorious, but not required.


Practical Details

  • Part-time (up to 50%) contract until summer 2026, with potential for full-time thereafter.
  • Based in Stockholm, with flexibility for remote work but ideally regular in-person meetings.
  • Applications reviewed on a rolling basis. The last date to apply is September 28.


﻿To apply, send your CV and a cover letter to  jobs@theinnerfoundation.org describing your interest, relevant experience, and availability. Please note that we will only be reviewing applications submitted directly to this email address.

About the job Advisory | Accounting | Audit | Tax | Payroll

About Us

ITC Worldwide Advisors, LLP is a rapidly growing, modern provider of professional services to the middle market, operating globally. Our mission is to instill confidence in a world of change. Enable Transformation while doing more with less! Our exceptional people are the cornerstone of our unique culture and talent experience.

Responsibilities

  • Understand clients' needs and expectations, their business and industry, accounting and control systems, employees, company values, and industry-related GAAP and GAAS issues.
  • Develop an understanding of the ITC audit automation approach and ERP tools.
  • Assess risks and evaluate the client's internal control structure.
  • Perform substantive tests and tests of internal controls to identify and resolve accounting or reporting issues.
  • Prepare financial statements under prescribed formats.

Required Skills And Qualifications

  • Completion of 90+ credit hours towards a B.A. / B.S. degree or equivalent from an accredited university in Accounting or Finance.

companyDescription: At Quinyx, we help you to optimize your business, manage your workforce, and engage your frontline workers in one single platform! Our all-in-one solution helps over one million workers keep track of their daily schedules for a better work-life balance. And through AI forecasting and more efficient time reporting, we optimize your scheduling process to make sure shifts start on time and that your workers have the flexibility to celebrate birthdays, make the school run, or just catch a break—without the fuss—so that work just...works!
companySlogan: The all-in-one Frontline Platform
descriptionHtml:
Do you want to help us unleash the secret to how happy employees create a happy business? We are an innovative, agile, and continuously growing modern tech company on a path to improving the work lives of millions around the world.

We develop workforce management solutions - software that makes scheduling and time reporting more smooth and more flexible for almost a million users worldwide!

We value passion, quality, innovation, trust, and collaboration. You’ll be given the opportunity to make an impact and directly contribute to our success, bring new ideas, and have full ownership of your area.

Are you curious and ready to take the first steps on an awesome journey and make magic happen with us? As a member of Quinyx, you will be part of an inclusive and diverse workplace where everyone is allowed to be themselves and thrive.

We have a hybrid working set up and therefore you will be required to attend our Stockholm office minimum twice per week.

About The Role

As a Financial Controller you will be responsible for the accurate and timely reporting of financial information within the group. With Quinyx's strong growth, you will also be a central part of the continued development of Quinyx's global financial processes and routines. You will manage different finance related development projects and processes to improve our way of working.

What You’ll Be Doing

  • Proactively develop, implement and improve Quinyx's financial processes, routines and working methods together with the CFO, Head of Accounting and Manager, Business Controlling to enable the company's strong growth and international expansion. In particular, actively improve the use and implementation of our financial systems including accounting and FP&A.
  • Driving the monthly close of the Swedish entities and making sure numbers are correctly reported in a timely manner.
  • Preparation of fast and fair period and annual accounts including running reports on accounts receivables, the period’s revenue recognition, annual reports, tax returns, declarations and audits.
  • Analysis and presentation of P&L, cash flow and balance sheet items on a monthly basis. Reporting is done both internally and externally towards the management team, the board and our owners.
  • Supporting our Business Controller with forecasting and budgeting processes.
  • Supporting our Business Controller with cash reporting and improving our cash forecasting.
  • Controlling the financing of the business including reporting of covenants to our lenders.
  • Assisting your team members as well as external contacts on accounting-related questions.
  • Ad hoc analyses and reports.

What You’ll Bring To The Team

  • Practical experience in extensive accounting roles.
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar in accounting, finance, economics or other relevant fields.
  • Effective problem solving skills.
  • Excellent communications skills.
  • Great if the person has experience working for SaaS or other technology company, but it’s not a requirement.
  • Fluent English and Swedish are required.

What is it for you?

We promote and encourage a healthy lifestyle with flexible work hours and latest tools, enhanced vacation allowance, gym membership contribution, and last but not least, you will have the chance to participate in our well-known yearly ugly Xmas sweater competition!

Life at Quinyx

We are an innovative, agile and continuously growing modern tech company. We value passion, creativity, quality and simplicity. You’ll be given the opportunity to make an impact and directly contribute to our success, bring new ideas and have full ownership of your area.

Our global team, of 40 nationalities, have fun together whether it is during a typical Swedish Fika, workshop, going for a run with a colleague or spending time together during our regular after-work socials. We have built a family atmosphere here at Quinyx and are committed to share knowledge and support one another!

Want to join the family?

At Quinyx, we provide equal employment opportunities and we strive to have an inclusive and diverse value-driven culture. All applications will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, disability or Veteran status.

Happy Workforce, Happy Business!

All candidates are subject to background checks.

companyDescription:
Supporting the Journey from Hospital to Home | 

At Breas Medical, we are focused on one of the most critical journeys in healthcare: the transition from hospital to home. We support this journey by providing clinicians and patients with reliable, thoughtfully engineered respiratory solutions.

Serving over 45 countries, our work is centered on:
- Life Support Ventilation: Advanced and dependable solutions for adult and pediatric patients.
- Sleep Therapy: Effective, portable CPAP systems designed for home use and travel.
- Airway Clearance: Devices developed to help patients maintain respiratory health.
- Connected Solutions: Our EveryWare™ platform, providing secure data for patient management.

Follow our journey as we share developments in connected respiratory care, insights for clinicians, and stories that highlight the importance of quality of life.
companySlogan: Be More
descriptionHtml:
When you can’t breathe, nothing else matters. That’s why a career at Breas Medical matters.At Breas, we believe every breath counts—and so does every team member who helps make that possible.

Our mission is to improve the quality of life and care of respiratory patients around the world through a personal commitment to innovation, quality, and customer focus.

Join Breas as a Senior Financial Controller – Drive Strategic Impact in a Transforming Organization

Breas is entering an exciting phase of growth and transformation, and we’re looking for a Global Senior Financial Controller to help shape our financial future. In this pivotal role, you’ll lead the financial oversight of operational expenditures with a strong focus on Research & Development (R&D), acting as a Global strategic business partner to our R&D teams, as well as IT and HR. Based in Gothenburg, you’ll be part of a dynamic, forward-thinking environment where your insights will directly influence investment decisions, drive financial discipline, and support innovation across the organization.

Main Responsibilities

As Senior Financial Controller, you will play a key role in driving financial excellence across R&D, IT, HR and other operational functions. Your responsibilities will span strategic planning, financial oversight, and business partnering in these cost-intensive functions, ensuring that financial decisions support Breas’ growth and transformation journey.

Financial Planning & Analysis

  • Lead budgeting, forecasting, and long-range planning for R&D, IT, HR and other operational expenses.
  • Analyze actual vs. budgeted costs, identify variances, and recommend corrective actions.
  • Maintain and optimize cost center reporting for supported functions.

R&D Financial Oversight

  • Track and report R&D project costs, including capitalized and expensed components.
  • Align financial planning with project milestones in collaboration with R&D leadership.
  • Support R&D tax credit claims and ensure accurate cost allocation.

IT, HR And Other Operational Expense Management

  • Monitor and control expenditures including programs, investments and labor cost planning.
  • Drive cost-saving initiatives and process improvements in these domains.
  • Ensure accurate and timely month-end and year-end closings.

Business Partnering

  • Act as a strategic advisor to R&D, IT and HR Leadership Teams.
  • Provide financial insights to support investment decisions and resource allocation.
  • Foster cross-functional collaboration to enhance financial transparency and accountability.

Compliance & Reporting

  • Ensure compliance with IFRS/local GAAP and internal financial policies.
  • Prepare financial reports for internal stakeholders and external auditors.
  • Support statutory audits and regulatory filings related to R&D and operational finance.

Qualifications

To be successful in this role, we believe you bring:

  • A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field.
  • At least 7 years of experience in financial controlling, preferably in a manufacturing or R&D-intensive environment.
  • Experience working in a global, matrixed organization.
  • Strong understanding of R&D capitalization, cost tracking, and financial modeling.
  • Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., JDE), financial planning tools (e.g., Power BI), and Excel.
  • Excellent analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Strong proficiency in English, both written and verbal.

This position is based at Breas HQ in Mölnlycke, Sweden, with a hybrid work model.

To apply, please submit your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your experience and motivation for joining Breas. We look forward to hearing from you.

Please note that we work with continuous recruitment.

More About Us

Breas Medical is a global company headquartered in Sweden, with over 30 years of expertise in ventilation, airway clearance, and sleep solutions. We are passionate experts dedicated to respiratory care, with unwavering focus on the patient and the caregiver, delivering high-quality and reliable products. We respond to your changing needs with agility, and while growing globally, we take customer care personally.

Our solutions cover a broad range of devices, consumables, service solutions, educational & training programs, and comprehensive connectivity capabilities to improve the quality of life of patients—from hospital to home.

Breas Medical employs over 300 people globally. Our solutions reach 50 countries around the world, and we manufacture our products in four countries: China, UK, Sweden, and the US.

Find Out More About Breas

🌐 https://educationbybreas.com

🌐 https://breas.com

Or check out our channel on Twitter, LinkedIn, or Facebook.

descriptionHtml:
Vi söker en engagerad och nyfiken junior redovisningsekonom som vill växa i en roll där kvalitet, noggrannhet och utveckling står i fokus. Du har god förståelse för redovisningens grunder och trivs i ett samarbetsinriktat team där kunskap delas och kompetens byggs tillsammans. Hos oss får du en unik möjlighet att fördjupa dig i fondredovisning, med stöd från erfarna kollegor och en kultur som uppmuntrar lärande, teknikintresse och förbättringsarbete.


Om rollen


Nu söker vi en Fund Accountant till vårt affärsområde Fund Services som jobbar med administration av Alternativa investeringsfonder.


Som Fund Accountant kommer du att arbeta med löpande bokföring, avstämningar och rapportering. Du får möjlighet att delta i arbetet med kvartalsrapporter och årsredovisningar, samtidigt som du successivt bygger upp din kompetens inom fondredovisning och finansiella transaktioner.


Här arbetar vi med fondernas hela livscykel, från det att våra kunder hör av sig om att etablera en ny fond till fondens upplösning. Arbetet innebär ett tätt samarbete med ett team av kollegor som har kompetens inom ekonomi och juridik.


Vi söker dig som är driven, analytisk och initiativtagande med en naturlig fallenhet för strukturerat och noggrant arbete. Du är nyfiken på nya utmaningar och har lätt för att lära. Du trivs i en miljö där vi anpassar våra processer efter kundernas behov och alltid levererar hög kvalitet i tid. Som en viktig del av vårt team är du är en lagspelare som gärna delar med dig av din kunskap och bidrar till teamets utveckling. Vi ser gärna att du har ett intresse för att arbeta med digitala verktyg och automatisering för att effektivisera processer.


Arbetsuppgifter


  • Löpande bokföring och fondredovisning
  • Upprättande av avstämningar
  • Rapportering till Skatteverket
  • Stöd till Senior Accountant vid upprättande av kvartalsrapporter och årsredovisningar


För att lyckas i rollen krävs det att du arbetar strukturerat, proaktivt och har självförtroende kring din kompetens. Eftersom vi är ett växande företag söker vi dig som är prestigelös, lösningsorienterad och som har lätt för att sätta sig in i nya uppgifter.


Kvalifikationer


  • Eftergymnasial utbildning inom ekonomi/redovisning (t.ex. YH eller universitet)
  • Minst två års arbetslivserfarenhet som redovisningsekonom efter studierna,
  • Mycket goda kunskaper i såväl svenska som engelska, då vi kommunicerar på dessa språk dagligen i vår verksamhet,
  • Vana att arbeta med Word, Excel och övriga delar av Officepaketet.
  • Intresse för teknik och digitala verktyg inom ekonomi är ett plus


Personliga egenskaper och färdigheter


På GOT YOUR BACK™ har vi roligt tillsammans. Vi bryr oss faktiskt på riktigt om att du ska må bra och utvecklas både som människa och i din yrkesroll. Vi är ett snabbväxande, entreprenöriellt företag och vi söker därför dig som vill ha en utvecklande roll med mycket frihet och ansvar i ett företag under stark tillväxt. Vi präglas av en snabbföränderlig och högpresterande miljö och vi ser därmed att du själv trivs i och drivs av att arbeta i sådan miljö med likasinnade kollegor. Rollen kräver ett starkt driv och engagemang i vår kvalitet – vi kallar det grit. Samtidigt vill vi att du är en empatisk person som är prestigelös och lyssnar på kollegor och kunder.

 

Vi erbjuder


Rollen ger en möjlighet att i hög grad påverka och vara delaktig i vår utveckling, att arbeta mot synnerligen välrenommerade kunder i finansbranschen samt att delta i spännande och ofta nydanande och komplexa transaktioner. Vi erbjuder marknadsmässiga ersättningsvillkor och förmåner. Vi sitter i nyrenoverade lokaler vid Östermalmstorg och erbjuder möjlighet att arbeta hemifrån två dagar i veckan.


Om oss


GOT YOUR BACK™ är en av Sveriges ledande leverantörer av administrations- och förvaringsinstitutstjänster åt förvaltare av alternativa investeringsfonder. Vi tillhandahåller vidare så kallade Corporate Services – en fullserviceadministration av aktiebolag och näringsdrivande stiftelser.


Ansökan


Då vi arbetar med löpande urval, ser vi gärna att du skickar in din ansökan så snart som möjligt! Ansökan sker via vår hemsida: www.gotyourback.se/careers/


Rekryteringsfirmor undanbedes vänligen från att kontakta oss.

companyDescription:
Randstad är ett av världens största HR-serviceföretag med över 600 000 anställda i fler än 39 länder. Vi erbjuder lösningar inom bemanning, rekrytering och omställning . För dig som söker jobb erbjuder vi lediga tjänster inom i princip alla branscher och yrkesområden med spännande karriärmöjligheter.

2016 förvärvade Randstad det nordiska bemanningsföretaget Proffice Group. Samma höst bytte Proffice namn till Randstad. 

Randstad-koncernen har sitt säte i Amsterdam, Holland. Det var här Randstad grundades 1960 och i princip uppfann den flexibla arbetsmarknaden i Holland. Sedan dess har mycket hänt. Storleken gör Randstad till en stabil partner. Men det är den nära, personliga kontakten med våra konsulter och kunder på lokal nivå som är nyckeln till vår framgång. 

Randstad Sverige är auktoriserat av Bemanningsföretagen. Det innebär att vi uppfyller högt ställda krav som bl a omfattar etiska regler, kollektivavtal, jämställdhetsplan, ansvarsförsäkring och allmänna leveransvillkor. Bemanningsföretagen ingår i Almega och är medlem i Svenskt Näringsliv.
companySlogan: Vi vill påverka vårt samhälle positivt, framför allt när det handlar om mångfald, jämställdhet och ett tryggt arbetsliv.
descriptionHtml:
Arbetsbeskrivning

Är du en erfaren och självgående Finansiell Controller med minst två års erfarenhet som är intresserad av ett konsultuppdrag från oktober till april? Har du en bakgrund inom offentlig sektor och starka kunskaper i ekonomistyrning? Då kan du vara den vi söker! Läs mer om rollen nedan!

Ansvarsområde

I Rollen Som Finansiell Controller Kommer Du Att Ha Ett Brett Ansvarsområde Som Kräver Både Strategisk Insikt Och Operativ Skicklighet. Dina Huvudsakliga Arbetsuppgifter Inkluderar

  • Ekonomisk styrning och uppföljning: Du planerar, arbetar med och följer upp budgetar och den ekonomiska styrningen för verksamheten.
  • Analys och utredning: Du genomför ekonomiska och administrativa utredningar och analyser samt följer upp resultat för att säkerställa att mål nås.
  • Systemhantering: Du arbetar aktivt med ekonomisystem utifrån ett verksamhetsperspektiv.
  • Redovisning och rådgivning: Du hanterar löpande redovisning, avstämningar och fungerar som rådgivare i ekonomifrågor.
  • Stöd och samordning: Du ger stöd till ekonomihandläggare och arbetar med att planera och samordna intern administrativ verksamhet som även innefattar andra områden.
  • Underlagsframtagning: Du tar fram underlag för budgetarbete, tertialuppföljningar och årsredovisningar.

Kvalifikationer

För att lyckas i denna roll ser vi att du har:

  • Relevant utbildning: Minst en 3-årig ekonomisk gymnasieutbildning, men gärna en högre relevant utbildning.
  • Erfarenhet: 2-4 års erfarenhet som finansiell controller, ekonomicontroller eller senior ekonom inom offentlig sektor.
  • Tekniska färdigheter: Goda kunskaper i Excel är ett krav.

companyDescription:
Assessio exists because humans cannot make completely objective decisions – in recruitment processes, we can be our own worst enemy. Today, your chances of getting a job are higher if you are European, tall, look healthy and do not have children. We want to change that.

Assessio is a SaaS company specialising in strategic HR and developing tests and tools in occupational psychology. Our tools can help organisations predict who has the best potential to succeed in a role, find future leaders within the organisation, identify toxic behaviours in leadership, and match candidates to the organisational culture through values.

We provide our customers with data-driven insights about both current and potential employees. By making decisions based on reliable data rather than gut feeling, we can discover the potential that exists and ensure that no talent goes to waste.

To teach organisations how to make better decisions based on data, our consultants hold both customer-specific and open training courses in our tools. Among other things, the focus is on our digital platform, where we have gathered all our tools for matching, selection, development and business-driven HR. The platform makes it easy to combine multiple tools, create ranking lists in screening processes, compare candidates with each other or compare a candidate with a group.
descriptionHtml:
At Assessio, we have a bold ambition: to become the leading HR Tech provider in Europe, eliminating talent waste by helping people thrive in roles where they can perform at their best.

Through our transformation into a scalable, AI-powered people data platform, we deliver solutions that span recruitment and development. By combining over 70 years of scientific heritage with a modern, intuitive product experience, Assessio serves global enterprises and public organizations with high HR tech maturity. Our mission? To lead the transformation toward a future with Zero Talent Waste by empowering individuals to unleash their full potential in roles aligned with their strengths.

With offices in Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Germany, Estonia, France, Romainia and the Netherlands- and more on the way- we’re building a strong international presence. Join us in shaping the future of talent management in Europe. We are 200+ dedicated co-workers who share our vision of Zero Talent Waste.

About The Role

We are now looking for an experienced Financial Controller for our Nordics region to join our growing Finance team at HQ in Stockholm. We are looking for a proactive and detail-oriented Financial Controller to support our Nordic operations. As part of the Group Chief Accountant’s team, you will take responsibility for the accounting and financial control of our Swedish entities, while also coordinating external accounting partners in Norway, Estonia, and Finland. This role offers hands-on involvement in day-to-day finance operations, reporting, and cash management in a dynamic and international environment.

Main Responsibilities

  • Full ownership of accounting and financial reporting for Swedish legal entities
  • Overseeing third-party accountants and ensuring accurate reporting in Norway, Estonia, and Finland
  • Managing and developing a junior accountant, ensuring quality and timeliness of work
  • Monitoring and managing cash flow across Nordic entities, including liquidity planning and forecasting
  • Liaising with local banks and auditors to ensure smooth operations and compliance
  • Supporting monthly and year-end close processes
  • Ensuring compliance with local accounting standards and tax regulations
  • Assisting in internal control improvements and financial process development

Who are you?

At Assessio, we believe in potential. While experience matters, your drive, curiosity, and collaborative mindset are what truly set you apart.

You are a structured, energetic, and pragmatic team player- someone who thrives in a dynamic international setting. You're hands-on when needed, but also capable of leading and inspiring remote teams. You enjoy simplifying complexity, taking ownership of strategic processes, and are comfortable managing cross-border collaboration.

We believe you’ll thrive in this role if you:

  • Hold a university degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • Have 3–5 years of experience in international finance roles, preferably in SaaS or audit (Big 4 experience is a plus).
  • Solid understanding of Swedish accounting standards and experience working with external service providers in other countries
  • Have hands-on experience with ERP systems — Visma experience is highly valued.
  • Strong Excel skills and familiarity with accounting systems
  • Fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken
  • Strong sense of ownership, attention to detail, and ability to work independently

A fair and data-driven recruitment process

We practice what we preach. That’s why you will be asked to complete three short assessments (personality, cognitive ability, and motivation) - a process that takes around 50 minutes and ensures a fair, unbiased selection based on potential rather than background alone.

You can read more about our hiring approach on our Career Site.

We look forward to getting in touch!

Questions?

Please contact our CFO Bryan Bailey at bryan.bailey@assessio.se

companyDescription:
TP-Link is the world's #1 provider of consumer WiFi networking devices, shipping products to over 120 countries and hundreds of millions of customers.

TP-Link is a global provider of reliable networking devices and accessories, involved in all aspects of everyday life. The company is consistently ranked by analyst firm IDC as the No. 1 provider of WLAN and broadband CPE devices*, supplying distribution to more than 120 countries and serving hundreds of millions of people worldwide.

With a proven heritage of stability, performance and value, TP-Link has curated a portfolio of products that meet the networking needs of all individuals. Now, as the connected lifestyle continues to evolve, the company is expanding today to exceed the demands of tomorrow.
companySlogan: TP-Link is a global provider of reliable networking devices and accessories, involved in all aspects of everyday life.
descriptionHtml:
Om TP-Link

TP-Link är en global ledare inom nätverksprodukter och avancerade kommunikations-lösningar, rankad som världens främsta leverantör av WLAN-produkter. Med verksamhet i över 170 länder har TP-Link Nordic en aktiv närvaro i Sverige, Norge, Danmark och Finland, med ett etablerat kontor i Sverige sedan 2013. Vårt produktsortiment omfattar bland annat intelligenta mesh-system, molnbaserade övervakningslösningar och nätverksverktyg för företag. Genom innovation, engagemang och strategiska partnerskap bidrar vi till regional tillväxt och kundframgång.


Vår affär och verksamhet växer och vi söker nu en Accountant Specialist som vill bli en del av vårt team. Är du nyfiken, driven och vill göra skillnad i ett företag som värdesätter mångfald och entreprenörsanda – då vill vi gärna höra från dig.


Din roll

Som Accountant Specialist kommer du att vara en viktig del av vårt team inom Finance. Du arbetar nära vår International Controller och ansvarar för operativa uppgifter inom leverantörsreskontra (Accounts Payable). Din noggrannhet och goda samarbetsförmåga är avgörande för effektiva ekonomiprocesser och bidrar till hög kvalitet i vår finansiella rapportering.


Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter:

  • Hantera dagliga transaktioner inom leverantörsreskontra
  • Förbereda och bokföra
  • Besvara frågor från leverantörer och interna avdelningar
  • Utföra kontoavstämningar och sammanställningar för internationella enheter
  • Kommunicera med leverantörer och kunder kring betalningar och betalningsplaner
  • Delta i månadsbokslut och ta fram rapporter och balansräkningar
  • Bidra till förbättringsinitiativ och utvecklingsprojekt inom ekonomi
  • Säkerställa efterlevnad av redovisningsregler tillsammans med vår International Controller
  • Utföra andra ekonomirelaterade uppgifter vid behov


Din profil

Vi söker dig som kombinerar erfarenhet och analytisk skärpa med en positiv och samarbetsinriktad inställning.


Vi ser gärna att du har:

  • Examen inom redovisning eller liknande område
  • 5 års erfarenhet av arbete med leverantörsreskontra i en internationell miljö
  • Erfarenhet från IT-branschen
  • God förståelse för momsregler, både nationellt och internationellt, samt andra relevanta skatteregler.
  • Erfarenhet av ERP-system, gärna SAP
  • Självständighet och initiativförmåga i en dynamisk och händelserik arbetsmiljö.
  • Avancerade kunskaper i Excel
  • Flytande i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.
  • Kunskaper i kinesiska (mandarin) är ett plus
  • Hög integritet, god samarbetsförmåga och ett öga för detaljer


Vi erbjuder

  • Konkurrenskraftigt lönepaket
  • Bonusprogram
  • Flexibel och dynamisk arbetsmiljö
  • Balans mellan arbete och fritid
  • Möjlighet till karriärutveckling i en växande roll


Låter det intressant?


Ladda upp din CV redan idag på At TP-Link, everyone powers connection.

descriptionText: Om TP-LinkTP-Link är en global ledare inom nätverksprodukter och avancerade kommunikations-lösningar, rankad som världens främsta leverantör av WLAN-produkter. Med verksamhet i över 170 länder har TP-Link Nordic en aktiv närvaro i Sverige, Norge, Danmark och Finland, med ett etablerat kontor i Sverige sedan 2013. Vårt produktsortiment omfattar bland annat intelligenta mesh-system, molnbaserade övervakningslösningar och nätverksverktyg för företag. Genom innovation, engagemang och strategiska partnerskap bidrar vi till regional tillväxt och kundframgång.Vår affär och verksamhet växer och vi söker nu en Accountant Specialist som vill bli en del av vårt team. Är du nyfiken, driven och vill göra skillnad i ett företag som värdesätter mångfald och entreprenörsanda – då vill vi gärna höra från dig.Din rollSom Accountant Specialist kommer du att vara en viktig del av vårt team inom Finance. Du arbetar nära vår International Controller och ansvarar för operativa uppgifter inom leverantörsreskontra (Accounts Payable). Din noggrannhet och goda samarbetsförmåga är avgörande för effektiva ekonomiprocesser och bidrar till hög kvalitet i vår finansiella rapportering. companyDescription: Binero utvecklar och levererar smidig och säker teknik. Vi är den rätta partnern för företag som söker en tillförlitlig leverantör i Norden. Tänk på oss som den tysta motorn som ger företag kraften att växa. Med decennier av IT-expertis och djup affärsförståelse levererar vi tekniska lösningar som både uppfyller långsiktiga mål och dagliga behov.
descriptionHtml:
Söker du en ny utmaning där du får vara en del av en driven och snabbrörlig koncern i teknikens framkant? Vill du vara med på en framgångsrik och spännande resa? Då kan du vara den vi söker!

Som Senior Accountant på Binero har du en central roll inom ekonomiteamet med ansvar för redovisningen i flertalet av våra bolag. Binero har en ambitiös tillväxtagenda så för att trivas i rollen är det viktigt att du trivs i en föränderlig miljö och motiveras av att arbeta i ett team som sporrar och stöttar varandra.

I Dina Arbetsuppgifter Ingår Bl. a

  • Upprätthålla processer och rutiner
  • Leverera korrekt redovisning med god kvalité
  • Hantera moms- och skattefrågor
  • Säkerställa att samtliga bolag följer koncernens redovisningsprinciper
  • Månads- kvartals- och årsbokslut
  • Avstämningar
  • Vara delaktig i upprättande av årsredovisningar och deklarationer
  • Samarbeta med revisorer, skattekonsulter och extern lönebyrå
  • Bidra till utveckling av processer och rutiner
  • Assistera Head of Accounting i olika projekt

Du kommer att rapportera till vår Head of Accounting och vara placerad i nyinflyttade moderna ljusa lokaler på huvudkontoret i Alvik Strand.

Vem söker vi?

Vi Söker Dig Som Har

  • Akademisk examen eller likvärdigt
  • 5-10 års erfarenhet av liknande arbetsuppgifter
  • Erfarenhet av att arbeta i en koncern
  • Mycket goda kunskaper i regelverket enligt K3
  • Mycket starka redovisningskunskaper och vana att arbeta mot deadlines
  • Drivs av att arbeta i en föränderlig miljö
  • Goda kunskaper i Microsoft Office, varav mycket goda kunskaper i Excel

Meriterande

  • Revisionsbakgrund
  • Erfarenhet från noterad miljö
  • Vana av att jobba i MS Dynamics 365 Business Central
  • Erfarenhet av dansk, finsk och norsk redovisning

För att lyckas och trivas är du en positiv och anpassningsbar person som trivs med att arbeta i en miljö med korta beslutsvägar. Du är van att självständigt driva ditt arbete framåt och har förmågan att ta egna initiativ. Vidare är du en person med hög problemlösningsförmåga som är bra på att skapa strukturer i en föränderlig miljö och ditt arbete kännetecknas av en hög kvalitet och noggrannhet. Eftersom du kommer att ha många kontaktytor i rollen är det avgörande att du trivs i samarbetet med andra och är lyhörd och smidig i ditt sätt att kommunicera på såväl svenska som engelska (koncernspråk är engelska).

Stämmer detta in på dig? Då kan vi erbjuda dig en spännande och utvecklande tjänst i en fartfylld miljö på ett drivet och framåtlutat företag med sunda värderingar där prestigelöshet och frihet under ansvar gäller. I denna rekrytering lägger vi stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.

Tjänsten är en tillsvidaretjänst på 100% med 6 månaders provanställning.

Har du ytterligare frågor om tjänsten kontakta vår Head of Accounting Caroline Svanström på caroline.svanstrom@binero.com

Powering Customer delight

Binero utvecklar och levererar smidig och säker teknik. Vi är den rätta partnern för företag som söker en tillförlitlig leverantör i Norden. Tänk på oss som den tysta motorn som ger företag kraften att växa. Med decennier av IT-expertis och djup affärsförståelse levererar vi tekniska lösningar som både uppfyller långsiktiga mål och dagliga behov.

Vi delar in vår tjänsteportfölj i tre områden - molntjänster, managerade tjänster och uppkopplade tjänster. Som kund hos Binero kan man välja att konsumera hela eller delar av vårt erbjudande. I alla områden erbjuder vi integrerade tjänster så som support 24/7 och projektledning.

Binero har huvudkontor i Stockholm och är cirka 160 anställda representerade i Sverige, Norge, Finland och Danmark.

companyDescription:
We deliver open source to the world faster,  more securely and more cost effectively than any other company.

We're also the publishers of Ubuntu, the world’s most popular enterprise Linux from cloud to edge, together with a passionate global community of 200,000+ contributors. 

Ubuntu means 'humanity to others'​. We chose it because it embodies the generosity at the heart of open source, the new normal for platforms and innovation.

Together with a community of 200,000, we publish an operating system that runs from the tiny connected devices up to the world's biggest mainframes, the platform that everybody uses on the public cloud, and the workstation experience of the world's most productive developers.

Secure and reliable, elegant and intuitive, and open for innovation - this is the future of open source, which is why we're proud to be the developers of the fastest growing Linux in the world despite already being the most widely deployed.

If you're interested in a career at Canonical, we are a remote-first company so please apply to any suitable role as skills are valued more than location, despite some having a preferred geographic preference. 

www.canonical.com
companySlogan: Enterprise open source, secured and delivered by the publisher of Ubuntu.
descriptionHtml:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. We are also a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1000+ colleagues in 70+ countries and very few roles based in offices. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder led, profitable and growing. We are hiring a Systems & Controls Accountant with experience to drive best practices in systems, processes, and controls.

In our finance function we aspire to set the industry pace for speed and accuracy of reporting. We aim to achieve this by driving our product pricing and terms, contracting, sales processes and systems into alignment. We are looking for a professionally qualified accounting leader with expertise in NetSuite to build scalable processes, automated through correct and auditable implementations in NetSuite, Salesforce and other connected business systems.

This lead will work across teams including product management, legal, FP&A, sales operations and commercial business systems to align understanding and ensure that the accounting and revenue recognition treatment of contracts and products is straightforward and correct, with a very high level of automation. Perhaps unusually we prefer a leader with deep personal interest in the software and technology industry, and ideally some data analytics and software engineering experience. The Systems & Controls Accountant will ensure the proper application of revenue accounting guidance for public market scrutiny. Ideally they will also be technically savvy and able to work effectively with technical teams and software engineers.

This is a driving position which requires confidence in presentation, setting goals, and directing the work of others. We expect to grow a team with a particular emphasis on systems and automation, that puts us at the top of the leaderboard for clarity, correctness, transparency and speed of reporting as a global tech company.

Location: This role is home-based in EMEA time zones, with the option to be based in our London office. It will report to our Global Head of Accounting.

Key responsibilities

  • Lead complex NetSuite configuration, workflows, customization, and integration
  • Implement best practices for data governance, security, and compliance within NetSuite and connected systems.
  • Demonstrate proficiency in script understanding and problem resolution
  • Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and KPIs to provide insights into business performance
  • Coordinate regular system maintenance, upgrades, and troubleshooting to ensure optimal performance and reliability
  • Provide training and support to end-users to enhance their efficiency and proficiency with NetSuite.
  • Oversee Sandbox accounts for development and testing, ensuring seamless deployment of customizations and updates to the production environment
  • Stay updated on new NetSuite features and functionalities, assessing their potential impact on business processes
  • Drive process and systems improvements across teams for end-to-end automation of the full financial life cycle
  • Incorporate industry best practices and emerging technology to raise the bar for reporting speed and accuracy
  • Mentor and aid in developing the wider team of accounting and finance professionals
  • Develop and maintain effective internal over revenue transactions and reporting
  • Research and write accounting memos on relevant policy matters
  • Act as the primary liaison with external auditors in related matters

Valued Skills And Experience

  • An exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
  • An undergraduate degree in Accounting or Finance, or a compelling narrative about your alternative chosen path
  • CPA, CA or equivalent with excellent technical accounting skills and IFRS experience
  • A detailed understanding of IFRS and relevant rules related to SaaS and services
  • Leadership or management responsibility
  • Drive and a track record of going above-and-beyond expectations
  • Experience of software licensing, subscription and services revenue accounting and operations
  • Experience driving and scaling accounting processes in a high-growth environment
  • Experience with ERP, Rev Rec, CRM, CPQ and other relevant software categories - NetSuite and Salesforce preferred
  • Experience in a multi-country multi-currency, operations and tax environment
  • Professional written and spoken English
  • Excellent interpersonal skills, curiosity, flexibility, and accountability
  • Thoughtfulness and self-motivation
  • Result-oriented, with a personal drive to meet commitments
  • Ability to travel twice a year, for company events up to two weeks long

Additional, Optional Skills We Value

  • Public company reporting and revenue accounting experience
  • Software implementation, integration and user acceptance
  • Software engineering qualification or experience
  • A passion for technology and a desire to work with motivated colleagues
  • MBA or other relevant business degree

What we offer you

We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus or commission. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.

  • Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
  • Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
  • Annual compensation review
  • Recognition rewards
  • Annual holiday leave
  • Maternity and paternity leave
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
  • Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events

About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Systems & Controls Accountant

companyDescription:
KPMG is a global network of professional firms providing audit, tax and advisory services. We operate in 143 countries and have nearly 265 000 
 people working in member firms around the world. KPMG Sweden has 1650 employees in offices around Sweden. With a worldwide presence, KPMG continues to build on its success thanks to clear vision, defined values and, above all, its people.
companySlogan: KPMG är ett av världens ledande kunskapsföretag. Vi jobbar för att göra skillnad & förbättra framtiden för våra kunder.
descriptionHtml:
Do you want to help shape the future of tax advisory? KPMG offers a unique opportunity to take a leading role in the growth of their tax transformation business. You'll work closely with Nordic and global networks in a flexible culture. Together, we make a real impact – for our clients, the industry, and your career.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Tax Accounting Specialist to join KPMG Swedens Tax Transformation team. You will leverage the great work done by the rest of KPMG, supporting clients in implementing innovative tax solutions, navigating complex regulations, identifying critical data needs, and resolving data gaps. You will be instrumental in expanding our practice, generating leads, and demonstrating the value of our services to both colleagues and clients.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Lead and support clients through the implementation of tax transformation processes, ensuring compliance and addressing complex tax challenges e.g. OECD Pillar 2, Public CbCR, Tax Administration 3.0.
  • Collaborate closely with clients’ finance and tax departments, as well as KPMG specialists globally, to understand regulations, defined data needs, address data gaps and implement analytics and automation solutions.
  • Understand and analyze organizations' financial and tax compliance processes, making complex laws actionable for clients and providing innovative solutions.
  • Build and maintain robust client relationships, acting as a trusted advisor and delivering tailored tax transformation strategies.
  • Drive business development initiatives by identifying, leveraging existing client relationships and pursuing new opportunities and communicating the value of our tax transformation services effectively to colleagues and clients.
  • Stay informed on the latest tax regulations and industry trends to provide innovative solutions that clients are eager to implement, enhancing the client experience and contributing to the growth and expansion of our tax transformation team.

The role is based in the office on Vasagatan in Stockholm, for the right person placement in another location is possible.

At KPMG, you become part of a corporate culture where the individual is important. KPMG offers great development opportunities in a diverse environment where you will be challenged through exciting and socially important involvement with clients. You are offered a varied and responsible role where the pace is often high, with many opportunities for stimulating social contacts, constant change and knowledge development. KPMG offers a comprehensive internal training and development program with the opportunity to develop within your areas of interest.

,

A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Accounting, Finance, Taxation, or a related discipline is required for this role. Professional certifications such as CPA, CA, or an equivalent are highly preferred. The ideal candidate will bring over six years of hands-on experience in accounting, consulting, or a similar role, preferably within a large consulting firm such as the Big Four or a major multinational corporation. Experience working in in-house corporate tax or finance departments is considered an advantage.

A strong background in accounting is essential, with a preference for candidates who also possess in-depth knowledge of tax regulations. The role requires a thorough understanding of financial and tax compliance processes, as well as familiarity with accounting and consolidated reporting standards commonly used by major Swedish corporations.

Candidates should have experience managing projects involving multiple stakeholders and demonstrate strong analytical abilities, particularly in relation to financial systems, accounting tools, and financial data. A genuine interest in driving digital transformation within finance and tax is important, as is a curious mindset and a willingness to challenge existing processes.

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills are essential as well as ability to build and maintain strong client relationships is a key requirement for success in this position. We value individuals who are passionate about driving digital transformation in finance and tax, and who bring a forward-thinking mindset with a willingness to challenge established processes in order to foster innovation. Strong communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills are critical for success in this role. Moreover, the ability to identify and act upon new business opportunities, build and maintain client relationships, and collaborate effectively within cross-functional teams is essential.

Do you want to know more?

In this process KPMG is working with Level Recruitment. To apply, please click the apply button. If you have further questions don´t hesitate to contact recruitment consultant Dennis Pater at +46 735 58 89 34 or Christian Smith at +46 8-120 50 400. Please note that we need your application written in English. We will interview ongoingly so please register your application as soon as possible.

You will be instrumental in expanding our practice, generating leads, and demonstrating the value of our services to both colleagues and clients.Your responsibilities will include:Lead and support clients through the implementation of tax transformation processes, ensuring compliance and addressing complex tax challenges e.g. OECD Pillar 2, Public CbCR, Tax Administration 3.0.Collaborate closely with clients’ finance and tax departments, as well as KPMG specialists globally, to understand regulations, defined data needs, address data gaps and implement analytics and automation solutions.Understand and analyze organizations' financial and tax compliance processes, making complex laws actionable for clients and providing innovative solutions.Build and maintain robust client relationships, acting as a trusted advisor and delivering tailored tax transformation strategies.Drive business development initiatives by identifying, leveraging existing client relationships and pursuing new opportunities and communicating the value of our tax transformation services effectively to colleagues and clients.Stay informed on the latest tax regulations and industry trends to provide innovative solutions that clients are eager to implement, enhancing the client experience and contributing to the growth and expansion of our tax transformation team.The role is based in the office on Vasagatan in Stockholm, for the right person placement in another location is possible.At KPMG, you become part of a corporate culture where the individual is important. KPMG offers great development opportunities in a diverse environment where you will be challenged through exciting and socially important involvement with clients. You are offered a varied and responsible role where the pace is often high, with many opportunities for stimulating social contacts, constant change and knowledge development. KPMG offers a comprehensive internal training and development program with the opportunity to develop within your areas of interest.,A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Accounting, Finance, Taxation, or a related discipline is required for this role. Professional certifications such as CPA, CA, or an equivalent are highly preferred. The ideal candidate will bring over six years of hands-on experience in accounting, consulting, or a similar role, preferably within a large consulting firm such as the Big Four or a major multinational corporation. Experience working in in-house corporate tax or finance departments is considered an advantage.A strong background in accounting is essential, with a preference for candidates who also possess in-depth knowledge of tax regulations. The role requires a thorough understanding of financial and tax compliance processes, as well as familiarity with accounting and consolidated reporting standards commonly used by major Swedish corporations.Candidates should have experience managing projects involving multiple stakeholders and demonstrate strong analytical abilities, particularly in relation to financial systems, accounting tools, and financial data. A genuine interest in driving digital transformation within finance and tax is important, as is a curious mindset and a willingness to challenge existing processes.Excellent communication and interpersonal skills are essential as well as ability to build and maintain strong client relationships is a key requirement for success in this position. We value individuals who are passionate about driving digital transformation in finance and tax, and who bring a forward-thinking mindset with a willingness to challenge established processes in order to foster innovation. Strong communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills are critical for success in this role. Moreover, the ability to identify and act upon new business opportunities, build and maintain client relationships, and collaborate effectively within cross-functional teams is essential.Do you want to know more?In this process KPMG is working with Level Recruitment. To apply, please click the apply button. If you have further questions don´t hesitate to contact recruitment consultant Dennis Pater at +46 735 58 89 34 or Christian Smith at +46 8-120 50 400. Please note that we need your application written in English. We will interview ongoingly so please register your application as soon as possible.
title: Tax Accounting Specialist with focus on transformation to KPMG, Stockholm
